CAREER: SCALABLE ADAPTIVE CONTROL WITH PERFORMANCE GUARANTEES FOR SAFE AND EFFICIENT AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS -THIS FACULTY EARLY CAREER DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM (CAREER) GRANT FUNDS RESEARCH ON AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS THAT ARE POISED TO TRANSFORM INDUSTRIES RANGING FROM TRANSPORTATION AND MANUFACTURING TO ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING AND SPACE EXPLORATION. USE OF THESE SYSTEMS IN SAFETY-CRITICAL APPLICATIONS FACES SIGNIFICANT CHALLENGES, SUCH AS ENSURING SAFETY AND PERFORMANCE IN UNPREDICTABLE ENVIRONMENTS AND ADAPTING TO LARGE AND SUDDEN CHANGES, AND HANDLING COMPLEX DYNAMICS. THIS PROJECT SEEKS TO OVERCOME THESE BARRIERS BY DEVELOPING ADVANCED CONTROL METHODS THAT ENSURE RELIABLE AND HIGH-PERFORMING OPERATION OF AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS, EVEN IN DYNAMIC AND UNCERTAIN CONDITIONS. THESE ADVANCEMENTS COULD IMPROVE THE SAFETY, RELIABILITY, AND PERFORMANCE OF TECHNOLOGIES SUCH AS SELF-DRIVING CARS, AUTONOMOUS AIR TAXIS, AND SPACECRAFT. IN ADDITION TO ITS TECHNICAL CONTRIBUTIONS, THE PROJECT WILL CULTIVATE THE NEXT GENERATION OF INNOVATORS IN CONTROL AND AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS. EDUCATIONAL AND OUTREACH EFFORTS WILL INCLUDE DEVELOPING NEW COURSE MATERIALS, ORGANIZING WORKSHOPS, AND ENGAGING K-12 STUDENTS IN ALABAMA THROUGH INTERACTIVE ACTIVITIES SUCH AS DRONE RACE CHALLENGES AND TWISTY FLYER EVENTS, SPARKING INTEREST IN SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, ENGINEERING, AND MATHEMATICS (STEM) FIELDS. THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP ADVANCED CONTROL METHODOLOGIES THAT ENABLE THE RELIABLE DEPLOYMENT OF AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS IN DYNAMIC AND UNCERTAIN ENVIRONMENTS, ADDRESSING KEY CHALLENGES SUCH AS SAFETY, PERFORMANCE, ADAPTABILITY, AND THE ABILITY TO MANAGE COMPLEX, HIGH-DIMENSIONAL SYSTEMS. TO ACHIEVE THIS, THE RESEARCH FOCUSES ON THREE MAIN THRUSTS: (I) DESIGNING A CONTROL ARCHITECTURE THAT INTEGRATES ROBUST ADAPTIVE UNCERTAINTY COMPENSATION AND CONSTRAINED CONTROL TO ENSURE SAFE AND EFFICIENT OPERATION OF NONLINEAR SYSTEMS UNDER COMPLEX UNCERTAINTIES, (II) ESTABLISHING AN ADAPTIVE NONLINEAR PARAMETER-VARYING CONTROL FRAMEWORK TO HANDLE LARGE UNCERTAINTIES, INCLUDING THOSE ARISING FROM CONTROL AUTHORITY CONSTRAINTS AND UNMATCHED UNCERTAINTIES, AND (III) LEVERAGING MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUES TO ENHANCE THE SCALABILITY AND PERFORMANCE OF THE PROJECTED ROBUST ADAPTIVE CONTROL ALGORITHMS, ENABLING THEIR APPLICATION TO HIGH-DIMENSIONAL SYSTEMS WITH STRINGENT PERFORMANCE DEMANDS. TOGETHER, THESE EFFORTS AIM TO ADVANCE THE STATE OF THE ART IN MANAGING UNCERTAINTY, CONSTRAINTS, AND NONLINEAR DYNAMICS, SETTING A FOUNDATION FOR DEPLOYING SAFETY-CRITICAL AUTONOMOUS SYSTEMS ACROSS A WIDE RANGE OF APPLICATIONS. THE EDUCATIONAL AND OUTREACH COMPONENTS WILL COMPLEMENT THE RESEARCH BY EQUIPPING STUDENTS ACROSS ALL LEVELS WITH THE SKILLS AND MOTIVATION TO PURSUE CAREERS IN CONTROL AND AUTONOMY, THEREBY CONTRIBUTING TO A STRONGER WORKFORCE AND REGIONAL ECOSYSTEM IN INTELLIGENT SYSTEMS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

REGULATORY NUCLEIC ACID INTERACTIONS AND TELOMERE COMPLEXES - PROJECT ABSTRACT THIS MIRA AWARD REQUEST WILL UNIFY TWO AREAS OF R01-FUNDED RESEARCH IN THE LABORATORY OF DEBORAH WUTTKE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO BOULDER. THE COMMON THEME IS PROBING THE MULTI-LAYER REGULATORY NETWORKS THAT MODULATE GENE EXPRESSION AND CELLULAR HOMEOSTASIS THROUGH REGULATION BY NUCLEIC ACIDS. SPECIFICALLY, THE PROGRAM WILL ADDRESS OUTSTANDING QUESTIONS REGARDING THE NON-CANONICAL RECOGNITION OF RNA BY DNA-BINDING TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS (TFS) AND THE MANAGEMENT OF SSDNA BY REPLICATION A (RPA) TYPE FACTORS. DURING THE LAST GRANT PERIOD, WE DISCOVERED A ROBUST, AND UNEXPECTED, RNA-BINDING ACTIVITY INHERENT IN NUMEROUS TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS. THIS RNA-BINDING ACTIVITY WAS FOUND TO BE MEDIATED BY EXTENDED VERSIONS OF THEIR DNA-BINDING DOMAINS AND DIRECTLY COMPETE WITH DNA BINDING. FURTHERMORE, WE FOUND THAT THIS ACTIVITY WAS STRUCTURE-SPECIFIC RATHER THAN SEQUENCE SPECIFIC IN VITRO. THESE OBSERVATIONS RAISE CRITICAL QUESTIONS REGARDING THE ROLE OF RNA BINDING BY TFS IN TRANSCRIPTIONAL REGULATION AND PROVIDE THE DIRECT MOTIVATION FOR THE RESEARCH PROGRAM DESCRIBED HERE. THE NEXT STEPS ARE TO FURTHER DEFINE AND UNDERSTAND THE SPECIFICITY FOR THE IN VIVO RNA TARGETS AND DETERMINE THE IMPACT TF RNA INTERACTIONS HAS ON THE TRANSCRIPTIONAL PROGRAM. WE WILL TAKE ADVANTAGE OF BIOCHEMICALLY VALIDATED SEPARATION OF FUNCTION MUTANTS THAT ALLOW US TO INDEPENDENTLY DISRUPT EITHER RNA- OR DNA-BINDING ACTIVITY IN CELLS AND PROBE THEIR MECHANISM OF ACTION USING INTEGRATED TRANSCRIPTOMICS AND SINGLE MOLECULE IMAGING. THE IMPACT OF THIS PROGRAM IS HIGH BECAUSE UNDERSTANDING THE ROLES OF GENOME-WIDE PERVASIVE TRANSCRIPTION AND HOW THIS ACTIVITY INFLUENCES DIVERSE CELLULAR PROCESSES REMAINS A MAJOR UNANSWERED QUESTION IN BIOLOGY. TELOMERES ARE SPECIALIZED NUCLEOPROTEIN STRUCTURES AT THE ENDS OF EUKARYOTIC CHROMOSOMES THAT ARE REQUIRED FOR CHROMOSOME STABILITY AND CELLULAR PROLIFERATION. THESE STRUCTURES ARE ESSENTIAL FOR HUMAN HEALTH BECAUSE DYSREGULATION OF EITHER TELOMERE PROTECTION OR TELOMERASE ACTIVITY CAUSES MANY INHERITED AND ACQUIRED HUMAN DISEASES, WITH TELOMERE DYSFUNCTION CLOSELY TIED TO CANCER AND AGING. OUR PROGRAM IS FOCUSED ON TWO PROTEIN COMPLEXES THAT MANAGE SSDNA IN MANY CHROMOSOMAL CONTEXTS, INCLUDING AT TELOMERES AND REPLICATION FORKS. HUMAN CST IS A HETEROTRIMERIC PROTEIN COMPLEX THAT PROTECTS AND MAINTAINS SITES OF G-RICH SSDNA THROUGHOUT THE GENOME, ACTING PROMINENTLY AT TELOMERES THROUGH BINDING THE CONSERVED G-RICH OVERHANG TO COORDINATE THE ACTIVITIES OF TELOMERASE AND POL-ALPHA PRIMASE. MUTANTS OF CST ARE ASSOCIATED WITH A RANGE OF HUMAN DISEASES CHARACTERISTIC OF PROLIFERATION DEFECTS. THE STRUCTURAL, FUNCTIONAL AND BIOCHEMICAL PARALLELS BETWEEN THE CST AND RPA COMPLEXES SUGGEST A HIGHLY TUNED INTERPLAY OF THEIR ACTIVITIES THAT ALLOW FOR THEIR CROSSTALK IN THE MANAGEMENT OF DIFFICULT G-RICH REGIONS OF CHROMATIN AND OUR RECENT DATA POINT TO THE INTRICATE INTEGRATION OF THE GENERAL DNA-MAINTENANCE AND TELOMERE MACHINERIES. THIS IS KEY TO UNDERSTANDING THE BASIC BIOLOGY OF CHROMOSOME MAINTENANCE AND THE CATASTROPHIC CONSEQUENCES OF ITS MISREGULATION.

solicitations.title: TWO (2) BRAND NAME OR EQUAL FREEFLY ASTRO MAX NDAA/BLUE QUADCOPTER UNCREWED AERIAL SYSTEM (UAS; AKA DRONES)

AFTER FIVE YEARS OF THE REEU (RESEARCH AND EXTENSION EXPERIENCE FOR UNDERGRADUATES)SUMMER PROGRAM AT THE UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND EASTERN SHORE (UMES), WHICH STARTED DURING THE PANDEMIC, AND BASED ON EXPERIENCE WITH POST-PANDEMIC STUDENTS, WE CAME UP WITH AN ELEVATED PROGRAM DESIGN, ADDRESSING BOTH THE RISING DEMAND FOR STEM (SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, ENGINEERING, AND MATHEMATICS)PROFESSIONALS IN AGRICULTURE AND FOOD SCIENCES, AND THE NEED TO ATTRACT STEM STUDENTS TO THE FIELD. THE PROGRAM IS A HYBRID OF ONLINE WEBINARS AND ACTIVITIES DURING THE ACADEMIC YEAR AND A TEN-WEEK SUMMER INTERNSHIP, SIX OF WHICH ARE ON CAMPUS, WITH HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE. IT INCLUDES THREE THEMATIC WEEKS OF SEQUENCED ACTIVITIES, PRACTICUMS, AND FIELD TRIPS. LEADERSHIP TRAINING AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT ARE PROVIDED, INCLUDING TRAINING ON INTERACTING WITH HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS AND TEACHERS IN EXISTING FANE AND ACS SEED PROGRAMS, OUTREACH ACTIVITIES TO FARMERS AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES, AND SKILLS IN WORKING IN INTERDISCIPLINARY GROUPS. PARTICIPANTS FROM EXISTING FANE AND ACS SEED PROGRAMS WILL SHADOW REEU STUDENTS' RESEARCH ACTIVITIES FOR SEVERAL DAYS THROUGHOUT THE SUMMER PROGRAM AND PARTICIPATE IN SEVERAL JOINT WEBINARS, REPLICATING TIERED TEACHING SESSIONS DURING THE ACADEMIC YEAR, TO BUILD A COMMUNITY OF MOTIVATED LEARNERS AT ALL LEVELS.THE SUMMER EXPERIENCE ENDS WITH A PRESENTATION AND A TEAM-WRITTEN RESEARCH REPORT. FURTHER OPPORTUNITIES TO PRESENT AT CONFERENCES ARE OFFERED. THE BEST STUDENTS WILL BE INVITED TO RETURN FOR ANOTHER TEN-WEEK SUMMER OF RESEARCH EXPERIENCE. THE PROJECT WILL CREATE A PIPELINE FROM COLLEGE TO GRADUATE SCHOOL AND TO AN AGRICULTURAL STEM CAREER FOR STUDENTS. THE PROGRAM WILL ALSO RETAIN BEST PRACTICES DEVELOPED IN THE FIVE YEARS OF THE COMPLETED AFRI-EWD-REEU PROGRAM AND THE CURRENT AFRI-EWD-FANE PROGRAM. TO ADDRESS CURRENT AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE PRIORITIES, WE WILL TRAIN STUDENTS IN AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY, DRONES, SMALL AND SMART AGRICULTURAL DEVICES, PRECISE IRRIGATION, BIOTECHNOLOGY AND MICROBIOMICS, SENSORY SCIENCE, AND HORTICULTURAL PHYTOCHEMISTRY, AND PRACTICE AI-ASSISTED LEARNING.

solicitations.title: TITLE: CULTIV8: GROWING TECH-SAVVY AGRICULTURAL YOUTHAFRI FARM BILL PRIORITY AREAS: AGRICULTURE SYSTEMS AND TECHNOLOGY AND PLANT HEALTH ANDPRODUCTION AND PLANT PRODUCTSPROJECT DURATION: SEPTEMBER 1, 2025, TO AUGUST 31, 2028PROJECT DIRECTOR: SCOTT FAULKNERLEADERSHIP TEAM MEMBERS: TORMOEHLEN, ROGER; SIKES, MELISSA; ERICK, KATRINA; DART, JOHN;AND NELSON, TAYLOREXTERNAL EVALUATOR: WANG, HUI-HUISUBMITTING ORGANIZATION: FAIRBANKS SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICT, FAIRBANKS, AK OTHER ORGANIZATIONS INVOLVED:FAIRBANKS SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION DISTRICTS -THEIR EDUCATION OUTREACH UNIT CONDUCTS PK-12 OUTREACH EDUCATION EFFORTS INCLUDING WORKING WITH AGRICULTURE IN THE CLASSROOM AND LOCAL FFA EDUCATIONAL INITIATIVES.PURDUE UNIVERSITY'S DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURAL AND BIOLOGICAL ENGINEERING- THE DEPARTMENT IS A LEADING AGRICULTURAL AND BIOLOGICAL ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT WITH SUBSTANTIAL PK-12 EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH INITIATIVES OFFERED IN BOTH FORMAL AND NONFORMAL SETTINGS.EFFIE KOKRINE CHARTER SCHOOL - A TITLE 1 GRADE 7-12 CHARTER SCHOOL IN THE FAIRBANKS NORTH STAR BOROUGH WITH 145 STUDENTS. STUDENTS WILL PROVIDE INPUT IN THE DEVELOPMENT AND TESTING OF CURRICULUM MATERIAL, ATTEND THE PROGRAM'S SUMMER CAMPS, AND SERVE ON THE PROJECT'S LEADERSHIP TEAM AND STEERING COMMITTEE.DOYON FOUNDATION - THE DOYON FOUNDATION, A NON-PROFIT MEMBERSHIP-BASED ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO IMPROVING THE IDENTITY AND QUALITY OF LIFE FOR RURAL ALASKAN COMMUNITY MEMBERS, SEEKS TO PROVIDE EDUCATIONAL, CAREER, AND CULTURAL LEARNING OPPORTUNITIES.ALASKA DIVISION OF AGRICULTURE, FFA ORGANIZATION - FFA, AN EXTRA-CURRICULAR AGRICULTURAL ORGANIZATION FOR YOUTH IN MIDDLE/HIGH SCHOOL, CONDUCTS EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING ACTIVITIES DESIGNED TO COMPLEMENT FORMAL-BASED LEARNING EXPERIENCES.YOUTH ADVISORY COMMITTEE -A 12-MEMBER YOUTH ADVISORY COMMITTEE COMPOSED OF YOUTH REPRESENTING RURALALASKAN COMMUNITIES, KEY ALASKAN AGRICULTURAL REGIONS, AND THE DIFFERENT YOUTH ORGANIZATIONS WILL BE FORMED AND EMPOWERED TO MAKE DECISIONS.STEERING COMMITTEE MEMBERS, INCLUDING REPRESENTATIVES FROM THE ALASKA DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE, ALASKA FARM BUREAU, ALASKA NATURAL RESOURCES CONSERVATION SERVICES, ALASKA AGRICULTURE IN THE CLASSROOM, NATIVE ALASKAN AGRICULTURAL ASSOCIATION, ALASKA AFTERSCHOOL PROGRAM, ALASKA AG TEACHERS ASSOCIATION, AND THE UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA FAIRBANKS. AT LEAST THREE YOUTH FROM THE YOUTH ADVISORY COMMITTEE WILL BE INCLUDED ON THE STEERING COMMITTEE.LOCATIONS OF PROPOSED ACTIVITIES: THE PRIMARY LOCATIONS WILL INCLUDE RURAL COMMUNITIESIN INTERIOR ALASKA (I.E., NENANA, MANLEY HOT SPRINGS, GLENNALLEN), KEY AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITIES (I.E., DELTA JUNCTION, NENANA, AND MATSU VALLEY). DOYON FOUNDATION, A KEY STRATEGIC PARTNER, WILL LEAD EFFORTS TO IMPLEMENT NONFORMAL EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING IN REMOTE COMMUNITIESTHROUGHOUT ALASKA THROUGH THE AGRICULTURE IN THE CLASSROOM PROGRAM, 4-H, FFA, AND OTHER NONFORMAL YOUTH EDUCATION ORGANIZATIONS AND PROGRAMS.TECHNOLOGY BEING ADDRESSED: THE PROPOSE,D PROJECT WILL DEVELOP THREE ENGAGING AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY CURRICULA FOCUSED ON AGRICULTURAL ROBOTICS, AGRICULTURAL DRONES/PRECISION AGRICULTURE,AND HYDROPONIC BIOLOGICALS. THE NONFORMAL DESIGNED CURRICULUM WILL UTILIZE ENGAGING HANDS-ON LEARNING EXPERIENCES DELIVERED BY VOLUNTEER INSTRUCTORS/EDUCATORS USING A VARIETY OF EDUCATIONAL EXPERIENCES ( CURRICULUM WITH HANDS-ON LEARNING EXPERIENCES, IMMERSIVE CAMPS, MINI WORKSHOPS, AND CAREER DEVELOPMENT EVENTS. ADDITIONALLY, THE PROJECT TEAM WILL WORK WITH INDUSTRY PARTNERS TO EXPLORE THE CREATION OF SPECIFIC CREDENTIALING PROGRAMS FOR EACH OF THE THREE CURRICULUM AREAS.NUMBER OF PARTICIPANTS PER YEAR: THE POTENTIAL AUDIENCE INCLUDES THE 158,000-YOUTH REACHED ANNUALLY THROUGH ALASKA'S AGRICULTURE IN THE CLASSROOM PROGRAM, THE STATE'S 3,167 4-H MEMBERS, AND ITS 250 FFA MEMBERS. THE PROJECT'S GOAL IS TO REACH THROUGH THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE THREE NONFORMAL CURRICULA, THE MINI WORKSHOPS, AND HANDS-ON CAMPS, A MINIMUM OF 200 YOUTH PER YEAR.RELEVANCE OF THE PROJECT TO THE GOALS OF FANE PROGRAM AREA: THIS PROPOSED PROJECT MEETS ALL OF THE KEY POINTS MENTIONED IN THE RFA. THE PROJECT WILL DESIGN AND IMPLEMENT PROGRAMMING TO ENHANCE YOUTH'S UNDERSTANDING OF DATA SCIENCE, AGRICULTURAL ROBOTICS AND DRONES, PRECISION AGRICULTURE, AND BIOTECHNOLOGY THROUGH NONFORMAL EDUCATIONAL LEARNING EXPERIENCES. IT WILL BUILD UPON AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY LEARNING EXPERIENCES IMPLEMENTED THROUGH AGRICULTURE IN THE CLASSROOM AND 4-H.RELEVANCE OF THE PROJECT TO THE GOALS OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURE NON-FORMAL EDUCATION (FANE) PROGRAM: THIS PROPOSED PROJECT'S ACTION STEPS, AS OUTLINED IN THE PROGRAM NARRATIVE, ADDRESS FANE'S FOUR GOALS AS STATED IN THE RFA. THE PROJECT WILL DEVELOP CURRICULA AND OTHER HANDS-ON LEARNING EXPERIENCES; THE MATERIALS WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE FOR USE IN A VARIETY OF OUTREACH PROGRAMS; THE PROGRAM BUILDS ON OTHER FORMAL AND NONFORMAL EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING; AND YOUTH WILL BE INVOLVED IN ALL ASPECTS OF THE PROJECT.

solicitations.title: MODERN WHEAT VARIETIES RELY HEAVILY ON A SINGLE CLASS OF GENES THAT KEEP PLANTS SHORT AND PREVENT THEM FROM FALLING OVER. THESE GENES ARE KEY TO BOOSTING FOOD PRODUCTION DECADES AGO, BUT THEY WERE DESIGNED FOR FARMING SYSTEMS THAT RELY ON ABUNDANT WATER AND FERTILIZER. TODAY, MANY FARMING REGIONS FACE MORE FREQUENT DROUGHT, RISING INPUT COSTS, AND PRESSURE TO REDUCE FERTILIZER USE. UNDER THESE CONDITIONS, CURRENT WHEAT VARIETIES OFTEN GROW SLOWLY EARLY IN THE SEASON AND PRODUCE FEWER GRAINS. AS RESOURCE LIMITATIONS CONTINUE TO RESHAPE AGRICULTURE, RELYING ON THE SAME OLD GENETIC SOLUTIONS PUTS FUTURE FOOD SECURITY AND FARM LIVELIHOODS AT RISK. DEVELOPING WHEAT VARIETIES THAT CAN THRIVE WITH FEWER INPUTS IS ESSENTIAL NOT ONLY FOR FARMERS BUT ALSO FOR CONSUMERS, RURAL ECONOMIES, AND COMMUNITIES THAT DEPEND ON A STABLE AND AFFORDABLE FOOD SUPPLY.THIS PROJECT ADDRESSES THIS CHALLENGE BY DEVELOPING NEW WHEAT VARIETIES THAT REMAIN SHORT AND LODGING-RESISTANT WHILE GROWING MORE VIGOROUSLY AND EFFICIENTLY UNDER STRESSFUL CONDITIONS. INSTEAD OF RELYING ON THE SAME GENES USED FOR DECADES, THE PROJECT WILL INTRODUCE ALTERNATIVE PLANT HEIGHT GENES INTO WELL-ADAPTED WHEAT VARIETIES AND TEST THEM UNDER FIELD CONDITIONS, INCLUDING DROUGHT AND LOW-FERTILIZER CONDITIONS. MODERN TOOLS SUCH AS DNA-BASED MARKERS, GENE EDITING, AND DRONE-BASED FIELD MONITORING WILL BE USED TO EFFICIENTLY MEASURE PLANT GROWTH, STRESS TOLERANCE, YIELD, AND GRAIN QUALITY. BY IDENTIFYING WHEAT VARIETIES THAT CAN PRODUCE RELIABLE HARVESTS WITH FEWER INPUTS, THIS WORK WILL PROVIDE FARMERS AND BREEDERS WITH NEW OPTIONS TO REDUCE PRODUCTION COSTS WHILE MAINTAINING PRODUCTIVITY. ULTIMATELY, THE PROJECT AIMS TO SUPPORT FARMERS' LIVELIHOODS, STRENGTHEN FOOD SECURITY, AND ENSURE THAT WHEAT PRODUCTION REMAINS COMPETITIVE IN GLOBAL MARKETS.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: SMART SENSING OF NITROGEN FOR PRECISION AGRICULTURE -EFFICIENT NITROGEN MANAGEMENT IS CRITICAL FOR SUSTAINING U.S. AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY WHILE REDUCING ENVIRONMENTAL POLLUTION AND ECONOMIC LOSSES ASSOCIATED WITH FERTILIZER OVERUSE. DESPITE WIDESPREAD ADOPTION OF PRECISION AGRICULTURE TOOLS SUCH AS DRONES AND SATELLITE IMAGERY, FARMERS STILL LACK THE ABILITY TO DETECT NITROGEN DEFICIENCY BEFORE VISIBLE SYMPTOMS APPEAR, WHEN YIELD LOSSES ARE OFTEN ALREADY UNAVOIDABLE. BECAUSE NITROGEN STRESS FIRST MANIFESTS AS MOLECULAR-LEVEL CHANGES WITHIN PLANTS, EARLY DIAGNOSIS REQUIRES DIRECT MEASUREMENTS RATHER THAN EXTERNAL OBSERVATIONS. THIS CAREER PROJECT ADDRESSES THIS NEED BY DEVELOPING A LOW-COST, ENVIRONMENTALLY SUSTAINABLE SENSOR SYSTEM THAT CAN MONITOR MOLECULAR SIGNALS IN PLANTS, SOILS, AND WATER IN REAL TIME, ENABLING TIMELY AND PRECISE NITROGEN MANAGEMENT DECISIONS. BY IMPROVING THE SYNCHRONIZATION BETWEEN NITROGEN SUPPLY AND CROP DEMAND, THE PROJECT AIMS TO ENHANCE NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY, REDUCE NITROGEN LOSSES, AND SUPPORT SMART AGRICULTURE PROCESSES. THE PROJECT INTEGRATES RESEARCH WITH EDUCATION BY ENGAGING K-12 STUDENTS, UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS, AND FARMERS IN HANDS-ON SENSOR DEPLOYMENT, DATA ANALYSIS, AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP TRAINING, THEREBY STRENGTHENING WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT FOR A FUTURE WITH SMART AND SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE THAT ADVANCES THE NATION?S HEALTH AND PROSPERITY. THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP AND VALIDATE AN INTEGRATED, MINIATURIZED, CIRCULAR SENSOR SUITE CAPABLE OF REAL-TIME, MULTIPARAMETRIC MONITORING OF MOLECULAR-LEVEL NITROGEN DYNAMICS DRIVEN BY GENETICS/ENVIRONMENT/MANAGEMENT INTERACTIONS. THE RESEARCH WILL ADVANCE MICRONEEDLE-STRUCTURED, MULTIPLEXED ELECTROCHEMICAL SENSORS FOR IN-PLANT, SOIL, AND WATER MEASUREMENTS, FABRICATED USING BIODEGRADABLE AND BIORESORBABLE MATERIALS TO ENABLE SENSOR RECYCLING AND ELIMINATE ELECTRONIC WASTE. FIELD-SCALE EXPERIMENTS WILL BE CONDUCTED TO CAPTURE SPATIOTEMPORAL NITROGEN RESPONSES ACROSS CROPS, ENVIRONMENTS, AND MANAGEMENT PRACTICES. MACHINE LEARNING/ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE MODELS WILL BE DEVELOPED TO ANALYZE SENSOR DATA, QUANTIFY NITROGEN DYNAMICS, AND GENERATE DATA-DRIVEN NITROGEN RECOMMENDATIONS. TOGETHER, THESE INNOVATIONS WILL OVERCOME THE LIMITATIONS OF CONVENTIONAL LABORATORY-BASED AND DISCRETE SENSING TECHNOLOGIES, PROVIDE NEW INSIGHTS INTO NITROGEN CYCLING UNDER REAL FIELD CONDITIONS, AND ESTABLISH A SCALABLE PLATFORM FOR SENSOR-DRIVEN PRECISION AND SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

solicitations.title: AI-POWERED MULTIMODAL PRECISION SENSING SYSTEM TO TRACK TICK PHENOTYPIC RESPONSES IN NATURAL AND SIMULATED ENVIRONMENTS - THE LONG-TERM GOAL OF OUR TEAM (DRS. HILL, MURGIA AND KAUR) IS TO REVOLUTIONIZE CONTROL OF VECTOR-BORNE DISEASES THROUGH INNOVATIONS IN AUTONOMOUS, AI-DRIVEN ROBOTICS SYSTEMS FOR PRECISION PEST MANAGEMENT. ON THIS TWO-YEAR R21, WE PROPOSE THE DEVELOPMENT OF A TRANSFORMATIVE AUTONOMOUS ROBOTIC SYSTEM FOR PRECISE, REAL-TIME DETECTION AND TARGETED TREATMENT OF THE LYME DISEASE TICK, IXODES SCAPULARIS IN OCCLUDED NATURAL ENVIRONMENTS. WE WILL LEVERAGE OUR COMBINED EXPERTISE IN TICK BIOLOGY, AI, SENSING, ROBOTICS AND PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT TO INTRODUCE TWO NOVEL TECHNOLOGIES: (1) A MMWAVE-SENSING SYSTEM CUSTOMIZED FOR DETECTION AND TRACKING OF MULTIPLE NYMPHS AND ADULT TICKS IN OCCLUDED ENVIRONMENTS AND (2) AI-BASED ALGORITHMS TO DRIVE THE AUTONOMOUS, LARGE-SCALE SAMPLING OF MULTIPLE LAND TYPES, AND SUBSEQUENT DETECTION OF TICK HABITAT AND TICK HOTSPOTS BY QUADRUPED ROBOTS (OR ALTERNATIVELY, DRONES). IN AIM 1, WE WILL DEVELOP AN AI- POWERED MULTIMODAL SENSING-BASED SYSTEM TAKING ADVANTAGE OF THE PURDUE PHENOCOSM, A VERSATILE TEST ARENA FOR DETAILED ASSESSMENT OF TICK BEHAVIORS OVER WEEKS-LONG STUDY PERIODS AND INVOLVING VARIOUS SUBSTRATE TYPES TO MIMIC NATURAL TICK HABITATS. THIS WORK WILL COMBINE MULTI-MODAL MMWAVE AND TRADITIONAL CAMERA TECHNOLOGY, TOGETHER WITH NEURAL NETWORK-DRIVEN TRACKING ALGORITHMS. THROUGH THIS WORK, WE EXPECT TO GENERATE THE FIRST SYSTEM CAPABLE OF CONTINUOUSLY MONITORING MULTIPLE TICKS IN COMPLEX, OCCLUDED ENVIRONMENTS, REPRESENTING A PARADIGM SHIFT FOR SURVEILLANCE. IN AIM 2, WE WILL DEVELOP SOPHISTICATED PATH PLANNING, NAVIGATION, AND CONTROL ALGORITHMS NEEDED TO SUPPORT AUTONOMOUS QUADRUPED ROBOT MOVEMENT IN OUTDOOR SETTINGS, INCLUDING A RANGE OF HABITATS SUCH AS DECIDUOUS FOREST/GRASS ECOTONES THAT TYPICALLY SUPPORT I. SCAPULARIS POPULATIONS. THE QUADRUPED ROBOT WILL HAVE CAPABILITY TO (1) NAVIGATE IN AND ADAPT MOVEMENTS FOR UNEVEN AND COMPLEX TERRAINS, (2) OPTIMIZE MOVEMENT FOR ENERGY EFFICIENCY, (3) COLLECT LARGE ENVIRONMENTAL DATA SETS AND (4) DETECT TICKS CONCEALED BY VEGETATION, LEAF LITTER AND OTHER MATTER. THE SENSING AND ROBOTICS TECHNOLOGIES DEVELOPED ON THIS PROJECT WILL LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR FUTURE FIELD STUDIES TO VALIDATE PROTYPES FOR AREA-WIDE TICK SURVEILLANCE AND TARGETED CONTROL. ONCE VALIDATED AND FULLY DEVELOPED, THIS AUTONOMOUS SYSTEM WILL GREATLY EXPAND U.S. PUBLIC HEALTH SURVEILLANCE CAPACITY, SUBSTANTIALLY REDUCING HUMAN-TICK ENCOUNTERS, THE INCIDENCE AND BURDEN OF TICK-BORNE DISEASES, PESTICIDE USE, HEALTHCARE AND PEST MANAGEMENT COSTS, PAVING THE WAY FOR A NEW ERA IN FULLY REMOTE PEST AND DISEASE MANAGEMENT. 1
solicitations.agency: Department of Health and Human Services

solicitations.title: OATS HAVE BECOME A MAJOR FORAGE CROP FOR LIVESTOCK DURING FALL AND WINTER MONTHS IN THE SOUTHERN US WHEN OTHER HIGH-PROTEIN FEEDS ARE SCARCE, DUE TO HIGH QUALITY, PALATABILITY, EARLY GRAZING AND REGROWTH CAPACITY, AND SILAGE PRODUCTION POTENTIAL. SYSTEMATIC GENETIC IMPROVEMENT OF BIOMASS YIELD IN WINTER OAT IS LIMITED, AS BREEDING PROGRAMS IN THE SOUTHERN US HAVE PREDOMINANTLY FOCUSED ON GRAIN YIELD IMPROVEMENT AND A LACK OF BREEDING TOOLS TO ENABLE EFFICIENT AND ACCURATE SELECTION FOR BIOMASS. INCREASED BIOMASS YIELD IS A CRUCIAL GOAL THAT MUST BE ACCOMPLISHED TO MEET THE INCREASING DEMAND FOR QUALITY LIVESTOCK FEED, BUT THE LACK OF IN-SEASON PLOT BIOMASS DATA IS A MAJOR LIMITING FACTOR TO GENETIC GAIN. HIGH-THROUGHPUT AND PRECISE IN-FIELD PHENOTYPING TECHNIQUES ARE NEEDED TO MAXIMIZE GENETIC GAIN FOR BIOMASS YIELD. THE PROPOSED RESEARCH AIMS TO COMBINE STATE-OF-THE-ART HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING TOOLS WITH GENOMIC SELECTION TO PREDICT GENOTYPIC PERFORMANCE FOR BIOMASS YIELD, TO TRAIN FUTURE PLANT BREEDERS, AND TO IMPLEMENT BIOMASS SELECTION PROTOCOLS BASED ON RESULTS. THIS WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED BY CHARACTERIZING A 480-LINE TRAINING AND A 200-LINE VALIDATION OAT PANELS IN FOUR STATES OVER THREE YEARS IN THE SOUTHERN US. UAV-BASED MULTISPECTRAL SENSOR, MOLECULAR MARKERS, AND BIOMASS DATA WILL BE COLLECTED AND USED TO DEVELOP DIFFERENT MULTIVARIATE AND MACHINE-LEARNING PREDICTION MODELS. TWO WORKSHOPS WILL BE ORGANIZED TO TRAIN PLANT BREEDING GRADUATE STUDENTS FROM SUNGRAINS UNIVERSITIES AT THE UAS-WHEATCAP HUB. RESULTS WILL PROVIDE CLARITY AND DIRECTION FOR BREEDERS, AND EASY-TO-USE TOOLS TO BREED HIGH BIOMASS-YIELDING VARIETIES.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: TASK ORDER FOR MR-UAS DEPLOYMENT SERVICES FOR CG NATIONAL SECURITY CUTTERS KIMBALL AND JAMES.

THE PURPOSE OF THIS REQUISITION IS TO COMMIT $750,000.00 FOR A NEW TASK ORDER UNDER 70RSAT20D00000001 FOR UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (UAS) AND COUNTER-UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (C-UAS), PROVIDES DHS WITH DATA-DRIVEN INSIGHTS, OPERATIONAL RECOMMENDATIO
NO IDC: AUTONOMOUS PRECISION LANDING OF A VARIABLE SKEW-QUAD PLANE UAV ON MOVING GROUND VEHICLES

FARMERS TODAY RELY HEAVILY ON MANUAL CROP SCOUTING TO DETECT INSECT PESTS EARLY, A PROCESS THAT IS TIME-CONSUMING, LABOR-INTENSIVE, AND INCREASINGLY DIFFICULT DUE TO LABOR SHORTAGES AND RISING PRODUCTION COSTS. WHILE DRONES AND GROUND ROBOTS CAN SURVEY FIELDS AT A LARGE SCALE, THEY OFTEN CANNOT SEE PESTS HIDING ALONG STEMS, BRANCHES, AND DENSE FOLIAGE--PRECISELY WHERE EARLY INFESTATIONS BEGIN. MISSING THESE EARLY WARNING SIGNS CAN LEAD TO INCREASED PESTICIDE USE, CROP LOSSES, AND HIGHER FOOD COSTS. DEVELOPING NEW TOOLS THAT CAN MONITOR CROPS MORE CLOSELY AND EFFICIENTLY IS IMPORTANT NOT ONLY FOR AGRICULTURE, BUT ALSO FOR ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY, RURAL ECONOMIES, AND FOOD SECURITY.THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A SMALL, LIGHTWEIGHT ROBOTIC DEVICE THAT CAN GENTLY CLIMB PLANTS AND INSPECT THEM UP CLOSE, MUCH LIKE AN INSECT MOVING ALONG A STEM. THE RESEARCH TEAM WILL DESIGN SOFT, FLEXIBLE MATERIALS THAT ALLOW THE ROBOT TO GRIP PLANTS WITHOUT DAMAGING THEM, ALONG WITH COMPACT ELECTRONICS AND SIMPLE ONBOARD VISION SYSTEMS THAT HELP THE ROBOT RECOGNIZE PESTS AND NAVIGATE PLANT STRUCTURES ON ITS OWN. THE ROBOT WILL BE TESTED IN LABORATORY SETTINGS AND IN REAL FARM ENVIRONMENTS TO ENSURE IT CAN MOVE RELIABLY, COLLECT USEFUL INFORMATION, AND OPERATE WITHOUT HUMAN CONTROL. THROUGHOUT THE PROJECT, STUDENTS WILL BE TRAINED IN AGRICULTURAL ROBOTICS, AND RESULTS WILL BE SHARED WITH RESEARCHERS AND AGRICULTURAL STAKEHOLDERS.BY ENABLING CLOSE-RANGE, PLANT-LEVEL MONITORING, THIS PROJECT AIMS TO IMPROVE EARLY PEST DETECTION AND SUPPORT MORE TARGETED PEST MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES. IN THE LONG TERM, THIS TECHNOLOGY COULD REDUCE THE NEED FOR EXCESSIVE PESTICIDE USE, LOWER LABOR DEMANDS FOR FARMERS, AND PROMOTE MORE SUSTAINABLE FARMING PRACTICES. THE KNOWLEDGE GAINED FROM THIS RESEARCH WILL HELP LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR FUTURE AGRICULTURAL ROBOTS THAT ARE AFFORDABLE, ENVIRONMENTALLY FRIENDLY, AND CAPABLE OF SUPPORTING FARMERS AND RURAL COMMUNITIES.

solicitations.title: WING-TAIL INTERACTIONS IN NEAR-GROUND IN AVIAN-INSPIRED FLIGHT: AN EXPERIMENTAL APPROACH -QUADCOPTERS ARE UNMANNED AERIAL VEHICLES (UAVS) WITH FOUR ROTORS. THE CONFIGURATION OF THE ROTORS IS FIXED, WHICH LIMITS THEIR AGILITY. STILL, THEY DOMINATE THE UAV MARKET. FLAPPING-WING UAVS THAT MIMIC THE FLIGHT DYNAMICS OF BIRDS ARE QUIETER AND MORE MANEUVERABLE. THEY ARE UNDERUTILIZED BECAUSE OF CHALLENGES IN LANDING AND TAKEOFF THAT REQUIRE RAPID CHANGES IN ANGLE OF ATTACK. BIRDS HAVE THE REQUIRED ABILITY TO PERFORM RAPID PITCH-UP MOTIONS DURING LANDING AND TAKEOFF WHILE PRECISELY SWEEPING THEIR WINGS. IF SMALL UAVS COULD OPERATE LIKE BIRDS, THEY COULD PROVE HELPFUL IN DISASTER RESPONSE, RECONNAISSANCE, OR PACKAGE DELIVERY. MOST RESEARCH HAS FOCUSED ON THE MAIN WINGS ALONE, IGNORING INTERACTIONS BETWEEN WINGS AND TAIL. THIS PROJECT WILL CONDUCT EXPERIMENTS TO UNDERSTAND HOW INTERACTIONS BETWEEN WINGS AND TAILS INFLUENCE THE FORCES AND FLOW FIELD DURING RAPID STARTS AND STOPS NEAR THE GROUND. BY UNCOVERING THE PHYSICAL PRINCIPLES THAT ALLOW BIRDS TO MANEUVER EFFECTIVELY, THE PROJECT WILL PROVIDE GUIDANCE FOR FUTURE DESIGNS OF AGILE UAVS. THE PROJECT WILL ALSO SUPPORT EDUCATION AND WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT BY INTEGRATING RESEARCH FINDINGS INTO HANDS-ON LEARNING EXPERIENCES AND ENGAGING GRADUATE AND UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS. THE PROJECT OUTCOMES WILL IMPROVE LOW-ALTITUDE FLIGHT SAFETY, ENABLE MORE EFFICIENT NEAR-GROUND OPERATIONS, AND SUPPORT ADVANCED MANUFACTURING OF UAVS AND DRONES. THIS PROJECT WILL INVESTIGATE THE INTERACTION OF THE MAIN WING AND TAIL DURING BIRD FLIGHT. THE UNSTEADY FORCES AND THE FLOW FIELD AROUND 3D PRINTED BIRD MODELS WILL BE MEASURED TO UNDERSTAND HOW THEY CHANGE NEAR THE GROUND DURING TAKEOFF OR LANDING. THE STUDY WILL FOCUS ON HOW RAPID PITCHING OF THE WING AND TAIL INFLUENCES THE FORMATION AND EVOLUTION OF VORTICES. THE INSTANTANEOUS FORCES, SUCH AS LIFT AND DRAG, WILL BE MEASURED BY A FORCE SENSOR. THE FLOW FIELD WILL BE MEASURED USING PARTICLE IMAGE VELOCIMETRY VELOCITY MEASUREMENTS. THE OUTCOME OF THIS PROJECT WILL REVEAL HOW DIFFERENT VORTICES FORMED DURING TAKEOFF OR LANDING GOVERN FORCE PRODUCTION DURING NEAR-GROUND MANEUVERS, WHICH WILL LEAD TO IMPROVED MATHEMATICAL MODELS. THE PROJECT WILL ADVANCE FUNDAMENTAL UNDERSTANDINGS OF UNSTEADY FLOWS IN COMPLEX, GROUND-ADJACENT CONDITIONS. UNDERGRADUATE AND GRADUATE STUDENTS WILL BE TRAINED, AND RESEARCH FINDINGS WILL BE INTEGRATED INTO COURSEWORK. OUTREACH ACTIVITIES WILL BE CONDUCTED FOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS, WHERE THEY WILL PERFORM EXPERIMENTS WITH ROBOTIC BIRD WINGS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

THEIR ACTIVITIES WILL GENERALLY INCLUDE WORK RELATED TO LONG RANGE TRANSPORTATION PLANNING SHORT RANGE TRANSPORTATION PLANNING TRANSPORTATION IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT AND MONITORING GENERAL AND COMPREHENSIVE PLANNING PROGRAM SUPPORT AND ADMINISTRATIVE ITEMS AND VARIOUS OTHER STUDIES AND PLAN ACTIVITIES.

SOUTHEAST GENOMIC MEDICINE ECONSULT SERVICE (SE-GEMS) - INTEGRATING GENOMIC MEDICINE INTO ROUTINE HEALTHCARE HOLDS IMMENSE PROMISE FOR IMPROVING PATIENT OUTCOMES THROUGH MORE TIMELY TESTING, DIAGNOSIS, AND PRECISION THERAPEUTICS. ALTHOUGH THE IMPORTANCE OF GENOMIC TESTING IS RECOGNIZED, ITS USE IN PATIENT CARE REMAINS SCANT DUE TO A LACK OF TRAINING (LITERACY), CONFIDENCE, AND ACCESS TO SPECIALISTS AND RESOURCES. THESE BARRIERS ARE ESPECIALLY PRONOUNCED AMONG THE SOUTHEAST REGION OF THE US. TO CATALYZE SIGNIFICANT ADVANCES IN GENOMIC MEDICINE AND MAKE IT AN INTEGRAL PART OF HEALTHCARE FOR CLINICAL POPULATIONS, WE PROPOSE TO CREATE THE SOUTHEAST REGIONAL GENOMIC MEDICINE ECONSULT SERVICE (SEGEMS), LED BY THE UNIVERSITY OF ALABAMA AT BIRMINGHAM (UAB) AND VANDERBILT UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER (VUMC). THIS CLINICAL AND RESEARCH COLLABORATIVE WILL BUILD ON SEPARATE WELL-ESTABLISHED ECONSULT SERVICES TO CONDUCT OUTREACH, PROMOTION AND EDUCATION AND PROVIDE COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC MEDICINE ECONSULTS (GM-ECONSULTS) TO ALL COMMUNITIES. WE HAVE IDENTIFIED COMMUNITY PARTNERS PROVIDING CARE TO CLINICAL POPULATIONS ACROSS THE REGION. THIS EFFORT AIMS TO REACH BEYOND THE 5 MILLION PATIENTS RECEIVING CARE AT OUR INSTITUTIONS, TO OVER 12 MILLION RESIDENTS IN ALABAMA AND TENNESSEE, AND SURROUNDING REGIONS TO CREATE A SUSTAINABLE SERVICE SCALABLE TO OVER 1,000 GM- ECONSULTS PER YEAR. WE WILL EVALUATE THE IMPLEMENTATION OF SEGEMS USING THE REACH EFFECTIVENESS ADOPTION IMPLEMENTATION MAINTENANCE (RE-AIM) FRAMEWORK, ASSESSING THE TECHNOLOGY USABILITY, REACH, ADOPTION, SUSTAINABILITY, AND CLINICAL IMPACT OF THE ECONSULT SERVICE USING MIXED METHODS ACROSS THE TWO MAJOR EHR VENDOR PLATFORMS, EPIC (VUMC) AND CERNER (UAB).  WE WILL DEVELOP, TEST, REFINE, AND SHARE A TRANSFERABLE AND SUSTAINABLE IMPLEMENTATION TOOLKIT TO SUPPORT SUBSEQUENT DISSEMINATION OF THE GENOMIC ECONSULT SERVICE TO OTHER ORGANIZATIONS. GUIDED BY AN INTEGRATED CONSOLIDATED FRAMEWORK FOR IMPLEMENTATION RESEARCH (CFIR), WE WILL IDENTIFY BARRIERS AND FACILITATORS TO IMPLEMENTATION OF GENOMIC ECONSULT SERVICES AND DEVELOP A TRANSFERABLE SET OF BEST PRACTICES TO ASSIST WITH FUTURE IMPLEMENTATIONS. WE WILL USE MIXED METHODS TO GATHER DATA ON KEY COMPONENTS OF SUCCESS FOR IMPLEMENTATION. WE WILL USE AND SHARE THE TOOLKIT TO WIDELY DISSEMINATE GENOMIC ECONSULT SERVICES FOR USE BY OTHER ORGANIZATIONS. WE BRING TOGETHER AN EXPERIENCED INVESTIGATIVE TEAM WITH ESTABLISHED HISTORY OF COLLABORATION AND LEADERSHIP IN NATIONAL GENOMIC MEDICINE EFFORTS. WE LEVERAGE OUR EXPERTISE IN GENOMICS (INCLUDING CLINICAL GENETICS, CARDIO-GENOMICS, CANCER GENETICS (GERMLINE AND SOMATIC), PHARMACOGENOMICS, MATERNAL-FETAL MEDICINE, GENETIC COUNSELING, NEPHROLOGY, NEUROLOGY, AND PRIMARY CARE), INFORMATICS, IMPLEMENTATION SCIENCE AND TELEHEALTH AND ECONSULTS TO CREATE, IMPLEMENT, EXPAND, AND EVALUATE A REGION-WIDE GENOMIC MEDICINE ECONSULT SERVICE.

THE SUSTAINABLE URBAN AGRICULTURE (SUA) FIELD IS EXPANDING RAPIDLY DUE TO THE GROWING NEED FOR CLIMATE-RESILIENT FOOD SYSTEMS AND THE RISING DEMAND FOR SUSTAINABLE, ORGANIC, AND LOCALLY SOURCED PRODUCE. AS THE FIELD EXPANDS, THERE IS A NEED FOR SKILLED AND TECHNOLOGICALLY SAVVY INDIVIDUALS TO ENTER THE WORKFORCE. THE FACILITATING OPPORTUNITIES FOR RE-ENTRY AND WORKFORCE ADVANCEMENT THROUGH AGRICULTURAL DEVELOPMENT (COD-FORWARD) PROJECT, LED BY COLLEGE OF DUPAGE'S (COD) HORTICULTURE DEPARTMENT, WILL PROVIDE 93 UNDER-RESOURCED AND INCARCERATED STUDENTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO ACQUIRE EMPLOYER-RECOGNIZED SUA STACKABLE CERTIFICATES FOR SUCCESSFUL EMPLOYMENT IN THE AGRICULTURAL INDUSTRY.COD-FORWARD FOCUSES ON PROVIDING COMPREHENSIVE CLASSROOM INSTRUCTION COUPLED WITH HANDS-ON LEARNING FOR COD STUDENTS AND STRATEGIC REENTRY PARTNERSHIPS FOR INCARCERATED STUDENTS, ENSURING THE PROJECT ADDRESSES BOTH REGIONAL AGRICULTURAL WORKFORCE NEEDS AND BROADER SOCIETAL GOALS. WITH ITS COMPREHENSIVE SUPPORT SYSTEM - ACADEMIC TRAINING, MENTORING, PAID INTERNSHIPS, STUDENT ENGAGEMENT OPPORTUNITIES, AND OTHER WRAPAROUND SERVICES SUPPORTED BY THE COLLEGE - CODFORWARD PAVES A CLEAR PATH TO SUSTAINABLE EMPLOYMENT. THROUGH A UNIQUE PARTNERSHIP WITH DUPAGE COUNTY CORRECTIONAL FACILITY AND JUST OF DUPAGE, INCARCERATED STUDENTS WILL EARN AN URBAN FARMING CERTIFICATE AND WILL RECEIVE HANDS-ON EDUCATION WITH THE OPPORTUNITY TO CONTINUE BUILDING ON EMPLOYER-RECOGNIZED CREDENTIALS POST-RELEASE. BY BUILDING AND EXPANDING RELATIONSHIPS WITH LOCAL PARTNERS AND EMPLOYERS, THE PROGRAM AIMS TO REDUCE BARRIERS TO EDUCATION AND ENHANCE STUDENT PERSISTENCE TO ULTIMATELY STRENGTHEN LOCAL FOOD SECURITY.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: THIS PROJECT WILL HELP PREPARE STUDENTS IN SOUTHWEST ARKANSAS FOR GOOD JOBS IN AGRICULTURE AND ANIMAL CARE. UA COSSATOT WILL CREATE TWO NEW TRAINING PROGRAMS: A HANDS-ON RGENERATIVE AGRICULTURE DEGREE AND A VETERINARY TECHNICIAN PROGRAM THAT LEADS TO NATIONAL CERTIFICATION.STUDENTS WILL LEARN PRACTICAL SKILLS THAT LOCAL EMPLOYERS NEED, SUCH AS WORKING WITH ANIMALS, USING FARM TECHNOLOGY LIKE GPS AND DRONES, WELDING AND EQUIPMENT REPAIR, AND MANAGING MODERN FARMS. THE PROGRAM IS DESIGNED ESPECIALLY TO SUPPORT FIRST-GENERATION, HISPANIC, AND RURAL STUDENTS BY OFFERING PAID INTERNSHIPS, STRONG STUDENT SUPPORT, AND CLEAR PATHWAYS TO JOBS.BY WORKING CLOSELY WITH LOCAL FARMERS, BUSINESSES, AND SCHOOLS, THIS PROJECT WILL STRENGTHEN THE LOCAL WORKFORCE, HELP STUDENTS EARN CREDENTIALS THAT LEAD DIRECTLY TO EMPLOYMENT, AND SUPPORT THE LONG-TERM SUCCESS OF AGRICULTURE IN ARKANSAS.

solicitations.title: DESCRIPTION OF THE PROBLEMS AND HOW THE PROJECT-HARVEST (HARNESSING AGRICULTURE READINESS THROUGH VOCATIONAL EDUCATION AND SKILLS TRAINING)-ADDRESSES THEMAGRICULTURE IS UNDERGOING RAPID TRANSFORMATION DUE TO ADVANCES IN TECHNOLOGY, RISING ENVIRONMENTAL PRESSURES, AND SHIFTING WORKFORCE DEMANDS. YET MOST STUDENTS AND THE EDUCATIONAL PATHWAYS SERVING THEM DO NOT REFLECT THIS NEW REALITY. THE CORE ISSUE IS THAT TODAY'S AGRICULTURAL WORKFORCE REQUIRES INTERDISCIPLINARY KNOWLEDGE AND TECHNICAL SKILLS THAT ARE NOT CURRENTLY BEING TAUGHT OR UNDERSTOOD AT THE LEVEL NEEDED IN COMMUNITY COLLEGES OR HIGH SCHOOLS.PROBLEM 1: STUDENTS DO NOT UNDERSTAND THAT AGRICULTURE IS AN INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE.MODERN AGRICULTURE IS NO LONGER LIMITED TO PLANTING AND HARVESTING. IT RELIES ON KNOWLEDGE DRAWN FROM: BIOLOGY AND ECOLOGY, ENGINEERING AND ROBOTICS, COMPUTER SCIENCE AND DATA ANALYTICS, ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCE, ECONOMICS AND AGRIBUSINESS. HOWEVER, MANY STUDENTS HAVE NEVER BEEN EXPOSED TO AGRICULTURE AS A FIELD THAT INTEGRATES TECHNOLOGY, SUSTAINABILITY, ENGINEERING, AND ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEM-SOLVING.AS A RESULT, THEY OFTEN:UNDERESTIMATE THE COMPLEXITY OF AGRICULTURAL CAREERS,ASSUME AGRICULTURE HAS LIMITED CAREER PATHWAYS,AND OVERLOOK THE HIGH-SKILLED, HIGH-WAGE OPPORTUNITIES AVAILABLE.?1.HARVEST ADDRESSES IT BY UPDATING AND EXPANDINGEXISTING AGRICULTURE COURSES TO EXPLICITLY INCORPORATE:BIOLOGYENVIRONMENTAL SCIENCEENGINEERING AND ROBOTICSDATA ANALYTICS AND COMPUTER SCIENCEIOT AND PRECISION AGRICULTURE TECHNOLOGIES2.HARVEST WILL PROVIDE HANDS-ON, INTERDISCIPLINARY LEARNING EXPERIENCESSUCH AS:FIELDWORKCAPSTONE PROJECTSSUMMER RESEARCHMULTI-LEVEL MENTORINGGUEST LECTURES FROM USDA/INDUSTRYLEARNING IN LABS, TECHNOLOGY CENTERS, AND FIELD STATIONSTHESE ACTIVITIES IMMERSE STUDENTS IN HOW SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, AND SUSTAINABILITY INTERSECT.3. HARVEST WILL ACHIEVE ENHANCED STUDENT AWARENESS OF MODERN AGRICULTURAL CAREERS:HARVEST AIMS TO INCREASE AWARENESS OF USDA PATHWAYS AND MODERN WORKFORCE OPPORTUNITIES, DIRECTLY CORRECTING THE MISCONCEPTION THAT AGRICULTURE IS OUTDATED OR LOW-SKILL.PROBLEM 2: STUDENTS DO NOT UNDERSTAND HOW SUSTAINABILITY IS CONNECTED TO CLIMATE-SMART TECHNOLOGIES.AGRICULTURE TODAY MUST ADAPT TO:CLIMATE UNPREDICTABILITYSOIL DEGRADATIONWATER SCARCITYSHIFTING DISEASE/PEST PATTERNS.YET STUDENTS RARELY LEARN HOW SUSTAINABILITY PRINCIPLES--LIKE RESOURCE CONSERVATION, SOIL HEALTH, OR PRODUCTIVITY REDUCTION--CONNECT DIRECTLY TO THESE TECHNOLOGIES. THIS DISCONNECT PREVENTS THEM FROM UNDERSTANDING WHY MODERN AGRICULTURE MUST BE BOTH TECHNICALLY ADVANCED AND ENVIRONMENTALLY RESPONSIBLE.1. HARVEST ADDRESSES THIS VIA INTEGRATION OFCLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE MODULES INTO UPDATED COURSES:PRECISION AGRICULTURE USING SMART FARMING & IOTCLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE PRACTICESAGRICULTURAL ROBOTICSENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP THROUGH AQUAPONICS AND HYDROPONICS2. HARVEST EMPHASIZES USE OF SMART GREENHOUSES,IOT-ENABLED LEARNING INFRASTRUCTURE, AND THE USE OFAI CENTER'S ROLE,IN ENABLING STUDENTS TO DESIGN, BUILD, AND TEST CLIMATE-SMART SOLUTIONS USING:IOT-BASED ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORINGSMART GREENHOUSE SYSTEMSIRRIGATION AND NUTRIENT OPTIMIZATION TOOLS3. HARVEST AIMS TO PROVIDE STUDENTS WITH REAL-WORLD SUSTAINABILITY APPLICATION THROUGH AQUAPONICS:STUDENTS GAIN HANDS-ON EXPERIENCE WITH AN AQUAPONICS SYSTEM BUILT ON CAMPUS, MODELING A CLOSED-LOOP, RESOURCE-EFFICIENT AGRICULTURAL SYSTEM.PROBLEM 3: WORKFORCE DEMAND IS GROWING FASTER THAN WORKFORCE PREPARATION.LOCAL, REGIONAL, AND NATIONAL EMPLOYERS URGENTLY NEED WORKERS WHO CAN:OPERATE AND INTERPRET DATA FROM PRECISION AGRICULTURE TOOLS,MANAGE CLIMATE-RESILIENT PRODUCTION SYSTEMS,PARTICIPATE IN FIELD RESEARCH,INTEGRATE ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP INTO DAILY AGRICULTURAL OPERATIONS.BUT COMMUNITY COLLEGES LACK UPDATED CURRICULUM, HANDS-ON TRAINING, AND EXPOSURE TO USDA-ALIGNED WORKFORCE PATHWAYS.1. HARVEST WILL DEVELOP WORKFORCE-ALIGNED CURRICULUM AND CREDENTIALS:HARVEST WILL UPDATE THE AGRICULTURE ASSOCIATE OF ARTS (AA) PROGRAM TO INCLUDE INDUSTRY-ALIGNED COMPETENCIES AND INDUSTRY-RECOGNIZED CREDENTIALS, ENSURING GRADUATES MEET EMPLOYER EXPECTATIONS.2. HARVEST AIMS TO PROVIDE DIRECT WORKFORCE TRAINING IN PRECISION AGRICULTURE AND CLIMATE-RESILIENT SYSTEMS WITH HANDS?ON COMPONENTS (FIELDWORK, RESEARCH, CAPSTONE PROJECTS, SUMMER PROGRAMS) TO PREPARE STUDENTS TO:OPERATE PRECISION AGRICULTURE TOOLSINTERPRET SENSOR AND DRONE DATAIMPLEMENT CLIMATE-RESILIENT SYSTEMSAPPLY SUSTAINABLE MANAGEMENT STRATEGIESBASIC METHODS AND APPROACHESHARVEST IS DESIGNED TO HELP STUDENTS IN MIAMIDADE COUNTY DISCOVER REWARDING CAREERS IN AGRICULTURE--ESPECIALLY IN AREAS THAT USE MODERN TECHNOLOGY TO GROW FOOD MORE SUSTAINABLY. THROUGH THIS PROJECT, MIAMI DADE COLLEGE (MDC) WILL UPDATE ITS AGRICULTURE ASSOCIATE OF ARTS (AA) PROGRAM AND CREATE NEW INSTRUCTIONAL MODULES THAT INTRODUCE STUDENTS TO PRECISION AGRICULTURE, CLIMATE-SMART FARMING, AGRICULTURAL ROBOTICS, AND SUSTAINABLE GROWING SYSTEMS LIKE HYDROPONICS AND AQUAPONICS. THESE UPDATES WILL MAKE AGRICULTURE EDUCATION MORE ENGAGING, PRACTICAL, AND ALIGNED WITH REAL WORKFORCE NEEDS.EACH YEAR, THE PROGRAM WILL SUPPORT 20 MDC UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS AND 10 LOCAL HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS, ESPECIALLY THOSE FROM HISPANIC, LOW-INCOME, OR OTHERWISE UNDERREPRESENTED BACKGROUNDS. STUDENTS WILL LEARN IN MODERNIZED LABS, TECHNOLOGY CENTERS, GREENHOUSES, AND FIELD STATIONS ON MDC'S NORTH CAMPUS. THEY WILL ALSO PARTICIPATE IN SUMMER RESEARCH, HANDS-ON FIELDWORK, MENTORSHIP, AND CAREER EXPLORATION ACTIVITIES.HARVEST IS A COLLABORATIVE EFFORT. MDC WILL PARTNER WITH ARCHBOLD BIOLOGICAL STATION, FAIRCHILD TROPICAL BOTANICAL GARDENS, UNIVERSITY OF FLORIDA'S TROPICAL RESEARCH AND EDUCATION CENTER (TREC), THE USDA'S SUBTROPICAL HORTICULTURE RESEARCH STATION (SHRS), AND LOCAL AGRICULTURAL ORGANIZATIONS. THESE PARTNERS WILL OFFER INTERNSHIPS, FIELD EXPERIENCES, GUEST LECTURES, AND EXPOSURE TO REAL-WORLD AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH.ULTIMATELY, THE PROJECT AIMS TO:INCREASE A,WARENESS OF USDA AND AGRICULTURE-RELATED CAREERSBOOST ENROLLMENT, RETENTION, AND GRADUATION IN MDC'S AGRICULTURE PROGRAMSSTRENGTHEN THE REGIONAL WORKFORCE BY PREPARING STUDENTS FOR HIGH-DEMAND, TECHNOLOGY-FOCUSED AGRICULTURAL JOBSSUPPORT SOUTH FLORIDA'S LONG-TERM ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL RESILIENCEIN SHORT, HARVEST HELPS STUDENTS GAIN THE SKILLS NEEDED TO THRIVE IN MODERN AGRICULTURE WHILE STRENGTHENING THE FUTURE OF FARMING IN SOUTH FLORIDA.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: HONEY BEES ARE ESSENTIAL TO U.S. AGRICULTURE AND HEALTHY ECOSYSTEMS, YET COLONIES CONTINUE TO FACE SERIOUS LOSSES ASSOCIATED WITH COLONY COLLAPSE DISORDER (CCD). SCIENTISTS STILL LACK CONSENSUS ON THE RELATIVE IMPORTANCE OF KEY STRESSORS (SUCH AS PARASITES, AGROCHEMICALS, AND NUTRITION), IN PART BECAUSE WE RARELY HAVE DETAILED INFORMATION ON WHERE INDIVIDUAL FORAGING BEES ACTUALLY GO IN REAL LANDSCAPES. WITHOUT THAT MISSING PIECE, IT IS DIFFICULT FOR BEEKEEPERS AND LAND MANAGERS TO ANTICIPATE RISKS AND PROTECT COLONIES, WITH POTENTIAL ECONOMIC CONSEQUENCES ON THE ORDER OF BILLIONS OF DOLLARS IN CROP PRODUCTION LOSSES. THIS PROJECT ADDRESSES THAT GAP BY MAKING IT FEASIBLE TO TRACK INDIVIDUAL BEES MORE CONTINUOUSLY AND OVER LARGER AREAS THAN IS CURRENTLY PRACTICAL.TO DO THIS, WE WILL BUILD AND TEST A PORTABLE, LOWER-COST RADAR TRACKING SYSTEM THAT CAN DETECT A TINY TAG PLACED ON A BEE. CURRENT RADAR-BASED BEE TRACKING APPROACHES CAN BE EXPENSIVE AND HARD TO DEPLOY, SO WE WILL DEVELOP A SYSTEM THAT USES A COMMERCIAL RADAR TRANSMITTER PAIRED WITH MULTIPLE RECEIVING UNITS TO EXPAND COVERAGE WHILE CUTTING COST. WE WILL ATTACH VERY SMALL, LIGHTWEIGHT TAGS TO INDIVIDUAL FORAGING BEES AND COLLECT THEIR MOVEMENT PATHS AT AN AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH SITE, WORKING WITH EXTENSION PARTNERS. AT THE SAME TIME, WE WILL MAP THE SURROUNDING LANDSCAPE USING PUBLICLY AVAILABLE LAND-COVER DATA AND DRONE-BASED IMAGERY TO CAPTURE CHANGES IN CROPS AND VEGETATION OVER TIME. BY COMBINING BEE MOVEMENT TRACKS WITH LANDSCAPE INFORMATION, WE WILL DEVELOP MODELS THAT HELP EXPLAIN HOW BEES USE SPACE AND HOW THAT BEHAVIOR MAY CHANGE UNDER DIFFERENT CONDITIONS (INCLUDING EXPOSURE RISK), SUPPORTING MORE INFORMED DECISIONS ABOUT HIVE PLACEMENT AND POLLINATOR HABITAT PROGRAMS. THE PROJECT WILL DELIVER A WORKING TRACKING PLATFORM PLUS OPEN DATASETS/MODELS AND STAKEHOLDER-FACING EXTENSION MATERIALS SO THE RESULTS CAN BE USED BY RESEARCHERS, BEEKEEPERS, AND THE BROADER AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITY.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: PEANUT IS AN IMPORTANT FOOD AND CASH CROP IN THE UNITED STATES AND AROUND THE WORLD. IN THE SOUTHEASTERN U.S.--ESPECIALLY GEORGIA, FLORIDA, AND ALABAMA--PEANUT FARMERS FACE SERIOUS CHALLENGES FROM PESTS AND DISEASES THAT REDUCE YIELD AND INCREASE PRODUCTION COSTS. ONE OF THE MOST DAMAGING PESTS IS THEROOT-KNOT NEMATODE, A MICROSCOPIC SOIL-DWELLING WORM THAT ATTACKS PEANUT ROOTS, WEAKENS PLANTS, AND CAN CAUSE MAJOR YIELD LOSSES. MANAGING THIS PEST OFTEN REQUIRES CHEMICAL TREATMENTS THAT ARE COSTLY AND RAISE ENVIRONMENTAL AND HEALTH CONCERNS.CURRENTLY, NEARLY ALL PEANUT VARIETIES WITH RESISTANCE TO ROOT-KNOT NEMATODE RELY ONA SINGLE, DECADES-OLD RESISTANCE SOURCEFROM ONE WILD PEANUT RELATIVE. RELYING ON A SINGLE RESISTANCE SOURCE IS RISKY, AS PESTS CAN EVENTUALLY OVERCOME IT, AND SOME EXISTING RESISTANT VARIETIES DO NOT YIELD AS WELL AS FARMERS NEED. THERE IS A CLEAR NEED FORNEW PEANUT VARIETIES THAT ARE HIGH-YIELDING, RESISTANT TO NEMATODES AND OTHER DISEASES, AND REQUIRE FEWER CHEMICAL INPUTS.THIS PROJECT ADDRESSES THAT NEED BY USINGNATURAL RESISTANCE FOUND IN WILD RELATIVES OF PEANUT. WILD PEANUT SPECIES CONTAIN STRONG RESISTANCE TO ROOT-KNOT NEMATODE AND OTHER IMPORTANT DISEASES, BUT TRANSFERRING THESE TRAITS INTO CULTIVATED PEANUT IS CHALLENGING. OVER THE PAST DECADE, RESEARCHERS HAVE SUCCESSFULLY MOVED THESE BENEFICIAL TRAITS INTO PEANUT BREEDING LINES USING ADVANCED BREEDING TECHNIQUES. THESE NEW LINES ARE NOW AT THE FINAL STAGES BEFORE RELEASE AS COMMERCIAL VARIETIES.THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TOTEST, REFINE, AND ADVANCE THE MOST PROMISING PEANUT LINESSO THEY CAN BE RELEASED AS NEW CULTIVARS FOR FARMERS. THESE LINES COMBINE NEAR-IMMUNITY TO ROOT-KNOT NEMATODE WITH RESISTANCE TO OTHER MAJOR DISEASES SUCH AS LEAF SPOT AND TOMATO SPOTTED WILT VIRUS, WHILE MAINTAINING HIGH YIELD AND GOOD PROCESSING QUALITY.RESEARCHERS WILL EVALUATE THESE PEANUT LINES IN MULTIPLE FIELD LOCATIONS, INCLUDING FIELDS WITH HIGH NEMATODE PRESSURE. THEY WILL MEASURE YIELD, DISEASE RESISTANCE, PLANT HEALTH, AND KERNEL QUALITY USING STANDARD AGRICULTURAL METHODS. ADVANCED TOOLS SUCH AS DRONE IMAGERY AND GENETIC TESTING WILL HELP ENSURE ACCURATE EVALUATION AND SELECTION. LINES THAT PERFORM BEST WILL BE PREPARED FOR RELEASE THROUGH GEORGIA'S PUBLIC CULTIVAR RELEASE SYSTEM, WHILE OTHERS WILL BE SHARED AS GERMPLASM FOR USE IN FUTURE BREEDING.THE PROJECT ALSO INCLUDESTRAINING THE NEXT GENERATION OF PLANT BREEDERS, INCLUDING A PHD STUDENT, AND CLOSE COLLABORATION WITH EXTENSION SPECIALISTS, SEED PRODUCERS, SHELLERS, AND FOOD COMPANIES. THIS ENSURES THAT NEW VARIETIES MEET THE NEEDS OF FARMERS AND THE PEANUT INDUSTRY.THE EXPECTED BENEFITS OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE:LOWER PRODUCTION COSTSFOR FARMERS DUE TO REDUCED NEED FOR NEMATICIDES, FUNGICIDES, AND INSECTICIDESIMPROVED YIELDS AND YIELD STABILITY, ESPECIALLY IN NEMATODE-INFESTED FIELDSREDUCED ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTFROM LOWER CHEMICAL USEMORE SUSTAINABLE AND RESILIENT PEANUT PRODUCTION SYSTEMSNEW PUBLICLY AVAILABLE PEANUT VARIE,TIES AND GERMPLASMTHAT BENEFIT GROWERS, BREEDERS, AND CONSUMERSOVERALL, THIS PROJECT WILL DELIVER PRACTICAL, FARMER-READY SOLUTIONS THAT STRENGTHEN PEANUT PRODUCTION, PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT, AND SUPPORT RURAL ECONOMIES IN THE SOUTHEASTERN UNITED STATES AND BEYOND.

solicitations.title: OMAO/PIAD, BLACK SWIFT IDIQ, SUAS PLATFORM, OPERATIONAL AND TECHNICAL SUPPORT SERVICES
INL DRONES AND GROUND CONTROL STATIONS
INL DRONES AND GROUND CONTROL STATIONS
TEAMING OF HUMANS AND UNMANNED NETWORKED DRONE RESEARCH

INVASIVE PESTS LIKE INSECTS, DISEASES, AND WEEDS HARM U.S. FORESTS AND FARMS BY KILLING TREES, REDUCING BIODIVERSITY, AND COSTING BILLIONS YEARLY. THIS PROJECT TACKLES EARLY DETECTION USING DRONES, SATELLITES, AND SMART COMPUTER PROGRAMS TO SPOT THESE THREATS BEFORE THEY SPREAD. THE MAIN AIM IS TO BUILD TOOLS FOR QUICK PEST DETECTION AND RESPONSE IN FORESTS. IN THE SHORT TERM, IT CREATES A COMPUTER SYSTEM THAT COMBINES DIFFERENT DATA TYPES AND LEARNS OVER TIME. IN THE LONG TERM, IT HELPS PROTECT AGRICULTURE AND FORESTSNATIONWIDE. THREE KEY PARTS GUIDE THE WORK: GATHERING AERIAL IMAGES AND DATA, BUILDING THE SMART DETECTION SYSTEM, AND TEACHING K-12 STUDENTS AND STAKEHOLDERS. THE PROJECT TEAMS WILL FLY DRONES OVER FORESTS IN PENNSYLVANIA AND WEST VIRGINIA. DRONES CARRY CAMERAS FOR COLOR PHOTOS, THERMAL SENSORS, SPECTRAL SENSORS, AND 3D SCANS. SATELLITES ADD WIDE-VIEW IMAGES FROM SPACE. GROUND TEAMS CHECK SPOTS ON FOOT TO CONFIRM THE PRESENCE OF INVASIVE PESTS. THEN, COMPUTERS WILL BLEND DRONE CLOSE-UPS WITH SATELLITE OVERVIEWS TO HIGHLIGHT PEST SIGNS. THE SYSTEM LEARNS CONTINUALLY AND GETS FEEDBACK FROM EXPERTS, WHICH MAKES IT ADAPT TO NEW PESTS OR SEASONS FOR EARLY DETECTION AND RAPID RESPONSES TO INVASIVE PESTS. FROM THIS PROJECT, NEW COLLEGE CLASSES AT WEST VIRGINIA UNIVERSITY AND SUNY ALBANY TEACH STUDENTS ABOUT DRONES, AI, AND FOREST HEALTH THROUGH HANDS-ON LABS AND FIELD TRIPS. MATERIALS TURN INTO FREE ONLINE RESOURCES FOR SCHOOLS AND THE PUBLIC, INCLUDING VIDEOS, GUIDES, AND WORKSHOPS.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: COORDINATED UNCREWED AERIAL SYSTEM SAMPLING OF BOUNDARY LAYER STRUCTURES -THE ATMOSPHERE ADJACENT TO THE GROUND IS CALLED THE ATMOSPHERIC BOUNDARY LAYER. IT PLAYS AN IMPORTANT ROLE IN INTERACTIONS BETWEEN EARTH?S SURFACE AND THE ATMOSPHERE. AIR FLOW IN THE BOUNDARY LAYER CAN BE TURBULENT, WHICH IS DIFFICULT TO MODEL. NEVERTHELESS, THE SUCCESS OF WEATHER MODELS DEPENDS CRITICALLY ON ACCURATE REPRESENTATIONS OF THE BOUNDARY LAYER. CURRENT MODELS DO NOT ACCOUNT FOR LARGE, ORGANIZED, ROLLING AIR CURRENTS THAT STRONGLY AFFECT FLOW INSIDE THE BOUNDARY LAYER. MOST STUDIES OF THESE ORGANIZED ROLLING MOTIONS, WHICH ARE CALLED COHERENT STRUCTURES, ARE BASED ON LABORATORY EXPERIMENTS OR COMPUTER SIMULATIONS. THIS PROJECT WILL USE FORMATIONS OF FIXED-WING DRONES TO COLLECT REAL-WORLD DATA ON AIR MOVEMENT INSIDE THE BOUNDARY LAYER. THE STUDIES WILL BE DESIGNED TO IDENTIFY KEY FACTORS THAT GOVERN THE FORMATION OF ORGANIZED ROLLING MOTIONS AND THEIR SUBSEQUENT BEHAVIOR. THE PROJECT WILL PROVIDE OPPORTUNITIES FOR UNDERGRADUATE STUDENTS TO PARTICIPATE IN RESEARCH. THEY WILL BE KEY MEMBERS OF THE TEAM, SERVING AS SYSTEM OPERATORS, SAFETY OFFICERS, AND AIRCRAFT MANAGERS, WHILE BEING TRAINED ON THE SCIENTIFIC OBJECTIVES OF THE RESEARCH. THE MEASUREMENTS WILL FOCUS ON EVALUATING SEVERAL HYPOTHESES ABOUT THE FORMATION AND INFLUENCE OF A STRUCTURE CALLED HORIZONTAL CONVECTIVE ROLLS. THE PROJECT WILL INVESTIGATE (1) THE CRITICAL STABILITY THRESHOLD AT WHICH HORIZONTAL CONVECTIVE ROLLS DEVELOP; (2) WHETHER THEIR FORMATION MECHANISM REPRESENTS AN EVOLUTION OF SUPERSTRUCTURES OBSERVED UNDER NEUTRAL STABILITY CONDITIONS; (3) THE CONTRIBUTION OF ROLL STRUCTURES TO MASS, MOMENTUM, AND ENERGY TRANSPORT UNDER CONDITIONS IN WHICH THEY FORM; AND (4) THEIR INFLUENCE ON THE ANISOTROPY OF SMALLER-SCALE TURBULENCE. MEASUREMENTS WILL BE CONDUCTED USING A LEADER-FOLLOWER APPROACH, IN WHICH A LEAD AIRCRAFT IS TASKED WITH NAVIGATION AND THE REST ARE TASKED WITH MAINTAINING THEIR RELATIVE SPATIAL SEPARATION. THIS SEPARATION WILL BE DESIGNED SO THAT WIND VELOCITY MEASUREMENTS MADE BY EACH AIRCRAFT CAN BE USED TO DETERMINE THE VELOCITY GRADIENT TENSOR, YIELDING UNPRECEDENTED INFORMATION ABOUT THE VORTICITY AND TURBULENCE WITHIN THE COHERENT STRUCTURES. THIS INFORMATION WILL SUPPORT THE BROADER OBJECTIVES OF THE PROJECT BY ENABLING ADVANCES IN BOUNDARY LAYER PARAMETERIZATIONS, ULTIMATELY ENHANCING THE ACCURACY OF METEOROLOGICAL MODELS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

FIRE-WUI: COMMUNITY RESILIENCE ASSESSMENT FRAMEWORK FOR WILDFIRES USING MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS -WILDFIRES POSE A GROWING THREAT TO COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE US, PARTICULARLY IN AREAS WHERE HOMES AND NATURAL LANDSCAPES MEET, KNOWN AS THE WILDLAND-URBAN INTERFACE (WUI). EACH YEAR, MORE THAN 60,000 WILDFIRES OCCUR IN THE US, OFTEN RESULTING IN TRAGIC LOSS OF LIFE, DESTRUCTION OF HOMES, AND SEVERE ECONOMIC DISRUPTION. EFFECTIVE WILDFIRE RESPONSE AND PREVENTION EFFORTS ARE HINDERED BY A LACK OF DETAILED, UP-TO-DATE INFORMATION ABOUT VEGETATION AND BUILDINGS IN THESE HIGH-RISK AREAS. TO ADDRESS THESE CHALLENGES, THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP A FRAMEWORK THAT CREATES DETAILED, 3D DIGITAL REPRESENTATIONS, REFERRED TO HERE AS DIGITAL TWINS, OF BOTH NATURAL AND BUILT ENVIRONMENTS. THESE DIGITAL TWINS WILL PROVIDE ACCURATE, LOCATION-SPECIFIC INFORMATION NEEDED TO SIMULATE HOW FIRES SPREAD AND ENABLE THE DEVELOPMENT OF MORE EFFECTIVE MITIGATION STRATEGIES. THE INTELLECTUAL MERIT OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDES THE CREATION OF A GROUNDBREAKING FRAMEWORK FOR GENERATING SCALABLE, MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS THAT COMBINE DATA FROM SATELLITES, DRONES, AND GROUND SENSORS. BY INTEGRATING AI AND ADVANCED MODELING TECHNIQUES, THE SYSTEM WILL ENHANCE THE UNDERSTANDING OF FIRE BEHAVIOR IN WUI ENVIRONMENTS AND SUPPORT MORE ACCURATE FIRE-SPREAD PREDICTIONS. THIS RESEARCH REPRESENTS A CONVERGENT AND INTERDISCIPLINARY APPROACH THAT COMBINES GEOSPATIAL SCIENCE, WILDFIRE MODELING, AND DATA ANALYTICS IN NOVEL WAYS STILL UNREALIZED AT THIS SCALE. THE BROADER IMPACTS OF THIS PROJECT ARE SIGNIFICANT. THE DIGITAL TWIN FRAMEWORK WILL HELP IMPROVE COMMUNITY RESILIENCE AND PREPAREDNESS AGAINST WILDFIRES BY PROVIDING FIRST RESPONDERS, PLANNERS, AND POLICYMAKERS WITH THE TOOLS TO MAKE FASTER, DATA-DRIVEN DECISIONS. THE SYSTEM IS DESIGNED TO SCALE NATIONWIDE AND CAN BE ADAPTED TO OTHER TYPES OF NATURAL DISASTERS. THE PROJECT WILL ALSO PREPARE THE NEXT GENERATION OF EXPERTS THROUGH INTERDISCIPLINARY EDUCATION AND TRAINING PROGRAMS, INCLUDING NEW COURSES, ONLINE LEARNING MODULES, MENTORSHIP, AND PROFESSIONAL TRAINING DEVELOPED IN COLLABORATION WITH INDUSTRY ORGANIZATIONS. THESE EFFORTS WILL ENSURE A MORE KNOWLEDGEABLE WORKFORCE READY TO CONFRONT THE COMPLEX CHALLENGES OF WILDFIRE MANAGEMENT. THE ?COMMUNITY RESILIENCE ASSESSMENT FRAMEWORK AGAINST WUI-FIRES USING MULTI-RESOLUTION DIGITAL TWINS ? (CRAFT)? PROJECT AIMS TO DEVELOP A SCALABLE, DYNAMIC DIGITAL TWIN FRAMEWORK TO SUPPORT WILDFIRE MODELING AND MITIGATION IN THE WUI. CRAFT WILL INTEGRATE HETEROGENEOUS GEOSPATIAL DATA FROM SATELLITE, AERIAL, AND GROUND-BASED PLATFORMS USING AI-BASED SUPER-RESOLUTION, GENERATIVE MODELING, AND QA/QC PROTOCOLS TO CREATE MULTI-RESOLUTION, CONTINUOUSLY UPDATED 3D MODELS. THESE DIGITAL TWINS WILL FEED INTO ENHANCED FIRE-SPREAD MODELS TO IMPROVE PREDICTION ACCURACY AND INVESTIGATE THE TRADE-OFF AMONG RESOLUTION, PERFORMANCE, AND COMPUTATIONAL COMPLEXITY. THE IMPROVED MODELS ARE THEN USED TO EVALUATE MITIGATION STRATEGIES. VALIDATION WILL OCCUR THROUGH TESTBEDS WITH DISTINCT CHARACTERISTICS IN THE U.S. EASTERN AND WESTERN WUI REGIONS. EDUCATIONAL COMPONENTS WILL SUPPORT WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT IN GEOSPATIAL AND WILDFIRE SCIENCES. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

AGRICULTURAL RUNOFF IS A MAJOR CONTRIBUTOR TO WATER POLLUTION IN THE UNITED STATES, AFFECTING DRINKING WATER SUPPLIES, LOCAL LAKES AND RIVERS, AND DOWNSTREAM ECOSYSTEMS SUCH AS THE GULF OF AMERICA. IN THE MIDWEST, UNDERGROUND DRAINAGE PIPES INSTALLED BENEATH FARM FIELDS QUICKLY CARRY EXCESS NUTRIENTS AND FARM CHEMICALS INTO NEARBY STREAMS, OFTEN WITHOUT BEING NOTICED OR MEASURED. WHILE PRACTICES SUCH AS PLANTING COVER CROPS CAN REDUCE NUTRIENT LOSSES, THEIR EFFECTIVENESS VARIES ACROSS LANDSCAPES AND SEASONS. AT THE SAME TIME, INCREASED USE OF HERBICIDES TO MANAGE THESE PRACTICES RAISES CONCERNS ABOUT UNINTENDED ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS. MONITORING WATER QUALITY AT THESE DRAINAGE OUTLETS IS DIFFICULT, EXPENSIVE, AND TIME-CONSUMING USING CURRENT METHODS, LEAVING FARMERS, COMMUNITIES, AND POLICYMAKERS WITH LIMITED INFORMATION TO GUIDE DECISIONS THAT PROTECT BOTH AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY AND WATER RESOURCES.THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP AND TEST A NEW LAB-ON-A-DRONE SYSTEM THAT CAN SAFELY AND QUICKLY COLLECT AND ANALYZE WATER SAMPLES FROM HARD-TO-REACH LOCATIONS SUCH AS FIELD DRAINAGE OUTLETS AND NARROW STREAMS. INSTEAD OF TRANSPORTING SAMPLES BACK TO A LABORATORY, THE DRONE WILL CARRY LIGHTWEIGHT SENSORS THAT MEASURE NUTRIENTS, OXYGEN LEVELS, AND COMMONLY USED FARM CHEMICALS DIRECTLY AT THE SITE. THE SYSTEM WILL BE TESTED IN REAL AGRICULTURAL WATERSHEDS TO TRACK HOW WATER QUALITY CHANGES OVER TIME AND TO COMPARE AREAS WITH AND WITHOUT COVER CROPS. BY MAKING WATER QUALITY DATA EASIER TO COLLECT AND MORE FREQUENT, THIS PROJECT AIMS TO PROVIDE FARMERS, WATERSHED MANAGERS, AND COMMUNITIES WITH BETTER TOOLS TO EVALUATE CONSERVATION PRACTICES, REDUCE POLLUTION, AND PROTECT LOCAL AND DOWNSTREAM WATERS. ULTIMATELY, THE PROJECT SUPPORTS HEALTHIER ECOSYSTEMS, SAFER WATER SUPPLIES, AND MOREECONOMICALLY RESILIENT AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS.

solicitations.title: THE PROJECT AIMS TO SPEED UP THE DEVELOPMENT OF POTATO VARIETIES THAT CAN THRIVE IN THE HOT, HUMID CLIMATE OF THE SOUTHERN U.S. TRADITIONAL BREEDING CYCLES CAN TAKE TEN YEARS OR MORE, BUT THE TEAM PLANS TO CUT THAT TIME BY COMBINING THREE MODERN TOOLS: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE (AI), HIGH?THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING (HTP), AND CROP?SIMULATION MODELS LINKED TO WHOLE?GENOME PREDICTION (WGP). FIRST, AN INEXPENSIVE RGB VIDEO CAMERA WILL BE INSTALLED ON THE POST?HARVEST GRADING LINE. THE CAMERA CAPTURES EVERY TUBER AS IT MOVES THROUGH THE SORTER, AND AI MODELS WILL AUTOMATICALLY MEASURE SIZE, WEIGHT, SKIN COLOR AND DETECT DEFECTS SUCH AS CRACKS OR GREENING. SECOND, UNMANNED AERIAL VEHICLES (UAVS) AND HIGH?RESOLUTION SATELLITE IMAGERY WILL BE USED TO COLLECT MULTISPECTRAL, HYPERSPECTRAL AND 3?D POINT?CLOUD DATA FROM THE FIELD. AI ALGORITHMS THAT FUSE THESE MULTIPLE SENSOR STREAMS WILL ESTIMATE ABOVE?GROUND BIOMASS, TUBER BIOMASS, NITROGEN UPTAKE AND YIELD THROUGHOUT THE GROWING SEASON. THIRD, THE AI?DERIVED TRAIT ESTIMATES WILL BE FED INTO A POTATO CROP?SIMULATION MODEL (E.G., DSSAT?SUBSTOR?POTATO MODEL) THAT HAS BEEN ENHANCED TO HANDLE HEAT?STRESS AND SPECIFIC?GRAVITY EFFECTS TYPICAL OF SUBTROPICAL CONDITIONS. FURTHER CALIBRATION OF GENOTYPE?SPECIFIC PARAMETERS, AND THE CALIBRATED MODEL WILL BE COMBINED WITH WHOLE?GENOME PREDICTION TO FORECAST HOW EACH GENOTYPE WILL PERFORM ACROSS A RANGE OF ENVIRONMENTS, MANAGEMENT PRACTICES AND CLIMATE SCENARIOS. THE WORK WILL BE CARRIED OUT OVER THREE YEARS ON MORE THAN 7,000 EXPERIMENTAL PLOTS REPRESENTING OVER 2,000 DISTINCT GENOTYPES. DATA COLLECTION, MODEL DEVELOPMENT AND VALIDATION ARE ORGANIZED INTO A SERIES OF EXPERIMENTS THAT PROGRESS FROM VIDEO?BASED TUBER PHENOTYPING, THROUGH UAV?BASED FIELD PHENOTYPING, TO THE FINAL INTEGRATED AI?CSM?WGP FRAMEWORK. ALL CODE, MODELS AND CURATED DATASETS WILL BE RELEASED OPENLY ON REPOSITORIES, AND THE TEAM WILL PUBLISH FINDINGS IN OPEN?ACCESS JOURNALS AND PRESENT THEM AT CONFERENCES. BY PROVIDING RAPID, LOW?COST, AND HIGHLY ACCURATE PHENOTYPIC INFORMATION, THE PROJECT IS EXPECTED TO (1) SHORTEN THE BREEDING CYCLE, (2) LOWER LABOR AND RESEARCH EXPENSES, (3) IMPROVE THE PHYSIOLOGICAL REALISM OF CROP MODELS UNDER CLIMATE?STRESS CONDITIONS, AND (4) DELIVER NEW, HIGH?YIELDING, HEAT?TOLERANT POTATO VARIETIES THAT SUPPORT THE LONG?TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF THE POTATO INDUSTRY AND CONTRIBUTE TO NATIONAL FOOD?SECURITY GOALS. THE APPROACH ALSO CREATES A TRAINING GROUND FOR POST?DOCTORAL RESEARCHERS AND GRADUATE STUDENTS IN AI, REMOTE SENSING, CROP MODELING AND GENOMICS, BUILDING A SKILLED WORKFORCE THAT CAN APPLY THESE METHODS TO OTHER CROPS AND REGIONS.

solicitations.title: UNDERWATER DRONE
ON CALL/CALL WHEN NEEDED UAS SERVICES

TIMELY FOREKNOWLEDGE OF SOIL WATER CONTENT (SWC) AND SOIL ORGANIC CONTENT (SOC) HAS THE POTENTIAL TO STRONGLY IMPACT WATERING AND SEQUESTRATION DECISIONS, THROUGHOUT THE GROWING SEASON. BUT CURRENTLY, MONITORING, REPORTING, AND VERIFICATION (MRV) OF THESE IS COSTLY AND TIME-CONSUMING. BARRIERS INCLUDE HIGH EQUIPMENT COSTS, INFRASTRUCTURE INSTALLATION, AND SENSING CAPABILITIES. OUR RECENT TECHNOLOGICAL BREAKTHROUGH IN AERIAL ROBOTICS, THE CAPABILITY TO DIG INTO SOIL, COUPLED WITH ADVANCES IN SENSING TECHNOLOGIES GIVES US THE ABILITY TO BUILD UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (UAS) TO LARGELY AUTOMATE THIS PROCESS. WE ADDRESS THE ISSUE OF SOC/SWC MONITORING, REPORTING, AND VERIFICATION BY BUILDING A MULTI-AGENT UAS TEAM AND ACCOMPANYING CONTROLLERS, TASK PLANNERS, AND MACHINE-LEARNING CLASSIFIERS CAPABLE OF PERSISTENT ATMOSPHERIC MONITORING VIA TETHERED UAS, AND HETEROGENEOUS SAMPLING UAS FOR INSERTION OF KEY SENSOR PROBES, AND EXTRACTION OF SOIL SAMPLES FOR AUTOMATED COLLECTION. TOGETHER THE UAS AND ALGORITHMS PROVIDE A MECHANISM TO COLLECT AUTOMATED, ACCURATE, AND HIGH TEMPORAL AND SPATIAL RESOLUTION (E.G., MUCH HIGHER THAN SATELLITES) SWC AND SOC DATA WHICH WE THEN MAKE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC. THE DATA CAN BE EASILY USED TO HELP MAKE TIMELY AGRICULTURAL, SEQUESTRATION, AND WATER MANAGEMENT DECISIONS BY STAKEHOLDERS.

solicitations.title: INSECTS AND PLANT DISEASES THREATEN FRUIT PRODUCTION, FOOD SECURITY, AND FARM PROFITABILITY, YET DETECTING THESE PROBLEMS EARLY IS DIFFICULT AND LABOR-INTENSIVE. MOST ORCHARD PEST SCOUTING IS DONE MANUALLY, REQUIRING PEOPLE TO WALK THROUGH FIELDS AND VISUALLY INSPECT TREES, WHICH IS COSTLY, TIME-CONSUMING, AND OFTEN MISSES PESTS HIDDEN INSIDE DENSE CANOPIES. WHEN PESTS ARE NOT DETECTED EARLY, GROWERS MAY SUFFER YIELD LOSSES OR APPLY PESTICIDES BROADLY AS A PRECAUTION, INCREASING COSTS AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS. IMPROVING HOW PESTS ARE DETECTED IS IMPORTANT NOT ONLY FOR FARMERS, BUT ALSO FOR CONSUMERS AND COMMUNITIES THAT DEPEND ON RELIABLE, AFFORDABLE, AND ENVIRONMENTALLY RESPONSIBLE FOOD PRODUCTION.THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP AND TEST AN AUTOMATED PEST SCOUTING SYSTEM THAT USES SMALL AERIAL DRONES AND GROUND-BASED ROBOTIC VEHICLES TO MOVE THROUGH ORCHARDS AND COLLECT CLOSE-UP IMAGES INSIDE TREE CANOPIES. COMPUTER TOOLS WILL ANALYZE THESE IMAGES TO IDENTIFY PESTS AND PLANT DISEASES AND CREATE THREE-DIMENSIONAL MAPS SHOWING WHERE PROBLEMS OCCUR WITHIN TREES AND ACROSS ORCHARDS. BY PROVIDING FASTER AND MORE PRECISE PEST INFORMATION, THIS APPROACH AIMS TO SUPPORT TARGETED PEST MANAGEMENT, REDUCE UNNECESSARY PESTICIDE USE, LOWER PRODUCTION COSTS, AND IMPROVE THE LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF FRUIT PRODUCTION SYSTEMS, BENEFITING AGRICULTURE, THE ENVIRONMENT, AND THE BROADER PUBLIC.

solicitations.title: ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED WITH THE FUNDS FROM THE AWARD. TO ENHANCE THE AQUACULTURE RESEARCH STATION TO INCLUDE AN INDOOR HATCHERY, SEVERAL RESEARCH LABORATORIES, FEED PROCESSING AND STORAGE ROOMS, MACHINE SHOPS, AND A GREENHOUSE OFTEN RIGGED TO DEMONSTRATE AQUAPONICS DESIGN AND PRODUCTION. IT IS FOR DEPARTMENTAL TOURS AND A VENUE TO HOST RECRUITMENT AND OUTREACH EVENTS SUCH AS THE AQUATIC SCIENCES DAY FOR LOCAL HIGH SCHOOLS AND FIELD DAY TO DEMONSTRATE CURRENT RESEARCH PROJECTS. AS DEMAND FROM THE AQUACULTURE INDUSTRY HAS CHANGED OVER THE PAST SEVERAL YEARS, WE WILL REPURPOSE THIS FACILITY INTO A MULTIUSE MULTIDISCIPLINARY DEMONSTRATION SITE AND ECOSYSTEM THAT INCORPORATE ALL THREE DEPARTMENTS OF THE SCHOOL OF AGRICULTURE, FISHERIES, AND HUMAN SCIENCES. IN ADDITION, PURCHASE NEW FARM EQUIPMENT FOR ALL THREE FARMS (LONOKE, MARIANNA AND ON-CAMPUS IN PINE BLUFF) AND MUCH NEEDED REPAIRS TO EXISTING EQUIPMENT. THE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SCIENCES PROGRAM WILL BEGIN THE KITCHEN LAYOUT FOR A TEACHING KITCHEN SPACE TO DESIGN AND RENOVATE A STATE-OF-THE-ART 'TEACHING KITCHEN' AT UAPB. THE PROPOSED 3,500 SQUARE FEET SPACE WILL FEATURE INDUCTION COOKTOPS, INDUSTRIAL OVENS AND RANGES, REACH-IN REFRIGERATORS AND FREEZER EQUIPMENT, FOOD STORAGE EQUIPMENT, VENT HOODS, GRANITE-TOPPED COUNTERS, COOKING EQUIPMENT, COMMERCIAL CARTS AND SHELVING, AND ROUND TABLES FOR CLASSROOM-STYLE SEATING. IN ADDITION, ACQUISITION OF PROPERTY (FISH PROCESS IN FACILITY) IN EUDORA, AR. AS IT RELATES TO RESEARCH, FUNDING TO ENHANCE OUR OVERALL LAB SPACES AND ENSURE THAT OUR FUME HOODS ARE UPDATED AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CURRENT DAY STANDARDS. WHEREIN, WE WILL INCREASE THE NUMBER OF 1890 FACULTY RESEARCHERS IN 1890 AS WELL AS EMPLOY MORE UNDERGRADUATES AND GRADUATE STUDENTS TO WORK ON FACULTY RESEARCH PROJECTS THROUGHOUT SAFHS. TO ENHANCE OUR STUDENTS' ABILITY FOR HANDS-ON WORK EXPERIENCES NEEDED FOR THE WORKFORCE. MOREOVER, FUNDING WILL ALLOW HIRING AND RETAINING OUR EXISTING HIGHLY SKILLS PERSONNEL TO ASSIST US IN CARRYING OUR LAND-GRANT MISSION. LASTLY, FUNDING TO BETTER EXPOSE OUR UNDERGRADUATE AND GRADUATE STUDENTS TO LOCAL, REGIONAL AND NATIONAL CONFERENCES. DELIVERABLES AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES FROM THE ACTIVITIES. TO ENHANCE THE 1890 RESEARCH, WE WILL BE RENOVATING THE POULTRY SCIENCE BUILDING ON CAMPUS, WHICH HAS BEEN DOWN FOR THE PAST 10 YEARS. THIS RENOVATION WILL ALLOW CLASSROOM INSTRUCTION AND LAB SPACE TO CONDUCT CUTTING-EDGE RESEARCH IN THE UNIT. UPGRADES TO ALL THE OUTDATED COMPUTERS TO MORE MODERN COMPUTERS THROUGHOUT THE UNITS WITHIN SAFHS. ENHANCE OUR OVERALL LAB SPACES AND ENSURE THAT OUR FUMES ARE UPDATED AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CURRENT STANDARDS. HIRE AND RETAIN OUR EXISTING HIGHLY SKILLS PERSONNEL TO ASSIST US IN CARRYING OUR LAND-GRANT MISSION. EXPOSING OUR UNDERGRADUATE AND GRADUATE STUDENTS TO LOCAL, REGIONAL AND NATIONAL CONFERENCE INTENDED BENEFICIARIES OF THE ACTIVITIES. LOCAL HIGH SCHOOLS AND FIELD DAY TO DEMONSTRATE CURRENT RESEARCH PROJECTS FROM UAPB FACULTY AND INTERACTION WITH OUR STATE AND PRIVATE FISH CULTURE STAKEHOLDERS. ALL THREE FARMS (LONOKE, MARIANNA AND ON CAMPUS IN PINE BLUFF) NEEDED REPAIRS TO EXISTING EQUIPMENT, TO BETTER SERVE OUR CLIENTELE BASE AND ASSIST OUR RESEARCHERS IN THEIR EFFORT OF CONDUCTING CUTTING-EDGE EVIDENCE BASE RESEARCH. TO ENHANCE OUR STUDENTS' ABILITY TO REACH ON-HANDS WORK EXPERIENCE THAT IS NEEDED TO PREPARE THEM FOR THE WORKFORCE ONCE THEY GRADUATE FROM UAPB. HENCE, ENHANCING OUR UAPB STUDENTS, FACULTY AND STAFF AND OVERALL LOCAL AND STATEWIDE CLIENTELE IN LEARNING THE FUNDAMENTALS OF DELICIOUS COOKING MEALS WHILE GAINING KNOWLEDGE OF NUTRITION, SUSTAINABILITY, AND KITCHEN SAFETY.

solicitations.title: THE HATCH ACT OF 1887 (REGULAR RESEARCH FUND) PROGRAM ASSISTS STATE AGRICULTURAL EXPERIMENT STATIONS, THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, AND THE U.S. INSULAR AREAS IN CONDUCTING AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH PROGRAMS. THESE ACTIVITIES ARE BROAD AND INCLUDE RESEARCH ON ALL ASPECTS OF AGRICULTURE, INCLUDING SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION AND USE; PLANT AND ANIMAL PRODUCTION, PROTECTION, AND HEALTH; PROCESSING, DISTRIBUTION, SAFETY, MARKETING, AND UTILIZATION OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTS; FORESTRY, INCLUDING RANGE MANAGEMENT AND RANGE PRODUCTS; MULTIPLE USE OF FOREST RANGELANDS, AND URBAN FORESTRY; AQUACULTURE; HOME ECONOMICS AND FAMILY LIFE; HUMAN NUTRITION; RURAL AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT; SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE; MOLECULAR BIOLOGY; AND BIOTECHNOLOGY. RESEARCH ADDRESSES PROBLEMS OF LOCAL, STATE, REGIONAL, NATIONAL, AND INTERNATIONAL CONCERN AND INTEREST. THE UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS DIVISION OF AGRICULTURE (UADA) UTILIZES HATCH FUNDING TO SUPPLEMENT STATE APPROPRIATION SUPPORTED FACULTY SALARIES AS WELL AS EXPENDITURES FOR SUPPLIES THAT DIRECTLY SUPPORT IMPLEMENTATION OF HATCH PROJECTS. DIVISION RESEARCHERS CONDUCT BASIC AND APPLIED RESEARCH FOR ARKANSAS PRODUCERS, BUSINESSES, COMMUNIT IES, AND FAMILIES. THE UADA FACULTY HAS A LONG HISTORY OF PROVIDING LEADERSHIP IN THE DEVELOPMENT, DISSEMINATION AND DELIVERY OF KNOWLEDGE, INNOVATIVE PRACTICES, AND EMERGING TECHNOLOGY.

solicitations.title: SOUTHERN UNIVERSITY AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH AND EXTENSION CENTER UTILIZES THE SECTION 1445 FUNDING TO ADVANCE ITS AGENDA ASSOCIATED WITH FOOD AND AGRICULTURAL PROJECTS BENEFITTING THE STATE OF LOUISIANA'S STAKEHOLDERS, AS REFLECTED IN ITS APPROVED 5-YEAR PLAN OF WORK (POW). THE POW CONSISTS OF A CONSOLIDATED AND INTEGRATED PLAN FOR RESEARCH AND EXTENSION PROGRAMS AT BOTH SOUTHERN AND LOUISIANA STATE UNIVERSITIES. THE MISSION OF SUAREC IS TO ENHANCE THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR PEOPLE IN LOUISIANA THROUGH MISSION-ORIENTED BASIC AND APPLIED RESEARCH THAT ADDRESSES SCIENTIFIC, TECHNOLOGICAL, SOCIAL, ECONOMIC AND CULTURAL NEEDS OF ITS CITIZENS. TO ADDRESS EMERGING ISSUES OF ITS STAKEHOLDERS, EMPHASIS IS PLACED IN FIVE PROGRAMMING AREAS: (1) FAMILY AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT: FOCUSING OF ISSUES AFFECTING INDIVIDUALS, FAMILIES, COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT; DISASTER, EPIDEMIC AND PANDEMIC PREPAREDNESS, MITIGATION AND RECOVERY; RISK AWARENESS; SUSTAINABLE HOUSING; AGRISECURITY; AND AGRI-TOURISM. (2). FOOD SECURITY <(>&<)> SYSTEM: FOCUSED ON INCREASING THE PROFITABILITY OF LOUISIANA'S ANIMAL, PLANT, AND AQUACULTURE SYSTEMS AND IMPROVED FOOD ACCESSIBILITY, AFFORDABILITY, POLICY; FOOD SAFETY, TO INCLUDE SEAFOOD, PRODUCE, AND PROCESSED COMMODITIES - SAFETY, CERTIFICATION PROGRAMS, FARM FINANCING, AND VALUE-ADDED PRODUCTS USING THE NEWLY TRADEMARKED 'SUNFOODS' DESIGNATION. (3). NATURAL RESOURCES <(>&<)> ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY: FOCUSES ON CONSERVATION AND PRESERVATION OF NATURAL RESOURCES AND PARTICULARLY THE HEALTH OF STATE'S GRAZING LANDS, STATE'S WILDLIFE, WETLAND PLANTS, WATER, WASTE MANAGEMENT, LANDSCAPES, EFFECTS FORESTRY, INDUSTRY, FEEDSTOCKS, ALTERNATIVE BIOFUELS, BAGASSE ALTERNATIVES, BIOMASS PROCESSING AND THE IMPACTS ON THE ENVIRONMENT. (4) OBESITY, HEALTH <(>&<)> WELLNESS FOCUSES ON INCREASED CONSUMPTION OF 'CLEAN' FOODS WHILE REDUCING INTAKES OF 'ULTRA-PROCESSED' FOODS AND DEVELOPING PROGRAMS ADDRESSING DECREASING OBESITY LEVELS IN ALL AGES, INCREASING CONSUMPTION OF FRUITS AND VEGETABLES, INCREASING TIME IN PHYSICAL ACTIVITIES; COMPLETE PHYSICAL, MENTAL, AND SOCIAL WELL-BEING OF ALL AGES; POLICY, SYSTEM AND ENVIRONMENTAL CHANGE; AND RELATED TO NUTRITION/HEALTH/WELLNESS ISSUES TO INCLUDE EPIDEMIC AND PANDEMIC PREVENTION; AND (5) YOUTH DEVELOPMENT: FOCUSES ON PROVIDING LOUISIANA YOUTH WITH POSITIVE DEVELOPMENT OPPORTUNITIES AND ACTIVITIES TO BUILD INSPIRATIONAL LEADERS AND HEALTHY CITIZENS. THESE PROGRAMS AND ASSOCIATED OUTCOMES ARE ACCESSIBLE TO STAKEHOLDERS ACROSS THE ENTIRE STATE OF LOUISIANA AND DISSEMINATED IN SEVERAL MODALITIES, INCLUDING RESEARCH CONDUCTED ON CAMPUS AND EXPERIMENT STATIONS, AND OFF-CAMPUS AT DIFFERENT STAKEHOLDER AND PARTNER FARMS AND WORK SITES. FACULTY, STUDENTS, STAFF, AND ADMINISTRATORS ACTIVELY PARTICIPATE IN PROGRAM DESIGN, PROGRAM EXECUTION, DEVELOPMENT, DELIVERY AND ASSESSMENT. TO MEET THE EVER-EVOLVING NEEDS OF STAKEHOLDERS IN THE AGRICULTURAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES COMMUNITIES, FREQUENT INTERACTIONS WITH STAKEHOLDER GROUPS WILL ENSURE RELEVANCE IN NEW AND EMERGING PROGRAMMING AREAS ADDRESSING THOSE NEEDS AND MAINTAIN FLEXIBILITY TO ALTER PLANS AS SITUATIONS ARISE. INCREASED USE OF TOOLS, SUCH AS A I, SOCIAL MEDIA, TELEVISION, FIELD DAYS, PASTURE WALKS; ALL WILL INCREASE PUBLIC AWARENESS AND MARKET PENETRATION OF THESE FAR-REACHING PROGRAMS. OHER VENUES ALONG WITH INCREASED FUNDING WILL ALLOW MORE FOCUSED AND CURRENT NEEDS TO BE ADDRESSED AT THE FARM, HOME AND SOCIETY LEVEL. THE SU AG CENTER WILL CONTINUE TO ENSURE RESOURCES ARE AVAILABLE AND APPROPRIATELY ALLOCATED TO ADDRESS THOSE MOST CRITICAL AND AREAS OF AGRICULTURE, HEALTH AND NUTRITION OF THE CONSUMING PUBLIC AND BE FLEXIBLE TO ALTER PLANS AS SITUATION ARISE.
