solicitations.title: KENTUCKY STATE UNIVERSITY'S 1890 COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM SERVES YOUTH, FARMERS, FAMILIES, AND COMMUNITIES THROUGHOUT THE STATE. TO ENSURE THAT OUR WORK IS IMPACTFUL, RESEARCHBASED, AND DEMAND-DRIVEN, KENTUCKY STATE UNIVERSITY CONDUCTS REGULAR NEEDS ASSESSMENTS OF OUR FELLOW KENTUCKIANS, COLLABORATES WITH OUR 1890 LAND-GRANT RESEARCHERS, AND STRATEGICALLY DELIVERS EDUCATIONAL AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS. OUR EXTENSION PROGRAMMING COVERS AGRICULTURE EDUCATION THROUGH PRODUCTION DEMONSTRATIONS, MONTHLY THIRD THURSDAY THING WORKSHOPS, AND ONE-ON-ONE TECHNICAL SUPPORT TO FARMERS FROM OUR EDUCATORS IN TOPICS SUCH AS SOIL MANAGEMENT, POND MANAGEMENT, CLIMATE-SMART AGRICULTURE, ORGANIC AGRICULTURE, HORTICULTURE, TREE FRUIT CULTURE, APICULTURE, AQUACULTURE, AQUAPONICS, HYDROPONICS, SMALL RUMINANTS, BEEF CATTLE, ETC. OUR EXTENSION DRONE TEAM, IN COLLABORATION WITH OUR REMOTE SENSING RESEARCHERS, IS A POWERFUL RESOURCE FOR FARMERS AND NEXT-GENERATION ORGANIZATIONS THAT SEEK TO TRAIN YOUTH AND UTILIZE MODERN TECHNOLOGY-BASED AGRICULTURE TO MANAGE AGRONOMIC CROPS, CROP PEST AND DISEASE, AND LIVESTOCK HERDS. OUR EXTENSION EDUCATORS PROVIDE KNOWLEDGE AND SUPPORT IN TOPICS RELATED TO HUMAN NUTRITION AND HUMAN HEALTH TO COMMUNITIES ACROSS THE STATE THROUGH VARIOUS AVENUES INCLUDING OUR MOBILE HEALTH UNITS. WORKING IN COLLABORATION WITH HUMAN HEALTH AND NUTRITION RESEARCHERS, THESE EDUCATORS ARE TRAINING STAKEHOLDERS OF ALL AGES HOW TO INCORPORATE NUTRITIOUS AND TASTY FOODS INTO HEALTHY, BUDGET-FRIENDLY DIETS, WHILE ALSO MITIGATING CHRONIC ILLNESSES SUCH AS DIABETES AND HEART DISEASE THROUGH INCREASED PHYSICAL ACTIVITY. OUR 4-H AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT STAFF ARE TRAINING YOUTH IN STEM, AGRICULTURE, NATURAL RESOURCES, THE ENVIRONMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FUTURE CAREERS, AND MENTAL HEALTHCARE. THESE EDUCATORS ARE REACHING SCHOOLS, RELIGIOUS ORGANIZATIONS, 4-H CLUBS, AND OTHER PLACES WHERE THEY CAN ENGAGE YOUTH AND PROMOTE POSITIVE YOUTH DEVELOPMENT APPROACHES. THEY ARE ALSO FORMING STRONG PARTNERSHIPS WITH PARENTS, GUARDIANS, SCHOOLTEACHERS, COACHES, RELIGIOUS LEADERS, ETC., TO ENGAGE YOUTH THROUGH IN-SCHOOL ENRICHMENT, AFTER-SCHOOL PROGRAMS, AND A MULTIPLICITY OF SUMMER CAMPS. OUR COMMUNITY RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT STAFF COLLABORATE WITH LOCAL LEADERS AND ORGANIZATIONS TO STRENGTHEN COMMUNITIES THROUGH LEADERSHIP TRAINING, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, WORKFORCE READINESS, LAND RETENTION, AND DISASTER EDUCATION INITIATIVES THAT PROMOTE SUSTAINABLE GROWTH AND RESILIENCE. THEY ALSO BUILD PARTNERSHIPS THAT ENHANCE CIVIC ENGAGEMENT, INCREASE ACCESS TO RESOURCES, AND SUPPORT EQUITABLE ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITIES ACROSS KENTUCKY COMMUNITIES. ALL OF OUR EXTENSION PROGRAMS ARE FUNDAMENTALLY DRIVEN BY THE NEEDS OF OUR LAND-GRANT CLIENTS AND ARE DESIGNED IN RES PONSE TO THE FOLLOWING CRITICAL ISSUES IDENTIFIED IN OUR USDA PLAN OF WORK: 1) AGRICULTURE, ENVIRONMENT AND NATURAL RESOURCES; 2) ECONOMIC AND FINANCIAL WELL-BEING; 3) FOOD SAFETY AND SECURITY; 4) LEADERSHIP AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT; 5) LIFE SKILL DEVELOPMENT; 6) NUTRITION AND HEALTHY LIFESTYLES; 7) SMALL FARM DEVELOPMENT; AND, 8) SUBSTANCE USE RECOVERY AND MENTAL WELLBEING.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: THE EXTENSION AND OUTREACH COMPONENT OF TENNESSEE STATE UNIVERSITY'S LAND-GRANT MISSION IS CARRIED OUT THROUGH THE COOPERATIVE EXTENSION PROGRAM IN THE COLLEGE OF AGRICULTURE. THE PROGRAM'S MISSION AND PURPOSE ARE TO PROVIDE RESEARCH-BASED, PRACTICAL INFORMATION TO TENNESSEE RESIDENTS THROUGH INFORMAL EDUCATION ACTIVITIES AIMED AT ENHANCING THE STATE'S SOCIAL, ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL STRENGTHS. TSU EXTENSION PLACES A SPECIFIC EMPHASIS ON REACHING OUT TO ASSIST FARMERS AND PRODUCERS, WORKING COLLABORATIVELY WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF TENNESSEE, THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE, AND COUNTY GOVERNMENTS. TSU EXTENSION HAS A BROAD REACH, WITH COUNTY EXTENSION AGENTS/EDUCATORS, PROGRAM ASSISTANTS, AND SUPPORT STAFF IN 62 COUNTIES ACROSS THE STATE. THESE COUNTY PERSONNEL RECEIVE TRAINING AND EDUCATIONAL/TEACHING MATERIAL FROM TSU'S 23 CAMPUS-BASED EXTENSION FACULTY/SPECIALISTS. OUR PROGRAM IS ORGANIZED AROUND ADDRESSING CRITICAL ISSUES IN FOUR EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMMING AREAS: 1) AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES (ANR), WHICH ENHANCES URBAN AND RURAL PROSPERITY BY ASSISTING FARMERS AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES IN PRODUCING FIELD CROPS, RAISING LIVESTOCK, MANAGING FORESTLANDS, GROWING FRUIT CROPS, AND TENDING HOME GARDENS, ACTIVITIES THAT PRODUCE FOOD AND ENHANCE THE ENVIRONMENTAL INTEGRITY OF THE AGROECOSYSTEM. THE ANR PROGRAM PROVIDES FARMERS WITH A WIDE VARIETY OF RESEARCH-BASED TRAINING PROGRAMS TO ENHANCE THEIR PRODUCTIVITY, FARM PROFITABILITY, AND QUALITY OF LIFE, FOCUSING ON ENTOMOLOGY, FORESTRY, SOIL HEALTH, BIOENERGY, PLANT NUTRITION, HORTICULTURE, DRONE TECHNOLOGY,  ECONOMICS, AGRICULTURAL COMMUNICATIONS, AND ORGANIC AGRICULTURE; 2) FAMILY AND CONSUMER SCIENCES, WHICH IMPROVES THE HEALTH AND WELL-BEING OF TENNESSEANS BY PROVIDING PROGRAMS ON COMMUNITY NUTRITION EDUCATION, NUTRITION SECURITY, FAMILY AND CHILD DEVELOPMENT, AND HEALTH EDUCATION; 3) EXTENSION 4-H YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, WHICH FOCUSES ON WORKFORCE PREPARATION, STEM EDUCATION, LIFE SKILLS DEVELOPMENT, YOUTH LEADERSHIP, CITIZENSHIP DEVELOPMENT, AND COMMUNICATION SKILLS; AND 4) COMMUNITY RESOURCES AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT, WHICH PROVIDES INFORMATION AND EDUCATION ON COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT, AND LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT AND INCLUDES AGRIBUSINESS/ENTREPRENEURSHIP TRAINING FOR FARMERS (MANAGEMENT, BASIC ACCOUNTING, ECONOMIC PRINCIPLES, BUDGETS, WHOLE-FARM PLANNING, LAND ACQUISITION, TAX MANAGEMENT) AS WELL AS TRAINING FOR LOCAL BUSINESSES (BUSINESS PLANNING, ENTREPRENEURSHIP, MODERN MARKETING AND ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT).
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2025. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS MANY OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN THE STATE'S 1890 AND 1862 LAND-GRANT INSTITUTIONS, FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY (FVSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA (UGA), THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDES CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS PLANT AND ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES (I.E. AI-POWERED DRONES AND AUTONOMOUS TRACTORS IN AGRICULTURE). USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE MODEL INCLUDES A METHOD FOR ASSESSING NEEDS AND IDENTIFYING PROBLEMS. IT ALSO INCLUDES PROGRAM EVALUATION TO DETERMINE IMPACTS OF EXTENSION EDUCATIONAL TRAININGS. THE MODEL WORKS IN UNISON WITH MULTIPLE ADVISORY SYSTEMS AT BOTH COUNTY AND STATE LEVELS. GOALS OF THESE PROGRAMS ARE TO DEMONSTRATE SHORT AND LONG-TERM IMPACTS. STATE FUNDING SUCH AS STATE-MATCH, COUNTY FUNDING, GRANTS AND GIFTS LEVERAGED AS A DIRECT RESULT OF THE WORK IN THIS PLAN AIDS IN CREATING THE GREATEST FINAL IMPACTS. CRITICAL ISSUES ARE RELATED TO ANIMAL PRODUCTION; COMMUNITY, HOME AND LIFE SKILLS; FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY; PLANT PRODUCTION; SUSTAINABILITY, CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT; URBAN AGRICULTURE; AND YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT. USDA FACILITY FUNDING AFFORDED FVSU EXTENSION THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLETE ITS COLD VEGETABLE PROCESSING FACILITY. THIS FACILITY IS A COMPLIMENT TO FVSU RESEARCH MEAT PROCESSING FACILITY (ENGAGED IN EXPANSION) AS THEY ARE USED TO ADDRESS CONSUMER DEMAND FOR FOOD AND VALUE-ADDED MARKETING OPPORTUNITIES FOR FARMERS. ALSO, DURING THIS FISCAL YEAR, EFFORTS TO MAXIMIZE RETURN ON INVESTMENT VIA IMPACTS, THE 1890 EXTENSION REGION INSTITUTED A PROJECT TO GATHER PROGRAM DATA ACROSS THE 1890 REGION (ALL 1890 LAND-GRANT UNIVERSITIES) CALLED COMMON MEASURES. THE GEORGIA POW IS DESIGN TO MEET THE EMERGING ISSUES OF OUR COMMUNITIES, SUPPORT THE S USTAINABILITY AND PROFITABILITY OF THE AGRICULTURE INDUSTRY WHILE PROVIDING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS FOR FAMILIES AND YOUTH.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: IN 2024, SC STATE UNIVERSITY ADVANCED ITS MISSION OF TEACHING, RESEARCH, AND OUTREACH BY DELIVERING IMPACTFUL PROGRAMS ACROSS 32 SOUTH CAROLINA COUNTIES. THE UNIVERSITY FOCUSED ON FIVE CRITICAL ISSUES: AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, ENVIRONMENTAL AND NATURAL RESOURCES CONSERVATION, FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT, AND FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION. THESE INITIATIVES AIMED TO IMPROVE THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR CITIZENS THROUGH EDUCATION, INNOVATION, AND COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT. UNDER AGRIBUSINESS AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT, SC STATE ADDRESSED CHALLENGES SUCH AS UNEMPLOYMENT, POVERTY, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS. STAFF FACILITATED 20 WORKSHOPS, 7 IN-PERSON, 7 HYBRID, AND 6 VIRTUAL, REACHING 9,269 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,948 HOURS AND 37,727 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,402 HOURS. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS REPORTED KNOWLEDGE GAINS, AND SIX JOBS WERE CREATED THROUGH A RURAL BUSINESS ENTERPRISE GRANT. IN AGRISYSTEMS PRODUCTIVITY AND PROFITABILITY, THE SMALL FARMS TEAM CONDUCTED 58 WORKSHOPS AND WORKED WITH OVER 577 SMALL FARMERS. THEY LOGGED 3,969 DIRECT CONTACT HOURS WITH 16,269 INDIVIDUALS AND 1,516 INDIRECT HOURS WITH 9,678 CONTACTS. PROGRAMMING COVERED ANIMAL PRODUCTION, SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE, AND RISK MANAGEMENT. TOPICS INCLUDED BEEF CATTLE HERD HEA LTH, COLLABORATIVE MARKETING, AGRICULTURAL TAXES, AND NRCS SERVICES. NINETY PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS HAD ACRES IMPACTED BY INTEGRATED CROP MANAGEMENT, REFLECTING A 56% INCREASE FROM THE PREVIOUS YEAR. A TOTAL OF 117 FARMERS REPORTED PROFIT GAINS, AND 650 PRODUCERS ADOPTED RECOMMENDED PRACTICES. FAMILY AND YOUTH DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS INCLUDED 400 WORKSHOPS FOCUSED ON CIVIC ENGAGEMENT, HEALTHY LIFESTYLES, FINANCIAL LITERACY, S.T.R.E.A.M. (SCIENCE, TECHNOLOGY, READING, ENGINEERING, AGRICULTURE/ARTS, AND MATHEMATICS), AND THE AGRICULTURE INNOVATION SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM. STAFF MADE 43,896 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 4 ,360 HOURS AND REACHED 1,484,408 INDIVIDUALS INDIRECTLY OVER 2,004 HOURS. YOUTH PARTICIPATED IN HANDS-ON LEARNING SUCH AS CODING, DRONE EXPLORATION, AND ROBOTICS, FOSTERING LIFELONG SKILLS AND CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY. FOOD SAFETY, SECURITY, AND NUTRITION INITIATIVES WERE DELIVERED THROUGH 1,181 WORKSHOPS PROMOTING HEALTHY BEHAVIORS AND DISEASE PREVENTION. STAFF COLLABORATED WITH ADVISORY COUNCILS AND STAKEHOLDERS TO DESIGN EVIDENCE-BASED NUTRITION EDUCATION PROGRAMS. THEY MADE 22,720 DIRECT CONTACTS OVER 2,969 HOURS AND 332,595 INDIRECT CONTACTS OVER 1,289 HOURS. SIXTY-EIGHT PERCENT OF PARTICIPANTS GAINED NUTRITION KNOWLEDGE. EIGHTEEN MEMORANDUMS OF UNDERSTANDING WERE SIGNED WITH EXTERNAL AGENCIES. STAFF EARNED SERVSAFE AND UTAH STATE NUTRITION CERTIFICATIONS. THE EFNEP EXPLORERS CAMP GRADUATED 636 YOUTH, ALL OF WHOM REPORTED INTENTIONS TO DRINK MORE WATER AND EAT MORE FRUITS. SC STATE'S RESEARCH PORTFOLIO INCLUDED SIX PROJECTS: AI AND DATA ANALYSIS, PHYTOCHEMICALS FROM SWEET POTATOES, FOOD HUB NETWORKS, AIENABLED SMART FARMING IOT SYSTEMS, POLYCYCLIC AROMATIC HYDROCARBONS IN SOIL, AND ATMO SPHERIC WATER GENERATION FROM RECYCLED PLASTICS. THREE PROJECTS CONCLUDED IN JULY 2025 AND PREPARED BULLETINS FOR PUBLICATION; THREE REMAIN ACTIVE. THESE RESEARCH EFFORTS HAVE BROAD IMPLICATIONS FOR SUSTAINABILITY, INNOVATION, AND AGRICULTURAL ADVANCEMENT. COLLECTIVELY, SC STATE UNIVERSITY'S PROGRAMS AND RESEARCH INITIATIVES DEMONSTRATE A STRONG COMMITMENT TO COMMUNITY EMPOWERMENT, SCIENTIFIC PROGRESS, AND INCLUSIVE EDUCATION, SERVING AS A CATALYST FOR POSITIVE CHANGE ACROSS SOUTH CAROLINA.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: SINCE 1882, THE UNIVERSITY OF TENNESSEE AGRESEARCH (FORMERLY THE TENNESSEE AGRICULTURAL EXPERIMENT STATION) HAS SERVED AS THE BACKBONE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRI-FORESTRY ECONOMY THROUGH RESEARCH, INNOVATION, AND APPLICATION. AS THE STATE'S TOP INDUSTRY, AGRI-FORESTRY CONTRIBUTES $103 BILLION ANNUALLY TO TENNESSEE'S ECONOMY. WORKING IN TANDEM WITH UT EXTENSION, MORE THAN 850 AGRESEARCH FACULTY, STAFF, AND GRADUATE STUDENTS ADDRESS CHALLENGES FACING AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES THROUGH SOLUTION-ORIENTED RESEARCH AND DISSEMINATION OF KNOWLEDGE TO FARMERS, ALLIED INDUSTRIES, AND CONSUMERS. UT AGRESEARCH OPERATES TEN RESEARCH AND EDUCATION CENTERS (RECS) ACROSS TENNESSEE, COVERING DIVERSE GEOGRAPHIES, PRODUCTION SYSTEMS, CROPS, AND LIVESTOCK. THESE 40,000 ACRES OF FIELD LABORATORIES ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO CONDUCT TRIALS UNDER REAL WORLD CONDITIONS AND SERVE AS PLATFORMS FOR OUTREACH THROUGH ROUGHLY 20 FIELD DAYS EACH YEAR, ATTRACTING TENS OF THOUSANDS OF PARTICIPANTS FROM ACROSS THE STATE, REGION, AND BEYOND. THE RECS ALSO PROVIDE HANDS-ON LEARNING FOR COLLEGE STUDENTS AND YOUTH. AGRESEARCH ENCOMPASSES SEVEN ACADEMIC DEPARTMENTS AND ONE SCHOOL OF NATURAL RESOURCES, WITH ABOUT 150 FACULTY HOLDING RESEARCH APPOINTMENTS. GUIDED BY THE UT INSTITUTE OF AGRICULTURE'S A DECADE OF EXCELLENCE (2018-2028) AND GROWING GREATNESS (2025-2030) STRATEGIC PLANS, UT AGRESEARCH HAS ESTABLISHED THE AGRESEARCH STRATEGIC ACTION PLAN (ASAP) AS A ROADMAP TO MEET STATE AND NATIONAL NEEDS. ASAP FOCUSES ON BALANCING A PORTFOLIO OF INTEGRATED, INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAMS; CULTIVATING COLLABORATIVE TEAMS; ADVANCING INNOVATIVE CONCEPTS WITH STRUCTURED SUPPORT; AND SUSTAINING RESEARCH CAPACITY THROUGH TARGETED INVESTMENTS. FOUR THEMATIC INTEGRATED PROGRAMS ADDRESS COMPLEX, EMERGING CHALLENGES: UT ONE HEALTH INITIATIVE - A UT SYSTEM-WIDE PROGRAM HOUSED WITHIN AGRESEARCH THAT ADVANCES HUMAN, ANIMAL, PLANT, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH TO PROMOTE ECONOMIC GROWTH AND ECOSYSTEM RESILIENCE AT LOCAL, NATIONAL, AND GLOBAL LEVELS. PRECISION AND DIGITAL AGRICULTURE - A TRANSDISCIPLINARY EFFORT INCORPORATING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE, SENSING TECHNOLOGIES (E.G., DRONES AND ROBOTICS), AND DATA ANALYTICS TO ENHANCE PRODUCTION EFFICIENCY, CONSERVE FINITE NATURAL RESOURCES, REDUCE LABOR DEMANDS, AND IMPROVE PROFITABILITY. PROJECTS SPAN PLANT AND ANIMAL SYSTEMS, ENABLING PRODUCERS TO MAKE INFORMED, DATA DRIVEN DECISIONS. AGRICULTURAL GENOMICS AND SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY - A TRANSFORMATIONAL INITIATIVE ADVANCING PRODUCTIVITY AND RESILIENCE IN PLANTS AND ANIMALS THROUGH GENETIC INNOVATION. RESEARCH FOCUSES ON DISEASE AND DROUGHT RESISTANCE, WATER-USE EFFICIENCY, YIELD IMPROVEMENT, AND MITIGATION OF WILDLIFE DAMAGE, POSITIONING TENNESSEE AT THE FOREFRONT OF NEXT-GENERATION CROP AND LIVESTOCK DEVELOPMENT. BIOECONOMY ADVANCEMENT - AN INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAM CREATING SUSTAINABLE FUELS, CHEMICALS, AND MATERIALS FROM RENEWA BLE OR STRANDED BIOMASS RESOURCES. THE CIRCULAR BIOECONOMY SYSTEM (CBS), A CONVERGENT RESEARCH INITIATIVE BETWEEN UT/UTIA AND OAK RIDGE NATIONAL LABORATORY, DEVELOPS TECHNOLOGIES THAT CONVERT BIOMASS (E.G., MISCANTHUS GRASS) GROWN ON MARGINAL LANDS INTO MATERIALS FOR AUTOMOTIVE, PACKAGING, AND CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRIES-STIMULATING RURAL ECONOMIC GROWTH ACROSS TENNESSEE AND THE REGION. THROUGH THESE INTEGRATED INITIATIVES, UT AGRESEARCH ENHANCES THE SUSTAINABILITY, PROFITABILITY, AND RESILIENCE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRICULTURAL AND NATURAL RESOURCE SECTORS, ENSURING THAT RESEARCH-BASED INNOVATION CONTINUES TO DRIVE THE STATE'S ECONOMIC VITALITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP INTO THE FUTURE
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: THE GEORGIA PLAN OF WORK (POW) ENCOMPASSES A FIVE-YEAR PERIOD BEGINNING OCTOBER 1, 2025. THE PLAN ADDRESSES MAJOR AGRICULTURAL ISSUES AS WELL AS MANY OTHER PROBLEMS FACING RURAL AND URBAN AREAS, THE ENVIRONMENT, FAMILIES AND YOUTH. GEORGIA'S POW REPRESENTS A COORDINATED EFFORT BETWEEN THE STATE'S 1890 AND 1862 LAND-GRANT INSTITUTIONS, FORT VALLEY STATE UNIVERSITY (FVSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA (UGA), THUS PRESENTING ONE JOINT POW AND ANNUAL REPORT TO USDA. THE JOINT POW INCLUDES CORE PROGRAMS AND TARGETED ISSUES SUCH AS PLANT AND ANIMAL PRODUCTION, COMMUNITY/HOME AND LIFE SKILLS, FOOD SAFETY AND QUALITY, HEALTH AND WELLNESS, SUSTAINABILITY/CONSERVATION AND THE ENVIRONMENT, YOUTH AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT, URBAN AGRICULTURE, AND EMERGING ISSUES (I.E. AI-POWERED DRONES AND AUTONOMOUS TRACTORS IN AGRICULTURE). USDA FUNDS FOR THESE CORE PROGRAMS SUPPORT STATE-LEVEL CORE AND TARGETED ISSUES FACING GEORGIA AND AMERICAN CITIZENS. THE GEORGIA PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT IS A MULTISTEP PROCESS THAT IS OPERATIONAL ANNUALLY. THE RESEARCH PORTION OF THE PLAN OF WORK UNDERGOES SCIENTIFIC PEER REVIEW PRIOR TO EACH PROJECT BEING SUBMITTED. ALL SCIENTISTS ARE REQUIRED TO HAVE ACTIVE PROJECTS FOR EXPENDITURES TO BE MADE. EACH PROJECT IS PEER-REVIEWED BY BOTH INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL REVIEWERS AND MUST BE APPROVED BY THE APPROP RIATE DEAN AND DIRECTOR PRIOR TO SUBMISSION TO THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF FOOD AND AGRICULTURE. CURRENT ANIMAL RELATED PROJECTS INVOLVE EFFICIENT ASSISTED REPRODUCTIVE TECHNOLOGY AND NUTRITION, ANIMAL HEALTH AND WELLBEING WITH INTERNAL PARASITE CONTROL USING SHEEP, GOATS, AND CATTLE AS MODELS. A NEW MULTISTATE EVANS-ALLEN PROJECT WAS INTRODUCED THIS YEAR ALONG WITH OTHER 1890 INSTITUTIONS UNDER THE TITLE ECOLOGICAL RESILIENCE AND EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS: IMPACTS ON FARMERS, LANDOWNERS AND COMMUNITIES. THE FOOD SAFETY PROGRAM EMPHASIZES NON-THERMAL METHODS TO CONTROL FOOD BORNE PATHOGENS IN MEAT AND NUTS. PLANT PRODUCTION PROGRAMS ENCOMPASS A BROAD ARRAY OF TARGETED FIELDS OF STUDY INCLUDING ENTOMOLOGY, PLANT PATHOLOGY, NEMATOLOGY, PLANT BREEDING AND GENETICS, SOIL SCIENCE, AND POSTHARVEST PHYSIOLOGY. SOME OF THESE PROJECTS ARE DONE IN COLLABORATION WITH ARS SCIENTISTS ON COMMODITY OF GREAT IMPORTANCE TO THE STATE OF GEORGIA. FUNDING FROM THE FEDERAL ALLOCATION AND THE CORRESPONDING MATCHING FUND FROM THE STATE ARE USED TO COVER EXPENSES RELATED TO THESE ACTIVITIES. ADDITIONAL FUNDS COME FROM EXTERNAL GRANTS SECURED BY THE RESEARCH SCIENTISTS. APPLIED RESEARCH PROJECTS USING THESE FUNDS TARGET MAINLY LIMITED-RESOURCE PRODUCERS IN THE STATE BUT SCIENTIFIC DISCOVERIES IMPACT THE CITIZEN OF STATE OF GEORGIA, THE COUNTRY, AND THE WORLD.
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: DRONE RACING TO EDUCATE AND ADVANCE MATH - DREAM -THIS PROJECT AIMS TO ENGAGE REGIONAL COMMUNITIES IN TECHNICAL EDUCATION AND THE WORKFORCE BY CONTEXTUALIZING MATH USING DRONES TO INCREASE MATH ABILITIES AND STEM PERFORMANCE, AND ADDRESS GAPS IN THE UNCREWED AUTONOMOUS VEHICLE (UAS) AND MATHEMATICS CURRICULUM. THE PROJECT AIMS TO ASSESS THE EFFICACY OF IMPLEMENTING A MATH AND DRONE EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING ENVIRONMENT THAT INCLUDES DRONE RACING, MATH AND DRONE BOOT CAMPS AND THE DEVELOPMENT OF CURRICULUM AND EDUCATIONAL MATERIALS TO BUILD STEM ABILITIES. THE OVERALL GOAL IS TO IMPROVE STUDENT LEARNING WITH INNOVATIVE PROGRAMMING AND TRAINING COMPONENTS. IT AIMS TO INCREASE CAPACITY AT THE INSTITUTION BY CREATING A LEADERSHIP MODEL AROUND MATH AND DRONE USAGE AND TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER/REPLICATION FOR FACULTY, TEACHERS, AND STUDENTS. THE INITIATIVE IS BASED ON A WORKFORCE NEEDS ASSESSMENT MODEL AND HAS THE POTENTIAL TO ASSIST WITH TRANSFORMING THE SOCIOECONOMIC FOUNDATION OF THE SURROUNDING COMMUNITIES. THE PROJECT INTENDS TO ADDRESS RECRUITMENT AND RETENTION NEEDS FOR A NEW UAS CERTIFICATE TO BUILD UAS AS AN EMERGING CAREER PATHWAY TO STEM DEGREES, MEET WORKFORCE DEMANDS, AND TO INCREASE STUDENT CONFIDENCE, PREPAREDNESS, AND PERFORMANCE IN MATH. IT WILL INTEGRATE REAL-WORLD APPLICATIONS, SUCH AS DRONE TECHNOLOGY, INTO THE CURRICULUM. THIS INITIATIVE PLANS TO DEVELOP MATH-AND-DRONE INTEGRATED CURRICULUM TARGETING BOTH UNDERGRADUATE AND SECONDARY SCHOOL STUDENTS THROUGH THREE MAIN GOALS: (1) INCREASE MATH ABILITIES AND PREPAREDNESS THROUGH ENHANCED CURRICULAR DESIGN, INCLUDING COLLABORATIVE RESEARCH TEAMS AND INNOVATIVE INSTRUCTIONAL MODULES FOR COLLEGE STUDENTS; (2) IMPLEMENT A MATHEMATICS AND DRONE FLIGHT BOOTCAMP TO IMPROVE MATH CONFIDENCE AND ACADEMIC OUTCOMES FOR HIGH SCHOOL STUDENTS; AND (3) ESTABLISH A DRONE RACING PROGRAM TO RECRUIT AND TRAIN STUDENTS IN ADVANCED TECHNOLOGICAL EDUCATION. RESEARCH ACTIVITIES INCLUDE PRE- AND POST-ASSESSMENTS OF MATH ATTITUDES, STEM ABILITIES, AND ACADEMIC PERFORMANCE. THE PROGRAM ALSO INTENDS TO MEASURE OUTCOMES THROUGH GRADES, COURSE EVALUATIONS, STUDENT JOURNALS, AND ENGAGEMENT METRICS. THIS PROJECT IS FUNDED BY THE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGICAL EDUCATION PROGRAM THAT FOCUSES ON THE EDUCATION OF TECHNICIANS FOR THE ADVANCED-TECHNOLOGY FIELDS THAT DRIVE THE NATION'S ECONOMY. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.
solicitations.recipient: THE COLLEGE OF WILLIAM AND MARY IN VIRGINIA (THE UNIVERSITY)

ASAP FOCUSES ON BALANCING A PORTFOLIO OF INTEGRATED, INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAMS; CULTIVATING COLLABORATIVE TEAMS; ADVANCING INNOVATIVE CONCEPTS WITH STRUCTURED SUPPORT; AND SUSTAINING RESEARCH CAPACITY THROUGH TARGETED INVESTMENTS. FOUR THEMATIC INTEGRATED PROGRAMS ADDRESS COMPLEX, EMERGING CHALLENGES: UT ONE HEALTH INITIATIVE - A UT SYSTEM-WIDE PROGRAM HOUSED WITHIN AGRESEARCH THAT ADVANCES HUMAN, ANIMAL, PLANT, AND ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH TO PROMOTE ECONOMIC GROWTH AND ECOSYSTEM RESILIENCE AT LOCAL, NATIONAL, AND GLOBAL LEVELS. PRECISION AND DIGITAL AGRICULTURE - A TRANSDISCIPLINARY EFFORT INCORPORATING ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE, SENSING TECHNOLOGIES (E.G., DRONES AND ROBOTICS), AND DATA ANALYTICS TO ENHANCE PRODUCTION EFFICIENCY, CONSERVE FINITE NATURAL RESOURCES, REDUCE LABOR DEMANDS, AND IMPROVE PROFITABILITY. PROJECTS SPAN PLANT AND ANIMAL SYSTEMS, ENABLING PRODUCERS TO MAKE INFORMED, DATA DRIVEN DECISIONS. AGRICULTURAL GENOMICS AND SYNTHETIC BIOLOGY - A TRANSFORMATIONAL INITIATIVE ADVANCING PRODUCTIVITY AND RESILIENCE IN PLANTS AND ANIMALS THROUGH GENETIC INNOVATION. RESEARCH FOCUSES ON DISEASE AND DROUGHT RESISTANCE, WATER-USE EFFICIENCY, YIELD IMPROVEMENT, AND MITIGATION OF WILDLIFE DAMAGE, POSITIONING TENNESSEE AT THE FOREFRONT OF NEXT-GENERATION CROP AND LIVESTOCK DEVELOPMENT. BIOECONOMY ADVANCEMENT - AN INTERDISCIPLINARY PROGRAM CREATING SUSTAINABLE FUELS, CHEMICALS, AND MATERIALS FROM RENEWA BLE OR STRANDED BIOMASS RESOURCES. THE CIRCULAR BIOECONOMY SYSTEM (CBS), A CONVERGENT RESEARCH INITIATIVE BETWEEN UT/UTIA AND OAK RIDGE NATIONAL LABORATORY, DEVELOPS TECHNOLOGIES THAT CONVERT BIOMASS (E.G., MISCANTHUS GRASS) GROWN ON MARGINAL LANDS INTO MATERIALS FOR AUTOMOTIVE, PACKAGING, AND CONSTRUCTION INDUSTRIES-STIMULATING RURAL ECONOMIC GROWTH ACROSS TENNESSEE AND THE REGION. THROUGH THESE INTEGRATED INITIATIVES, UT AGRESEARCH ENHANCES THE SUSTAINABILITY, PROFITABILITY, AND RESILIENCE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRICULTURAL AND NATURAL RESOURCE SECTORS, ENSURING THAT RESEARCH-BASED INNOVATION CONTINUES TO DRIVE THE STATE'S ECONOMIC VITALITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP INTO THE FUTURE
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO SCOUT AND MONITOR PREVIOUSLY ESTABLISHED GROVES FOR GROWTH AND PRODUCTIVITY AS ASIAN CITRUS PSYLLID, THE VECTOR OF CITRUS GREENING, CONTINUES TO AFFECT THE CALIFORNIA CITRUS INDUSTRY. THE RECIPIENT WILL SCOUT CURRENT SITES AND UNDERTAKE PSYLLID DATA COLLECTION, WHICH GROWERS USE TO INFLUENCE THEIR MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES. DELIVERABLES INCLUDE DATA CONTRIBUTIONS, MONTHLY GROVE REPORTS TO GROWERS, AND A FINAL REPORT TO APHIS. SUBCONTRACTS TO IMPERIAL COUNTY CITRUS PEST CONTROL DISTRICT AND AEROBOTICS WILL BE EXECUTED FOR DETECTOR DOG SURVEYS AND DRONE-FACILITATED MEASUREMENTS OF GROVE HEALTH, RESPECTIVELY. CALIFORNIA CITRUS GROWERS AND THE BROADER INDUSTRY ARE THE PRIMARY BENEFICIARIES OF THESE RESULTS, BUT STAKEHOLDERS AND RESEARCHERS FROM OTHER CITRUS PRODUCING STATES WILL ALSO BENEFIT FROM KNOWLEDGE SHARED THROUGH HLB MAC INFORMATION CHANNELS.
solicitations.sub_agency: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
solicitations.recipient: STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FOOD & AGRICULTURE

solicitations.title: EXPERIMENT STATION) HAS SERVED AS THE BACKBONE OF TENNESSEE'S AGRI-FORESTRY ECONOMY THROUGH RESEARCH, INNOVATION, AND APPLICATION. AS THE STATE'S TOP INDUSTRY, AGRI-FORESTRY CONTRIBUTES $103 BILLION ANNUALLY TO TENNESSEE'S ECONOMY. WORKING IN TANDEM WITH UT EXTENSION, MORE THAN 850 AGRESEARCH FACULTY, STAFF, AND GRADUATE STUDENTS ADDRESS CHALLENGES FACING AGRICULTURE AND NATURAL RESOURCES THROUGH SOLUTION-ORIENTED RESEARCH AND DISSEMINATION OF KNOWLEDGE TO FARMERS, ALLIED INDUSTRIES, AND CONSUMERS. UT AGRESEARCH OPERATES TEN RESEARCH AND EDUCATION CENTERS (RECS) ACROSS TENNESSEE, COVERING DIVERSE GEOGRAPHIES, PRODUCTION SYSTEMS, CROPS, AND LIVESTOCK. THESE 40,000 ACRES OF FIELD LABORATORIES ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO CONDUCT TRIALS UNDER REAL WORLD CONDITIONS AND SERVE AS PLATFORMS FOR OUTREACH THROUGH ROUGHLY 20 FIELD DAYS EACH YEAR, ATTRACTING TENS OF THOUSANDS OF PARTICIPANTS FROM ACROSS THE STATE, REGION, AND BEYOND. THE RECS ALSO PROVIDE HANDS-ON LEARNING FOR COLLEGE STUDENTS AND YOUTH. solicitations.title: THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO SUPPORT RESEARCH THAT ADDRESSES FOREST HEALTH AND MANAGEMENT AND ALIGNS WITH THE MANDATED PRIORITIES FOR THE MCINTIRE-STENNIS PROGRAM. FOR FY26, THE AWARD WILL SUPPORT PROJECTS INCLUDING: (A) NATURAL RESOURCE ECONOMICS RESEARCH TO BUILD ECONOMIC MODELS FOR MANAGEMENT AND DECISION MAKING ADDRESSING LABOR ISSUES; (B) FIELD RESEARCH ON HOW ELK REINTRODUCTION IN EASTERN KENTUCKY HAS IMPACTED THE ECOLOGY OF FORESTS IN THE REGION AND THE REFORESTATION OF RECLAIMED COAL SURFACE MINES; (C) FOREST ENTOMOLOGY RESEARCH DEVELOPING NEW MOLECULAR METHODS FOR CONTROLLING BARK BEETLES; (D) RESEARCH USING LONG-TERM ENVIRONMENTAL DATASETS FROM ROBINSON FOREST TO BETTER UNDERSTAND HOW WATER QUALITY AND QUANTITY ARE INFLUENCED BY MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, RESOURCE EXTRACTION, SEVERE WEATHER EVENTS, ETC.; (E) INVESTIGATIONS OF SILVICULTURAL PRACTICES AND GENETIC FACTORS THAT CAN CONTRIBUTE TO OAK GROWTH AND SUSTAINABILITY; (F) RESEARCH EVALUATING HOW WILDLIFE RESPONDS TO SURFACE MINE REFORESTATION EFFORTS IN CENTRAL APPALACHIA; (G) USING DRONES AND REMOTE SENSING TOOLS TO EVALUATE INVASIVE PLANTS IN DISTURBED LANDSCAPES; AND (H) UNDERSTANDING HOW CURRENT FOREST POPULATION DEMOGRAPHICS IMPACT SUSTAINABLE PRODUCTIVITY AND HARVESTING. DELIVERABLES FROM THE RESEARCH PROGRAMS SUPPORTED BY MCINTIRE-STENNIS FUNDING INCLUDE NEW DATA, SCIENTIFIC PUBLICATIONS AND PRESENTATIONS, AND GRADUATE STUDENTS TRAINED THROUGH PARTICIPATION IN THE PROJECT. TRANSLATION OF RESEARCH RESULTS WILL BE DELIVERED TO STAKEHOLDERS IN PART THROUGH THE CREATION OF DECISION MAKING TOOLS, RECOMMENDATIONS FOR BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, AND EDUCATION PROGRAMMING DEVELOPED IN COLLABORATION WITH EXTENSION. EXPECTED OUTCOMES FROM THIS WORK INCLUDE BETTER INFORMED TIMBER MANAGEMENT AND POLICY- DECISION-MAKING, IMPROVED CONSERVATION OF WATER RESOURCES AND ECOSYSTEM SERVICES IN CENTRAL APPALACHIA, NEW CONTROL METHODS FOR INVASIVE FOREST PESTS, AND IMPROVED MANAGEMENT PROCESSES IN SUPPORT OF THE WHITE OAK INDUSTRY. BENEFICIARIES WILL INCLUDE OTHER FORESTRY AND NATURAL RESOURCE SCIENTISTS, TIMBER RESOURCE MANAGERS, WILDLIFE MANAGERS, WOODLAND OWNERS, THE FOREST PRODUCTS INDUSTRIES, COMMUNITIES IN CENTRAL APPALACHIA, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC
solicitations.sub_agency: National Institute of Food and Agriculture

solicitations.title: COLLABORATIVE RESEARCH: FUSE: INDIUM SELENIDES BASED BACK END OF LINE NEUROMORPHIC ACCELERATORS -THIS PROJECT AIMS TO USE INNOVATIVE MATERIALS CALLED ?2D MATERIALS? TO ENHANCE THE CAPABILITIES OF MODERN INTEGRATED CIRCUITS. THESE MATERIALS HAVE UNIQUE ELECTRONIC PROPERTIES THAT MAKE THEM VERY PROMISING FOR COMPUTE, STORAGE, AND SENSING TECHNOLOGIES. HOWEVER, INTEGRATING THEM WITH EXISTING SILICON-BASED TECHNOLOGY HAS BEEN A CHALLENGE DUE TO TEMPERATURE RESTRICTIONS. LUCKILY, A NEW GROUP OF MATERIALS CALLED ?INDIUM-BASED CHALCOGENIDES? OFFERS A SOLUTION, AS THEY CAN BE SYNTHESIZED AT LOW TEMPERATURES COMPATIBLE WITH CURRENT TECHNOLOGY. THE PROJECT TEAM PLANS TO CREATE A RANGE OF DEVICES USING THESE MATERIALS TO ACCELERATE THE PERFORMANCE OF ENERGY-EFFICIENT SPIKING NEURAL NETWORKS (SNNS). THESE BRAIN-INSPIRED MICROCHIPS WILL REVOLUTIONIZE HOW AUDIO, VISUAL, TACTILE, AND OLFACTORY INFORMATION IS PROCESSED, MAKING DEVICES SMARTER AND MORE RESPONSIVE. MOREOVER, THESE MICROCHIPS COULD BE USED IN AUTONOMOUS VEHICLES, DRONES, AND ROBOTS, HELPING THEM NAVIGATE AND AVOID OBSTACLES. THE PROJECT ALSO FOCUSES ON TRAINING THE NEXT GENERATION OF SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS AND PROMOTING DIVERSITY AND INCLUSIVITY IN THE FIELD. THIS PROJECT AIMS TO ADDRESS THE CHALLENGE OF INTEGRATING NOVEL 2D MATERIALS WITH THE STATE-OF-THE-ART SILICON-BASED COMPLEMENTARY METAL OXIDE SEMICONDUCTOR (CMOS) TECHNOLOGY AT THE BACK END OF LINE (BEOL). THE KEY INNOVATION LIES IN LEVERAGING INDIUM-BASED CHALCOGENIDES, SUCH AS INSE AND IN2SE3, WHICH CAN BE SYNTHESIZED AT LOW TEMPERATURES, MAKING THEM COMPATIBLE WITH BEOL PROCESSES. THE TEAM PLANS TO SYNTHESIZE AND CHARACTERIZE THESE MATERIALS TO FABRICATE AN ARRAY OF SENSING, ENCODING, COMPUTING, AND MEMORY DEVICES FOR HARDWARE ACCELERATION OF ENERGY-EFFICIENT SPIKING NEURAL NETWORKS (SNNS). THE PROJECT WILL INVOLVE A CROSS-LAYER CO-OPTIMIZATION APPROACH THAT ENCOMPASSES MATERIAL DISCOVERY, SYNTHESIS AND DEPOSITION TECHNIQUES, PROCESS FLOW DEVELOPMENT, AND DEVICE-CIRCUIT-ARCHITECTURE CO-DESIGN. THE GOAL IS TO DEVELOP BRAIN-INSPIRED SNN MICROCHIPS THROUGH 2D/CMOS HETEROGENEOUS AND MONOLITHIC INTEGRATION, WHICH WILL LEAD TO SUBSTANTIAL REDUCTIONS IN ENERGY CONSUMPTION AND PAVE THE WAY FOR SUSTAINABLE COMPUTING PARADIGMS. THE BROADER IMPACT OF THIS WORK EXTENDS TO APPLICATIONS ON THE INTERNET OF THINGS (IOT) DOMAIN, WHERE THE BRAIN-MIMETIC SNN MICROCHIPS WILL ENABLE ADVANCED AUDIO, VISUAL, TACTILE, AND OLFACTORY INFORMATION PROCESSING. ADDITIONALLY, THE PROJECT EMPHASIZES EDUCATION AND TRAINING, PROMOTING DIVERSITY AND INCLUSIVENESS IN THE WORKFORCE. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

solicitations.title: FEDERAL AGENCY NAME: DEFENSE ADVANCED RESEARCH PROJECTS AGENCY (DARPA), DEFENSE SCIENCES OFFICE (DSO) FUNDING OPPORTUNITY TITLE: YOUNG FACULTY AWARD (YFA) FUNDING OPPORTUNITY NUMBER: DARPARA2501CATALOG OF FEDERAL DOMESTIC ASSISTANCE (CFDA) NUMBER(S): 12.910 RESEARCH AND TECHNOLOGY DEVELOPMENTPROGRAM DESCRIPTION: DARPA IS SOLICITING INNOVATIVE RESEARCH PROPOSALS IN THE AREAS OF INTEREST TO THE FOLLOWING DARPA TECHNICAL OFFICES: DEFENSE SCIENCES OFFICE (DSO), INFORMATION INNOVATION OFFICE (I2O), MICROSYSTEMS TECHNOLOGY OFFICE (MTO), AND THE TACTICAL TECHNOLOGY OFFICE (TTO). FURTHER DETAIL REGARDING THE SPECIFIC TECHNICAL AREAS OF INTEREST CAN BE FOUND BELOW UNDER OPEN OFFICE TOPICS. PROPOSED RESEARCH SHOULD INVESTIGATE INNOVATIVE APPROACHES THAT ENABLE REVOLUTIONARY ADVANCES IN SCIENCE, DEVICES, OR SYSTEMS. SPECIFICALLY EXCLUDED IS RESEARCH THAT PRIMARILY RESULTS IN EVOLUTIONARY IMPROVEMENTS TO THE EXISTING STATE OF PRACTICE.ULTRALOW SIZE, WEIGHT, POWER, AND COST (SWAP-C) ACCELEROMETERS WITH HIGH SENSITIVITY ANDSTABILITY ARE ESSENTIAL COMPONENTS FOR NAVIGATION AND MOTION SENSING USED IN DRONES, UNMANNEDAERIAL VEHICLES (UAVS), AND SATELLITES, ESPECIALLY AS AN ALTERNATIVE POSITIONING, NAVIGATION, ANDTIMING (PNT) TECHNOLOGY FOR SITUATIONS WHERE GPS SIGNALS ARE JAMMED OR UNAVAILABLE. HOWEVER,THE STANDARD QUANTUM LIMIT (I.E. SHOT NOISE) LIMITS THE SENSITIVITY THAT CAN BE ATTAINED EVEN AT THETHERMOMECHANICAL LIMIT. CURRENT STATE OF THE ART, COMPACT ACCELEROMETERS HAVE A SENSITIVITY  30NG HZ1 2 (1NG   9.8*10-9M S2) OVER A  7KHZ BANDWIDTH THAT IS LIMITED BY THE SHOT NOISE OF THESYSTEM. ONE CAN IMPROVE THE SENSITIVITY BY USING SQUEEZED LIGHT TO READ OUT THE MECHANICALMOTION  HOWEVER, OPTICAL SQUEEZING IS VERY SENSITIVE TO SYSTEM LOSS AND THE HIGHEST DEMONSTRATEDSQUEEZING IS ONLY 15DB (LAB DEMONSTRATION, NOT PRACTICAL). QUANTUM ACCELEROMETERS BASED ONATOMIC GASES ARE CHALLENGING TO MINIATURIZE AND ARE NOT MUCH BETTER THAN THE CLASSICAL OPTOMECHANICAL ONES.THE VISION OF THE PROPOSED WORK IS TO ENHANCE THE SENSITIVITY OF OPTOMECHANICAL ACCELEROMETERS BY 30DB USING WEAK VALUE AMPLIFICATION (WVA) AND ENABLE UNPRECEDENTED LEVELS OF INERTIAL NAVIGATION. IF THIS PROJECT IS SUCCESSFUL, COMPACT ACCELEROMETERS WITH SENSITIVITY OF 10PG HZ1 2 (1PG   9.8*10-12M S2) WILL BECOME A REALITY.THIS WILL ENABLE LONGER MISSIONS IN GPS DENIED ENVIRONMENTS, UNDERWATER, INDOORS, AND BELOW GROUND. THIS WORK WILL REDUCE THE UNCERTAINTY IN THE LOCATION OF A MISSION, I.E. THE CIRCULAR ERROR PROBABLE, AND WILL ENABLE LONGER DURATION MISSIONS IN SLOWER PLATFORMS. THESE RESULTS WILL ENABLE PRECISION SPACE NAVIGATION IN SMALL SPACECRAFT, IMPROVED SEISMOMETRY, AND INERTIAL NAVIGATION OF COMPACT VEHICLES.

ADVANCING STATE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE NATIONAL STRATEGY TO SUPPORT FAMILY CAREGIVERS - MONTANA FACES SUBSTANTIAL CHALLENGES SUPPORTING ITS 112,000 FAMILY CAREGIVERS, WHO PROVIDE 105 MILLION HOURS OF UNPAID CARE ANNUALLY, VALUED AT $1.76 BILLION (AARP, 2023). GEOGRAPHIC ISOLATION ACROSS THE FOURTH LARGEST STATE (U.S. CENSUS BUREAU, 2025), WHERE 83% OF COUNTIES ARE CONSIDERED FRONTIER (MONTANA OFFICE OF RURAL HEALTH, 2025), EXACERBATES CAREGIVER BURDEN, LEADING TO POOR WELL-BEING, FRAGMENTED SERVICES, AND DELAYED SUPPORT FOR RURAL, NATIVE AMERICAN, VETERAN, AND ALZHEIMER’S DISEASE AND RELATED DEMENTIAS CAREGIVERS. DESPITE SUCCESSFUL PAST PROGRAMS BY THE MONTANA STATE UNIT ON AGING (SUA), EFFECTIVE STATEWIDE SCALABILITY AND INTEGRATED SUPPORT REMAIN CRITICAL GAPS. THROUGH TARGETED INTERVENTIONS LIKE EXPANDED RESPITE RETREATS, LEGAL DOCUMENT CLINICS, A STREAMLINED VOUCHER PROGRAM, THE CAREGIVER RESPITE FOOD PROGRAM, AND TAILORED CAREGIVER ASSESSMENT AND REFERRAL® (TCARE®) INTEGRATION WILL EMPOWER CAREGIVERS WITH HOLISTIC, PERSON-CENTERED CARE PLANNING, ULTIMATELY STRENGTHENING MONTANA'S CARE INFRASTRUCTURE. THROUGH THE FOLLOWING OBJECTIVES. 1.	 RAISE PUBLIC AWARENESS OF CAREGIVER ROLES, CHALLENGES, AND AVAILABLE RESOURCES BY LEVERAGING TRUSTED COMMUNITY VOICES AND TARGETED MEDIA OUTREACH.  (ALIGNS WITH GOAL 1: INCREASE AWARENESS OF AND OUTREACH TO FAMILY CAREGIVERS) 2.	EXPAND EQUITABLE ACCESS TO CAREGIVER SUPPORT SERVICES BY ENHANCING EXISTING PROGRAMS —INCLUDING RESPITE CARE, LEGAL DOCUMENT ASSISTANCE, RESPITE VOUCHERS, RESPITE RETREATS, AND CAREGIVER RESPITE FOOD PROGRAM— AND DELIVERING THEM ACROSS MONTANA’S RURAL AND UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES (ALIGNS WITH GOAL 3: STRENGTHEN SERVICES AND SUPPORTS) 3. UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA FAIRBANKS/SIKULIAQ OCEANOGRAPHIC TECHNICAL SERVICES - YEAR 1 OF 5 -THE UNIVERSITY OF ALASKA, FAIRBANKS (UAF) PROPOSES TO SUPPORT OCEANOGRAPHIC TECHNICAL SERVICES ON R/V SIKULIAQ OPERATED AS PART OF THE U.S. ACADEMIC RESEARCH FLEET (ARF), WHICH IS SCHEDULED BY THE UNIVERSITY-NATIONAL OCEANOGRAPHIC LABORATORY SYSTEM (UNOLS). AS PART OF THEIR BASIC OPERATIONS, UAF WILL PROVIDE SHIPBOARD TECHNICIANS ON EACH SEAGOING RESEARCH PROJECT TO SUPPORT BASIC SERVICES. TECHNICIANS WILL MAINTAIN, CALIBRATE AND PROVIDE FOR QUALIFIED USERS, ITEMS FROM THEIR POOL OF SHARED-USE RESEARCH INSTRUMENTATION. RESEARCH VESSELS IN THE ARF PROVIDE SUPPORT FOR RESEARCHERS FROM A VARIETY OF FEDERAL AND STATE AGENCIES, AS WELL AS SOME PRIVATE SPONSORS. ALL USERS (OR THE APPROPRIATE FUNDING AGENCIES) SHARE SUPPORT COSTS FOR BASIC TECHNICAL SERVICES ON THE VESSEL EQUALLY, VIA A DAY-RATE, WITH EACH PAYING A SHARE OF THE COSTS BASED ON FRACTIONAL USAGE OF THE VESSEL. THE PRINCIPAL IMPACT OF THE PRESENT PROPOSAL IS UNDER MERIT REVIEW CRITERION 2 OF THE PROPOSAL GUIDELINES (NSF 23-525). IT PROVIDES INFRASTRUCTURE SUPPORT FOR SCIENTISTS TO USE THE VESSEL AND ITS SHARED-USE INSTRUMENTATION IN SUPPORT OF THEIR NSF-FUNDED OCEANOGRAPHIC RESEARCH PROJECTS (WHICH INDIVIDUALLY UNDERGO SEPARATE REVIEW BY THE RELEVANT RESEARCH PROGRAM OF NSF). THE ACQUISITION, MAINTENANCE AND OPERATION OF SHARED-USE INSTRUMENTATION ALLOWS NSF-FUNDED RESEARCHERS FROM ANY US UNIVERSITY OR LAB ACCESS TO WORKING, CALIBRATED INSTRUMENTS FOR THEIR RESEARCH, REDUCING THE COST OF THAT RESEARCH, AND EXPANDING THE BASE OF POTENTIAL RESEARCHERS. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

DETERMINING THE ROLE OF REPLICATION PROTEIN A IN POLYMERASE THETA-MEDIATED END-JOINING - PROJECT SUMMARY MANY FRONTLINE CANCER TREATMENTS, INCLUDING RADIATION THERAPY AND SEVERAL CHEMOTHERAPEUTICS, WORK BY INDUCING DOUBLE-STRAND BREAKS (DSBS) SINCE CANCER CELLS ARE TYPICALLY MORE VULNERABLE TO DSBS THAN HEALTHY CELLS. THUS, UNDERSTANDING THE COMPLEX PATHWAYS CANCER CELLS USE TO REPAIR DSBS IS CRITICAL TO UNDERSTANDING AND ADDRESSING RESISTANCE TO THESE THERAPEUTIC AGENTS, WHICH ALMOST INVARIABLY ARISES. DEFICIENCIES IN THE HIGH FIDELITY DSB REPAIR PATHWAY HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION PROMOTE TUMORIGENESIS. THE RESULTING CANCERS ARE DEPENDENT ON A MUTAGENIC DSB REPAIR PATHWAY CALLED POLYMERASE THETA-MEDIATED END-JOINING (TMEJ). POLYMERASE THETA (POLΘ) IS A MULTIFUNCTIONAL ENZYME CONSISTING OF AN N-TERMINAL HELICASE DOMAIN, DISORDERED LINKER DOMAIN AND C-TERMINAL POLYMERASE DOMAIN. POLΘ IS ESSENTIAL FOR TMEJ, BUT THE OTHER FACTORS INVOLVED IN THIS PATHWAY REMAIN POORLY DEFINED. WHILE GENETIC SCREENS HAVE REVEALED SEVERAL NON-ESSENTIAL PROTEINS THAT MAY PLAY A ROLE IN TMEJ, THESE SCREENS ARE INCAPABLE OF IDENTIFYING ESSENTIAL FACTORS THAT INTERACT WITH POLΘ TO PROMOTE TMEJ. IN THIS PROPOSAL, I WILL DETERMINE THE ROLE OF THE ESSENTIAL, EUKARYOTIC SINGLE STRAND DNA-BINDING PROTEIN, REPLICATION PROTEIN A (RPA), IN TMEJ. BASED ON PARTIAL KNOCKDOWN IN CELLS, RPA WAS INITIALLY THOUGHT TO INHIBIT TMEJ.1 HOWEVER, WHEN I DEPLETED RPA FROM A CELL-FREE TMEJ ASSAY IN XENOPUS EGG EXTRACTS, END- JOINING WAS ABOLISHED. TMEJ WAS RESCUED WITH RECOMBINANT RPA, DEMONSTRATING THAT RPA IS ESSENTIAL FOR TMEJ. THE DEEP LEARNING TOOL ALPHA-FOLD MULTIMER PREDICTS THAT RPA AND POLΘ INTERACT VIA TWO INTERFACES — ONE ON RPA1 AND ONE ON RPA2. WHEN I MUTATED THESE INTERFACES ON RPA, REPLICATION WAS UNAFFECTED BUT TMEJ WAS INHIBITED. MUTATING THE CORRESPONDING RESIDUES ON POLΘ ALSO ABOLISHED TMEJ. I HYPOTHESIZE THAT THESE INTERFACES ARE NECESSARY FOR POLΘ RECRUITMENT AND ANNEALING OF MICROHOMOLOGOUS REGIONS. IN AIM 1, I WILL TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT RPA1 BINDS AND RECRUITS POLΘ AND THAT THIS ALLOWS POLΘ TO BRIDGE TWO NON-HOMOLOGOUS STRANDS. IN AIM 2, I WILL TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT RPA2 PROMOTES TMEJ BY STIMULATING THE HELICASE DOMAIN OF POLΘ TO SIMULTANEOUSLY BIND DNA, ANNEAL TWO DISTINCT STRANDS AND DESTABILIZE AND REMOVE RPA. UNDERSTANDING THE ROLE OF RPA IN TMEJ WILL PROVIDE FUNDAMENTAL INSIGHT INTO HOW POLΘ INTERACTS WITH OTHER PROTEINS TO REPAIR DSBS BOTH IN HEALTH AND DISEASE.

solicitations.title: OCEAN FOUNDATION MANGROVE RESTORATION IN SALINAS PUERTO RICO EARMARK PROJECT DESCRIPTION: THE NATIONAL OCEANIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION (NOAA) IS PROVIDING $802,000 IN FEDERAL FUNDING TO THE OCEAN FOUNDATION FOR THE RESTORATION OF MANGROVES IN SALINAS PUERTO RICO. SPECIFICALLY, THE OCEAN FOUNDATION WILL USE THESE FUNDS TO SUPPORT THE IMPLEMENTATION OF HYDROLOGICAL, TOPOGRAPHIC, AND BIOLOGICAL RESTORATION ACTIVITIES FOR 221 ACRES OF MANGROVES AT TWO SITES IN THE JOBOS BAY NATIONAL ESTUARINE RESEARCH RESERVE (JBNERR). THE FIELDWORK WILL BE LED BY A CONSULTANT, MERELLO MARINE CONSULTING, LLC, WHO WILL HIRE AND TRAIN PUERTO RICAN LABORERS TO SUPPORT THIS EFFORT. ACTIVITIES WILL INCLUDE CHANNEL CREATION AND DREDGING, CULVERT MAINTENANCE AND CLEANING, MANGROVE DISPERSAL CENTER CONSTRUCTION, MANGROVE NURSERY CULTIVATION AND PLANTING, AND PROJECT MONITORING. IN ADDITION, TOF AND ITS PARTNERS WILL WORK WITH THE RESERVE ON COMMUNITY OUTREACH, EDUCATIONAL ACTIVITIES FOR VOLUNTEERS AND STUDENTS, AND RESTORATION MONITORING (PRE-, DURING, AND POST-RESTORATION). THE OCEAN FOUNDATION WILL SERVE AS THE OVERALL PROJECT MANAGER, INCLUDING PROCUREMENT, COMMUNICATIONS, REPORTING, AND DIRECTOR OF DRONE-RELATED ACTIVITIES. THE AWARD WILL SUPPORT PERSONNEL, FRINGE, AND TRAVEL FOR TOF STAFF, AS WELL AS SUPPLIES FOR RESTORATION AND MONITORING, A SOLE-SOURCE CONTRACT WITH MERELLO MARINE CONSULTING, AND INDIRECT COSTS. IN THE LONG-TERM, THIS PROJECT IS ANTICIPATED TO CONTRIBUTE SIGNIFICANTLY TO HURRICANE-RELATED WIND AND FLOOD PROTECTION FOR THE SURROUNDING COMMUNITIES INCLUDING THE PREPA AGUIRRE POWER COMPLEX, A 1,534 MW OIL-FIRED THERMOELECTRIC POWER FACILITY THAT PROVIDES A SIGNIFICANT PORTION OF THE ENTIRE ISLAND'S ELECTRICAL GENERATING CAPACITY. THIS POWER-GENERATING ASSET DIRECTLY BORDERS JBNERR AND IS NOW FAR MORE EXPOSED TO HURRICANE-RELATED STORM SURGES AND DESTRUCTIVE WINDS DUE TO THE SUBSTANTIAL LOSS OF HEALTHY MANGROVE COVERAGE IN THE SURROUNDING AREA. WE ANTICIPATE THIS PROJECT WILL ALSO CONTRIBUTE TO IMPROVED FISHERIES AND MIGRATORY BIRD HABITATS IN THE INTERVENTION AREAS. OVERALL, THIS MANGROVE R/R EFFORT, THE LARGEST OF ITS KIND EVER ATTEMPTED IN THE UNITED STATES, WILL CONTRIBUTE TO BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF PRACTICE THAT WILL CATALYZE ADDITIONAL LARGE-SCALE COASTAL RESILIENCE PROJECTS VIA KNOWLEDGE SHARING, ON-THE-GROUND TRAINING, AND EXPANDED RESEARCH OPPORTUNITIES.

SARGASSUM INUNDATION AND IMPACTS ON ST. THOMAS, USVI CORAL WORLD OCEAN AND REEF INITIATIVE, INC. (CWORI) LEAD-PI: DR. SAMUEL J. ELIADES, CO-PI: MS. JESSICA MICHAEL TOTAL PROPOSED COST: $438,000 BUDGET PERIOD: 09/1/202409/31/2025  SARGASSUM IS A LARGE, HOLOPELAGIC BROWN SEAWEED THAT FLOATS IN ISLAND-LIKE MASSES CREATING CRITICAL HABITAT FOR PELAGIC SPECIES IN THE OPEN OCEAN. ONCE SARGASSUM REACHES SHORELINES AROUND THE ATLANTIC HOWEVER, IT NO LONGER SUPPORTS MANY OF THOSE PELAGIC SPECIES AND CAN BE DAMAGING TO COASTAL ENVIRONMENTS AND HUMAN HEALTH. SARGASSUM INUNDATION HAS BEEN SHOWN TO NEGATIVELY IMPACT NEARSHORE MARINE SPECIES INCLUDING ENDANGERED CORALS AND SEA TURTLES AND MAY EVEN BE RESPONSIBLE FOR LARGE FISH DIE-OFFS. ALTHOUGH SARGASSUM IS HARMLESS TO HUMANS IN WATER, ON LAND IT DECOMPOSES AND RELEASES HYDROGEN SULFIDE GAS AND AMMONIA WHICH ARE TOXIC TO HUMANS. THE USVI HAS BEEN HEAVILY IMPACTED BY SARGASSUM ANNUALLY SINCE 2011 WITH SOME YEARS RESULTING IN MATS OF SEAWEED STRETCHING OVER 100 FEET FROM SHORE IN BAYS AROUND ST. THOMAS. IN JULY 2022, GOVERNOR BRYAN DECLARED A STATE OF EMERGENCY DUE TO THE IMPACTS OF SARGASSUM INUNDATION ON A DESALINIZATION PLANT ON ST. CROIX, TRIGGERING A RESPONSE BY THE U.S. FEDERAL EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AGENCY. DESPITE CLEAR PROBLEMS WITH NEARSHORE SARGASSUM IN THE TERRITORY, DETAILED ENVIRONMENTAL, ECONOMIC, AND HEALTH IMPACTS ARE UNDER-DOCUMENTED LOCALLY. FOR INSTANCE, IT IS LARGELY UNKNOWN WHEN EXACTLY SARGASSUM SHIFTS FROM CRITICAL HABITAT FOR PELAGIC SPECIES TO EMPTY RAFTS OF ALGAE SMOTHERING INSHORE HABITATS. MONITORING COASTAL ZONES BEFORE, DURING, AND AFTER SARGASSUM INFLUX IN THE USVI IS NEEDED TO UNDERSTAND THE IMPACT SARGASSUM HAS ON TERRITORIAL ECOSYSTEMS, THE MANY ENDANGERED, THREATENED, AND NEAR-THREATENED SPECIES FOUND LOCALLY, AND THE RESIDENTS THAT LIVE IN THE TERRITORY. THIS PROJECT AIMS TO BETTER UNDERSTAND AND ADDRESS LOCAL SARGASSUM INFLUX IN THE USVI THROUGH THREE PRIMARY OBJECTIVES: 1) QUANTIFYING THE VOLUME OF SARGASSUM ARRIVING AT POPULAR BAYS AND BEACHES THROUGHOUT A YEAR, 2) ASSESSING THE IMPACT OF SARGASSUM INUNDATION ON NEARSHORE HABITATS, PARTICULARLY CORAL REEFS, AND 3) STUDYING POINTS AT WHICH SARGASSUM MATS SHIFT FROM CRITICAL HABITAT TO NUISANCE MACROALGAE. TO CALCULATE THE AMOUNT OF SARGASSUM ARRIVING IN THE USVI, AERIAL DRONES WILL BE USED TO COLLECT PHOTOS AND VIDEOS OF RAFTS AND APPROXIMATE SIZES WILL BE DETERMINED USING MULTIPLE IMAGE IDENTIFICATION SOFTWARE PACKAGES. IN ASSESSING THE IMPACTS OF SARGASSUM ON LOCAL MARINE HABITATS, EXPERIMENTAL PLOTS WILL BE ESTABLISHED ON REEFS COMMONLY AFFECTED BY SARGASSUM. THESE SITES WILL BE MONITORED REGULARLY VIA DIVERS ON SCUBA TO QUANTIFY CORAL COVER AND DIVERSITY BEFORE, DURING, AND AFTER SARGASSUM MAT ARRIVAL USING ORTHOMOSAIC SOFTWARE. FINALLY, KEY TRANSITIONAL POINTS AT WHICH SARGASSUM RAFTS BECOME NUISANCES WILL BE DETERMINED WITH VISUAL AND DIGITAL SURVEYS TO DOCUMENT SPECIES DIVERSITY PRESENCE AND SHIFTS AS RAFTS APPROACH LOCAL BEACHES. WHILE DOING THIS, ENDANGERED AND THREATENED SPECIES LIKE STONY CORALS AND SEA TURTLES ASSOCIATED WITH SARGASSUM MATS WILL BE MONITORED CLOSELY AND LOCAL AND FEDERAL REGULATORY BODIES WILL BE CONSULTED TO DETERMINE IF WILDLIFE RESCUE OR RELOCATION ACTIONS MAY BE APPROPRIATE. ADDRESSING THESE GOALS WILL PROVIDE NECESSARY BASELINES TO FURTHER IMPROVE THE USVI RESPONSE TO NEARSHORE SARGASSUM INFLUXES IMMEDIATELY AND INTO THE FUTURE.
solicitations.recipient: CORAL WORLD OCEAN AND REEF INITIATIVE INC

solicitations.title: ROLE OF PROTEIN N-MYRISTOYLATION IN DRIVING DNA DAMAGE RESPONSE - PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT THE DNA DAMAGE RESPONSE (DDR) IS CRUCIAL FOR MAINTAINING GENOME STABILITY, WITH ITS DYSREGULATION IMPLICATED IN VARIOUS DISEASES. POST-TRANSLATIONAL MODIFICATIONS (PTMS) ORCHESTRATE DDR, INFLUENCING VITAL SIGNALING PATHWAYS FOR GENOMIC STABILITY. N-MYRISTOYLATION, CATALYZED BY N-MYRISTOYL TRANSFERASE (NMT) 1 AND 2, HAS EMERGED AS A PIVOTAL PTM THAT INVOLVES A RANGE OF BIOLOGICAL PROCESSES. DESPITE ITS POTENTIAL SIGNIFICANCE, A KNOWLEDGE GAP PERSISTS REGARDING THE ROLE OF N-MYRISTOYLATION IN DDR. MY PRELIMINARY DATA REVEAL A LINK BETWEEN REDUCED N-MYRISTOYLATION EXPRESSION AND HEIGHTENED GENOMIC INSTABILITY. UTILIZING QUANTITATIVE PROTEOMICS, 14 N- MYRISTOYLATED CANDIDATES WITHIN KEY DDR PROTEINS WERE IDENTIFIED, WITH FOUR OVERLAPPING THE INTERACTOME OF NMT1 AND NMT2. VALIDATION STUDIES CONFIRMED N-MYRISTOYLATION ON KU70 AND RPA70, THE KEY PROTEINS IN THE DOUBLE- STRAND BREAK REPAIR. THESE FINDINGS FORM THE BASIS FOR THE HYPOTHESIS THAT N-MYRISTOYLATION ACTIVELY PROPELS DDR PATHWAYS BY INFLUENCING THE DYNAMICS AND FUNCTIONS OF KEY PROTEINS PIVOTAL TO DDR REGULATION. THIS PROJECT SYSTEMATICALLY INVESTIGATES THE ROLE OF N-MYRISTOYLATION IN DDR. AIM 1 DELVES INTO THE ASSOCIATION OF N-MYRISTOYLATION, MODULATED BY NMT1 AND NMT2, WITH GENOMIC STABILITY AND DDR. THIS INCLUDES ASSESSING THE IMPACT OF N-MYRISTOYLATION ON DNA REPAIR MECHANISMS AND DISSECTING THE INTERACTOME OF NMT1 AND NMT2 IN RESPONSE TO VARIOUS DNA DAMAGE AGENTS. AIM 2 FOCUSES ON THE DETAILED CHARACTERIZATION OF N-MYRISTOYLATION ON KU70, INCLUDING THE IDENTIFICATION OF SPECIFIC N-MYRISTOYLATION SITES, EVALUATION OF THEIR IMPACT ON PROTEIN LOCALIZATION AND STABILITY, ELUCIDATION OF FUNCTIONAL CONSEQUENCES IN DNA REPAIR, AND INVESTIGATION OF REGULATORY MECHANISMS. TRANSITIONING INTO THE R00 PHASE IN AIM 3, I WILL BUILD UPON THE INSIGHTS GAINED DURING THE K99 PHASE, LEVERAGING THEM TO VALIDATE AND CHARACTERIZE ADDITIONAL N-MYRISTOYLATED DDR CANDIDATES IDENTIFIED THROUGH PROTEOMIC SCREENING. THIS PHASE AIMS TO DEVELOP THERAPEUTIC IMPLICATIONS BASED ON A COMPREHENSIVE UNDERSTANDING OF N-MYRISTOYLATION'S REGULATORY ROLE IN DDR, ENHANCING THE TRANSLATIONAL POTENTIAL OF THE RESEARCH. THE SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION OF THIS WORK HOLDS THE PROMISE OF SIGNIFICANTLY ADVANCING OUR UNDERSTANDING OF THE INTRICATE REGULATORY NETWORKS GOVERNED BY N-MYRISTOYLATION IN DDR. BEYOND SCIENTIFIC CONTRIBUTIONS, THIS AWARD PROVIDES COMPREHENSIVE TRAINING IN BOTH MY TECHNICAL AND PROFESSIONAL SKILLS UNDER THE GUIDANCE OF EXPERTS IN MY MENTORING COMMITTEE, FOSTERING PROFICIENCY IN THE DNA REPAIR FIELD AND DISEASE THERAPY DEVELOPMENT, THEREBY PREPARING ME FOR THE JOURNEY OF ESTABLISHING AND RUNNING A SUCCESSFUL INDEPENDENT LABORATORY.
solicitations.agency: Department of Health and Human Services

solicitations.title: PROTEIN MODIFICATION AND STABILITY REGULATION TO COPE WITH GENOTOXIC ENVIRONMENTS - PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT HUMANS FACE MANY TYPES OF ENVIRONMENTAL GENOTOXIC AGENTS, SUCH AS VARIOUS POLLUTANTS AND RADIATION, THAT CAN CAUSE DELETERIOUS MUTATIONS AND DISEASES. TO SURVIVE AND ADAPT TO GENOTOXIC CONDITIONS, CELLS RELY ON MANY HIGHLY CONSERVED AND COMPLEX MOLECULAR STRATEGIES. ONE STRATEGY TO WITHSTAND GENOTOXIC STRESS IS THE USE OF ERROR-PRONE REPAIR MECHANISMS TO INCREASE MUTATION RATES, WHICH CAN POTENTIATE NEW FUNCTIONS FOR BETTER ADAPTATION TO TOXIC ENVIRONMENTS. ANOTHER MECHANISM IS THE DNA DAMAGE CHECKPOINT (DDC) SYSTEM, WHICH CONTROLS THE ARREST AND RESUMPTION OF THE CELL CYCLE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE BURDEN OF GENOMIC LESIONS. WHILE THE FRAMEWORKS OF BOTH STRATEGIES ARE LARGELY ESTABLISHED, IMPORTANT MECHANISTIC DETAILS ARE LACKING. TO ADDRESS THESE GAPS IN KNOWLEDGE, I WILL CAPITALIZE ON THE BUDDING YEAST MODEL ORGANISM’S HIGHLY CONSERVED DDC AND REPAIR SYSTEMS. AIM 1 WILL BUILD ON THE RECENT OBSERVATION THAT THE DESUMOYLASE ULP1 IS SUMOYLATED UPON GENOTOXIN TREATMENT AND THAT MUTATING ITS TWO SUMOYLATION SITES RESULTS IN A PHENOTYPE INDICATIVE OF INCREASED HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION (HR). I THUS HYPOTHESIZE THAT ULP1 SUMOYLATION SUPPRESSES HR TO FAVOR ERROR-PRONE REPAIR. I WILL TEST THIS HYPOTHESIS AND DEFINE THE BROADER SUMO-BASED CONTROL OF DNA REPAIR PATHWAY USAGE IN RESPONSE TO GENOTOXINS. AIM 2 WILL ADDRESS THE RECENT FINDINGS THAT RPA IS DOWNREGULATED AFTER GENOTOXIN EXPOSURE. I HYPOTHESIZE THAT RPA DOWNREGULATION CAN INDUCE DDC RECOVERY. TO TEST THIS, I WILL ELUCIDATE HOW RPA DEGRADATION IS REGULATED AND HOW THIS IMPACTS DDC RECOVERY. DEFECTS IN EITHER OF THE DNA REPAIR PATHWAY CHOICES AND DDC RESPONSES CAN LEAD TO GENOME INSTABILITY SYNDROMES, CHARACTERIZED BY INCREASED SENSITIVITY TO ENVIRONMENTAL GENOTOXINS, DEVELOPMENTAL ABNORMALITIES, AND INCREASED TUMORIGENESIS. THIS WORK WILL LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR ELUCIDATING CELLULAR ADAPTATION STRATEGIES AND CAN INFORM DISEASE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT STRATEGIES. IT WILL ALSO PROVIDE IMPORTANT TRAINING FOR ME TO REACH MY LONG-TERM CAREER GOAL OF BECOMING A PRINCIPLE INVESTIGATOR STUDYING HOW HUMAN CELLS RESPONSE TO ENVIRONMENTAL GENOTOXINS.
solicitations.agency: Department of Health and Human Services
solicitations.recipient: WEILL MEDICAL COLLEGE OF CORNELL UNIVERSITY

COMMUNITY PROJECT FUNDING/CONGRESSIONALLY DIRECTED SPENDING - CONSTRUCTION - THE UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS FOR MEDICAL SCIENCES (UAMS) REQUESTS $8 MILLION FROM HRSA’S CONSTRUCTION AND EQUIPMENT ACCOUNT WITHIN THE FISCAL YEAR 2024 LABOR-HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES-EDUCATION APPROPRIATIONS BILL TO SUPPORT NEEDED FACILITY IMPROVEMENTS AT UAMS IN LITTLE ROCK THAT WILL ALLOW EXPANDED SERVICES TO DELIVER HIGH QUALITY SPECIALTY AND SUBSPECIALITY GASTROENTEROLOGY CARE.   GASTROENTEROLOGY IS A HIGH-DEMAND SERVICE THAT HAS BEEN UNDERDEVELOPED AT UAMS. IN ITS CURRENT STATE, THE SERVICE EXPERIENCES INADEQUATE ACCESS RESULTING IN LONG WAIT TIMES FOR PATIENTS AND LESS THAN IDEAL PATIENT SATISFACTION. NEW PATIENT SCHEDULING LAG MAY RANGE FROM THREE TO SIX MONTHS AND WAIT TIME FOR ROUTINE ENDOSCOPY PROCEDURES IS FOUR TO SIX MONTHS. EXCESSIVE WAIT TIME FOR GASTROINTESTINAL (GI) PROCEDURES CAUSES DOWNSTREAM NEGATIVE IMPACTS ON OTHER CLINICAL SERVICES INCLUDING TRANSPLANT AND COLORECTAL SURGERY.  EXPANDED GI SERVICES WILL ALLOW PATIENTS TO BE TREATED EARLIER, RESULTING IN BETTER OUTCOMES, EXPEDITED TRANSPLANT LISTING, AND EARLIER DETECTION OF CANCERS AND OTHER LIFE-THREATENING ILLNESSES.  THIS PROJECT PROPOSES THE DEVELOPMENT OF AN OFF-SITE GENERAL GI CLINIC AND OUTPATIENT ENDOSCOPY CENTER.  THE SPACE WILL BE DESIGNED TO IMPROVE PATIENT FLOW AND MAXIMIZE PHYSICIAN PRODUCTIVITY. THE ENVIRONMENT WILL BE CONDUCIVE TO FACULTY AND STAFF RETENTION AND WILL PROVIDE SUFFICIENT SPACE FOR RESIDENT AND STUDENT LEARNING.  THE OVERALL GOALS OF THE EXPANSION OF OUR GI SERVICES ARE TO IMPROVE ACCESS TO HIGH QUALITY SERVICES FOR THE PEOPLE OF ARKANSAS, TO IMPROVE PATIENT FLOW AND PROVIDER PRODUCTIVITY, AND TO ENHANCE RECRUITMENT AND RETENTION OF SKILLED FACULTY AND STAFF SPECIALIZING IN GI DISORDERS. ULTIMATELY, THIS IS EXPECTED TO ALSO INCREASE ACCESS TO SPECIALTY AND SUBSPECIALTY CARE FOR TRANSPLANT AND HEPATOLOGY COMPLEX PROCEDURES. ADDITIONALLY, THIS WILL ALLOW CURRENT ENDOSCOPY SPACE ON THE UAMS CAMPUS TO BE RENOVATED TO EXPAND ACCESS TO ADVANCED ENDOSCOP Y AND INPATIENT PROCEDURES. ENDOSCOPY IS A CRITICAL SERVICE TO SUPPORT BOTH THE CANCER AND TRANSPLANT MISSIONS.   ACHIEVING NATIONAL CANCER INSTITUTE (NCI) DESIGNATION FOR COMPREHENSIVE CANCER CARE IS A MAJOR INSTITUTIONAL OBJECTIVE. UAMS HAS A MULTIYEAR STRATEGY IT IS EXECUTING TO REACH THAT GOAL BY 2029. THEREFORE, THE CLINIC EXPANSION WILL NOT ONLY MEET THE PENT-UP DEMAND FOR GI SERVICES BUT ALSO COMPLEMENT THE UAMS EFFORT TO DRAMATICALLY IMPROVE CANCER CARE IN THE STATE THROUGH IMPROVED DETECTION AND PREVENTION.

solicitations.title: THE PURPOSE OF THIS AWARD IS TO PROVIDE IMPLEMENTATION, MONITORING, AND STRATEGY INTEGRATION CAPACITY TO SUPPORT THE SALMON AND CHALLIS FIELD OFFICES, THE IDAHO BLM STATE OFFICE, AND OTHER REGIONAL AND STATE PARTNERS TO IMPROVE CLIMATE RESILIENCE AND HABITAT QUALITY BY CONSERVING WATER AT NATURAL WATER SOURCES THROUGH WATER DEVELOPMENT IMPROVEMENTS, AND BY PROTECTING  WATER SOURCES, STREAMS, AND WETLAND AND RIPARIAN AREAS  VIA VIRTUAL FENCING AND TRADITIONAL RIPARIAN EXCLOSURE FENCING. ADDITIONALLY, OUR WORK WILL ACCELERATE RIPARIAN RESTORATION AND ASSOCIATED UPLAND RESTORATION VIA SEED COLLECTION, BOLSTERING POLLINATOR RESOURCES, AND THROUGH GRAZING MANAGEMENT PROJECTS IN HIGH PRIORITY IDAHO SAGEBRUSH LANDSCAPES. THE PROPOSED WORK WILL HELP SUPPORT AND MAINTAIN FUNCTIONING WILDLIFE HABITATS AND WILL HELP TO PROVIDE FOR THE LONG-TERM CONSERVATION OF WILDLIFE AND BIODIVERSITY ON PUBLIC LANDS. WE WILL DELIVER AND SUPPORT BLM AND PARTNERS ON ADDITIONAL RIPARIAN RESTORATION PROJECTS AND INTEGRATE THEM WITH ESSENTIAL SUPPORT SERVICES FOR SCIENCE-BASED PRIORITIZATION, IMPLEMENTATION, MONITORING, AND ADAPTIVE MANAGEMENT. THE ADDITIONAL IMPLEMENTATION OF GRAZING AND MANAGEMENT PRACTICES TOOLS THAT INCLUDE VIRTUAL FENCING AND STOCK WATER DISTRIBUTION SYSTEMS WILL ALIGN WITH BLM PRIORITIES AND PLANS TO IMPROVE WILDLIFE HABITAT QUALITY AND CONNECTIVITY AND IMPROVE RANGE CONDITIONS. WE WILL LOOK FOR OPPORTUNITIES TO CONNECT WORK IN THE UPPER SALMON WITH OTHER PRIORITY LANDSCAPES IN IDAHO. THE SCOPE OF WHAT WE ARE PROPOSING IS SCALABLE, AND WE WILL LOOK TO BLM TO HELP DETERMINE THE DESIRED SCOPE AND DELIVERABLES. THE PROPOSED EFFORTS WILL: 1) BENEFIT THE BLMS MISSION TO CONSERVE THE NATURAL RESOURCES OF THE REGION, 2) DIRECTLY BENEFIT THE PEOPLE AND COMMUNITIES OF LEMHI AND CUSTER COUNTIES AND OTHER PROJECT LOCATIONS THROUGH WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND CONTRACTING OPPORTUNITIES, 3) IMPROVE IMPORTANT HABITAT CONDITIONS FOR WILDLIFE INCLUDING SAGE-GROUSE AND AQUATIC SPECIES, AND 4) IMPROVE RIPARIAN-UPLAND INTEGRATION, THAT BETTER BALANCES GRAZING USE WITH WILDLIFE NEEDS IN SENSITIVE HABITAT LOCATIONS LIKE RIPARIAN RESOURCES. ALL TOLD, THIS APPROACH LEVERAGES SCIENCE, MONITORING, AND COMPLEMENTARY MANAGEMENT TOOLS TO ACHIEVE LASTING BENEFITS FOR WATERSHED FUNCTION WHILE BOOSTING SOCIAL SUPPORT FOR LTPBR AND DEVELOPING EVIDENCE-BASED INFORMATION ABOUT CONSERVATION OUTCOMES AND CLIMATE RESILIENCE. OUR OVERALL GOAL IS TO HELP THE BLM MEET ECOSYSTEM RESTORATION AND RESILIENCE TARGETS AND ADVANCE THE OBJECTIVES OF THE RESTORATION LANDSCAPE FUNDING. THROUGH A SCIENCE-BASED PRIORITIZATION APPROACH, WE WILL CONTRIBUTE TO COLLABORATIVE EFFORTS TO IMPLEMENT THE DEFEND THE CORE, GROW THE CORE FRAMEWORK, AND CREATE GREATER WATERSHED RESILIENCE TO DROUGHT, WILDFIRE, AND CLIMATE CHANGE TO ENSURE WILDLIFE POPULATIONS HAVE ABUNDANT AND CONNECTED HABITAT AND COMMUNITIES THRIVE.THIS PROPOSAL INCLUDES 1.4 FTE TO SUPPORT PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INCLUDING PLANNING, IMPLEMENTATION, STRATEGIC COORDINATION, AND CONTRACT MANAGEMENT. SUBAWARDS TO BOISE STATE UNIVERSITY (BSU) AND UNIVERSITY OF IDAHO (UI) WILL SUPPORT COLLABORATIVE WORK WITH TNC AND BLM TO DEVELOP SCIENCE-BASED AND EXPORTABLE FRAMEWORKS FOR SUPPORTING WATERSHED AND BASIN SCALE RESTORATION PRIORITIES AND FOR INTEGRATING MONITORING AT MULTIPLE SCALES (SITE-, DRONE-, AND SATELLITE-BASED) TO PROVIDE LONG-TERM QUANTITATIVE DATA FOR STREAM AND RIPARIAN RESTORATION OUTCOMES AND MESIC PERSISTENCE AND RESILIENCE.

GENOMIC BASIS OF TELOMERE LENGTH REGULATION AND CONSEQUENCES FOR COMPLEX TRAITS - PROJECT SUMMARY TELOMERE LENGTH IS CONSIDERED A GOOD BIOMARKER OF ORGANISMAL AND CELLULAR AGING. TELOMERES GET PROGRESSIVELY SHORTER WITH EACH CELL DIVISION AND RATES OF CELLULAR TURNOVER VARY FROM CELL TYPE TO CELL TYPE AND TISSUE TO TISSUE. WHEN TELOMERES BECOME CRITICALLY SHORT, CELLS ARREST THEIR PROLIFERATION AND GO INTO SENESCENCE RESULTING IN TISSUE DYSFUNCTION. TELOMERE LENGTH AND THE RATE OF TELOMERE SHORTENING SHOWS A GREAT DEAL OF INTERINDIVIDUAL VARIABILITY AND IS DEPENDENT ON MANY FACTORS INCLUDING GENETICS, AGE, ENVIRONMENT, AND LIFESTYLE. SEVERAL AGE- RELATED DISEASES HAVE BEEN ASSOCIATED WITH TELOMERE SHORTENING INCLUDING PULMONARY FIBROSIS, COPD, LIVER FIBROSIS, APLASTIC ANEMIA, MYELODYSPLASTIC SYNDROME, ALZHEIMER’S DISEASE, PARKINSON’S DISEASE, MYOCARDIAL HYPERTROPHY, DILATED CARDIOMYOPATHY, ISCHEMIA-REPERFUSION INJURY, CHRONIC KIDNEY DISEASE, KIDNEY FIBROSIS, OSTEOPOROSIS, AND OSTEOARTHRITIS. THE EFFECT OF GENETICS ON TELOMERE LENGTH HAS BEEN ESTIMATED TO BE 35-85% AND AT LEAST 17 GENES HAVE BEEN REPORTED TO REGULATE TELOMERE LENGTH AND CAUSE TELOMERE BIOLOGY DISORDERS (TBD) WHEN DISRUPTED (DKC1, TERC, TERT, NOP10, NHP2, TINF2, WRAP53, CTC1, RTEL1, PARN, ACD, POT1, NAF1, STN1, ZCCHC8, RPA1, AND DCLRE1B). THESE GENES DO NOT EXPLAIN ALL CASES OF TBD AND MORE TELOMERE LENGTH REGULATORS HAVE YET TO BE CHARACTERIZED. OUR PROPOSAL IS IN RESPONSE TO NOT-AG-23-020, WHICH IS MEANT “TO ENCOURAGE THE USE OF EXISTING COHORTS AND DATASETS FOR WELL-FOCUSED SECONDARY ANALYSES” TO ADDRESS CRITICAL QUESTIONS IN AGING RESEARCH. WE HAVE DESIGNED OUR PROPOSAL TO HARNESS THE UNIQUE LARGE DATA RESOURCES AT VANDERBILT UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER (VUMC), INCLUDING IDIOPATHIC PULMONARY FIBROSIS (IPF) AND BIOVU, AND THE LARGE PUBLIC DATA RESOURCES (GTEX CONSORTIUM, ALL OF US, AND THE UK BIOBANK). WE WILL REANALYZE THESE DATASETS, COMBINING MULTI-OMICS MODELING AND PHENOME-SCALE INTERROGATION, TO IDENTIFY NEW GENES AND VARIANTS RESPONSIBLE FOR TELOMERE SHORTENING, TELOMERE DISEASE RISK, PREMATURE AGING, AND THEIR PHENOME-WIDE CONSEQUENCES. BY RE-ANALYZING LARGE DATA SETS ALREADY AT OUR DISPOSAL, WE WILL BE ABLE TO IDENTIFY NOVEL GENES AND VARIANTS RESPONSIBLE FOR THE TELOMERE SHORTENING AND PROVIDE A MORE COMPLETE PICTURE OF THE ROLE OF ABERRANT TELOMERE LENGTH AND DYSFUNCTIONAL TELOMERE MAINTENANCE IN PATHOPHYSIOLOGICAL PROCESSES. A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF THE GENETIC AND BIOLOGICAL DRIVERS OF TELOMERE SHORTENING MAY PROVIDE INSIGHTS INTO DISEASE RISK, EARLIER DIAGNOSIS, AND NEW KINDS OF THERAPIES. OUR AIMS ARE TO: 1) DETERMINE THE GENETIC BASIS OF TELOMERE SHORTENING IN DIVERSE TISSUES, 2) DETERMINE THE PHENOME-WIDE CONSEQUENCES OF TELOMERE LENGTH USING LARGE-SCALE BIOBANKS, 3) IDENTIFY DISEASE CAUSING VARIANTS (DVS) IN NOVEL GENES ASSOCIATED WITH TELOMERE BIOLOGY DISORDERS (TBD)

solicitations.title: COLLABORATIVE RESEARCH: FRAMEWORKS: GROWING OPEN ONDEMAND: LEVERAGING UNIFIED COMMUNITY KNOWLEDGE (GOODLUCK) -THE ABILITY TO PROCESS LARGE SETS OF DATA ON RESEARCH COMPUTING (RC) PLATFORMS HAS LED TO REMARKABLE ADVANCES IN SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING AND HAS BECOME AN INDISPENSABLE TOOL FOR RESEARCHERS, INDUSTRY PROFESSIONALS, AND STUDENTS. THE IMPORTANCE OF RC PLATFORMS AND TOOLS (AND IN PARTICULAR THOSE INVOLVING AI TECHNOLOGY) HAS BEEN RECOGNIZED BY THE HIGHEST LEVELS OF THE UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT, AS EVIDENT BY THE NATIONAL AI INITIATIVE ACT OF 2020, WHICH CALLS FOR THE CREATION OF A NATIONAL ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE RESEARCH RESOURCE (NAIRR). UNFORTUNATELY, FOR MANY INDIVIDUALS, RC USE AND ADOPTION IS HINDERED BY THE COMPLEX WAY IN WHICH THESE RESOURCES ARE ACCESSED. WHILE WEB BROWSERS AND SMART DEVICES HAVE BECOME THE DOMINANT ACCESS MECHANISM FOR REMOTE CONSUMER AND ENTERPRISE COMPUTING SERVICES, THE ADOPTION OF SUCH MECHANISMS HAS LAGGED FOR MANY RC SERVICE PROVIDERS, CREATING AN ACCESSIBILITY GAP THAT IMPEDES FURTHER ADOPTION. OPEN ONDEMAND IS A MATURE, NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION (NSF) FUNDED, OPEN-SOURCE PLATFORM THAT ENABLES REMOTE WEB ACCESS TO RC SERVICES, THEREBY SIMPLIFYING USE OF THOSE RESOURCES AND FACILITATING COLLABORATION. RC CLIENTS CAN MANAGE FILES AND JOBS, CREATE AND SHARE APPS, RUN GUI APPLICATIONS, AND UTILIZE A TRADITIONAL TERMINAL ALL THROUGH A WEB BROWSER FROM ANYWHERE ON ANY DEVICE BECAUSE IT REQUIRES NO SPECIALIZED CLIENT SOFTWARE AND HAS A SIMPLE INTERFACE THAT IS EASY TO LEARN AND USE. THE OPEN ONDEMAND COMMUNITY HAS BEGUN TO EXPOSE PLATFORM LIMITATIONS AND REQUEST ADDITIONAL FEATURES AND CAPABILITIES, MANY OF WHICH WILL REQUIRE SIGNIFICANT COLLABORATIVE DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS TO IMPLEMENT. FURTHER ADOPTION OF THIS POWERFUL PLATFORM, AND THE RESULTANT SCIENTIFIC ADVANCES, CAN BE ACCELERATED VIA A VARIETY OF INNOVATIONS: (1) CATALOG, PROVIDING CAPABILITIES TO CONCEPTUALIZE, CREATE, MODIFY, SHARE, AND PUBLICIZE APPS; (2) CLASSROOM, FOCUSED ON OPEN ONDEMAND IN EDUCATIONAL ENVIRONMENTS; (3) CUSTOMIZATION, INVOLVING INNOVATION IN CLIENT INTERFACES AND NEW TECHNOLOGIES; AND (4) COMMUNITY, FACILITATING COMMUNICATIONS AMONG THE COMMUNITY. A CROSS-CUTTING ASPECT OF THE GOODLUCK PROJECT WILL PILOT AND DELIVER INTEGRATED SETS OF TOOLS, DOCUMENTATION, AND TRAINING MATERIALS, WHICH RESEARCHERS CAN SELECT FROM TO CREATE AN ENVIRONMENT TAILORED TO THEIR RESEARCH. INITIAL BENEFICIARIES OF THESE EFFORTS INCLUDE: (1) BIOCHEMISTS UTILIZING CRYOGENIC ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (CRYO-EM) TO IDENTIFY THE UNDERLYING MECHANISMS OF VIRAL ACTION; (2) MATERIALS SCIENTISTS DESIGNING NEW ALLOYS FROM FIRST PRINCIPLES; (3) ART DESIGN STUDENTS CREATING NOVEL WORKS VIA THE USE OF GENERATIVE ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE; AND (4) AEROSPACE ENGINEERS IMPROVING THE PERFORMANCE OF DRONES VIA COMPUTATIONAL FLUID DYNAMICS. IN SUMMARY, THE GOODLUCK EFFORT WILL PROVIDE TOOLS AND PROCESSES THAT ALLOW RESEARCHERS TO MORE EASILY AND EXTENSIVELY UTILIZE OPEN ONDEMAND, AS WELL AS TO ANALYZE AND SHARE THEIR COMPUTATIONAL RESULTS, REGARDLESS OF THEIR ACCESS TO RC RESOURCES OR POSITION IN THE RESEARCH LIFECYCLE. THIS AWARD REFLECTS NSF'S STATUTORY MISSION AND HAS BEEN DEEMED WORTHY OF SUPPORT THROUGH EVALUATION USING THE FOUNDATION'S INTELLECTUAL MERIT AND BROADER IMPACTS REVIEW CRITERIA.- SUBAWARDS ARE NOT PLANNED FOR THIS AWARD.

IMPROVING EFFICIENCY, QUALITY, AND EQUITY: RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED EVALUATIONS OF REMOTE VS. IN-PERSON CLINICAL TRIAL METHODS - PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT REMOTE TRIALS (AKA “VIRTUAL TRIALS” OR “DECENTRALIZED TRIALS”), IN WHICH PATIENTS RECEIVE TREATMENT AND PROVIDE STUDY DATA WITHOUT THE NEED TO TRAVEL TO A CLINIC OR MEDICAL CENTER, HAVE EMERGED AS A PROMISING APPROACH FOR ENHANCING CLINICAL TRIAL EFFICIENCY. HOWEVER, EVIDENCE FOR THE HYPOTHESIZED BENEFITS OF REMOTE TRIALS IS WEAK, CONSISTING PRIMARILY OF ANECDOTAL REPORTS, UNCONTROLLED STUDIES, AND EXPERT OPINION. THE SAME IS TRUE FOR HYPOTHESIZED CHALLENGES AND LIMITATIONS OF REMOTE TRIALS, INCLUDING CONCERNS ABOUT TECHNOLOGICAL BARRIERS AND MULTIPLE ASPECTS OF TRIAL QUALITY. CLEARLY, A STRONGER EVIDENCE BASE IS NEEDED TO EVALUATE THE IMPACT OF REMOTE TRIALS ON THE “QUALITY AND EFFICIENCY OF TRANSLATIONAL RESEARCH, PARTICULARLY MULTISITE TRIALS” (CTSA GOAL 4). RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED TRIALS (RCTS) ARE THE GOLD STANDARD FOR COMPARING TREATMENTS – AND, BY EXTENSION, FOR TESTING THE IMPACT OF REMOTE METHODS ON TRIAL EFFICIENCY AND QUALITY. ACCORDINGLY, WE PROPOSE AN INNOVATIVE, RIGOROUS EXPERIMENTAL EVALUATION OF REMOTE VS. IN-PERSON METHODS ON TRIAL EFFICIENCY (ACCRUAL) AND QUALITY (RETENTION, TREATMENT ADHERENCE, BIO-SPECIMEN COMPLETION RATES), ACROSS 3 RCTS ON 3 DIFFERENT USE CASES: A LARGER, MULTI-SITE DEMONSTRATION TRIAL OF PHARMACOTHERAPY FOR SMOKING CESSATION AT THE BUFFALO AND UPENN HUBS IN THE UG3 PHASE AND TWO SMALLER, SINGLE-SITE DISSEMINATION PROJECTS IN THE UH3 PHASE (A MHEALTH INTERVENTION FOR PEOPLE WITH DEPRESSIVE SYMPTOMS AT THE MUSC HUB; AN OPIOID OVERDOSE EDUCATION AND NALOXONE INTERVENTION AT THE UAB HUB). THESE TRIALS WILL USE A HARMONIZED DESIGN AND OUTCOMES AND FOLLOW PARTICIPANTS THROUGH 3-MONTH FOLLOW-UP. RESULTS FROM THESE 3 RCTS WILL BE ANALYZED JOINTLY TO ASSESS THE DEGREE TO WHICH THE FINDINGS FOR REMOTE VS. IN-PERSON TRIAL METHODS GENERALIZE ACROSS CONDITIONS AND TREATMENT MODALITIES. WE WILL ALSO EXPLORE WHETHER USE OF REMOTE VERSUS IN-PERSON METHODS DIFFERENTIALLY IMPACTS REPRESENTATION AND RETENTION AMONG UNDER-REPRESENTED GROUPS FACING HEALTH DISPARITIES. THE PROPOSED SERIES OF RCTS IS SIGNIFICANT AND NOVEL IN ITS ABILITY TO ADVANCE OUR UNDERSTANDING OF THE IMPACT OF REMOTE APPROACHES ON CLINICAL TRIAL EFFICIENCY AND RIGOR. THE QUANTITATIVE, QUALITATIVE, AND COST DATA GENERATED FROM THIS PROJECT WILL ESTABLISH A TRANSLATIONAL SCIENCE EVIDENCE BASE WHICH WILL GUIDE THE DESIGN OF FUTURE CLINICAL TRIALS. ADDITIONAL DISSEMINATION EFFORTS (E.G., PROJECT WEBSITE, WEBINAR SERIES, CONFERENCE PRESENTATIONS, PUBLICATIONS, NEWSLETTERS AND PRESS RELEASES, AND A NATIONAL WORKSHOP) WILL TARGET THE CTSA CONSORTIUM AND CLINICAL TRIALIST COMMUNITY AT LARGE.

DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT IS TO SUPPORT IMPLEMENTATION OF THE MASSACHUSETTS BAYS NATIONAL ESTUARY PARTNERSHIP (MBP) COMPREHENSIVE CONSERVATION AND MANAGEMENT PLAN (CCMP). THE GOAL IS TO RESTORE AND MAINTAIN THE CHEMICAL, PHYSICAL, AND BIOLOGICAL INTEGRITY OF THE ESTUARY AND SUPPORT ITS SUSTAINABLE HUMAN USES THROUGH TASKS OUTLINED IN ANNUAL WORKPLANS OF THE MBP AND ITS PARTNERS. THE COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT SUPPORTS ACTIVITIES THAT WILL ADDRESS THE KEY CHALLENGES FACING MASSBAYS: ALTERED HYDROLOGY; IMPAIRED WATER QUALITY AND HABITAT; CLIMATE VULNERABILITY; INVASIVE SPECIES; ECOSYSTEM FRAGMENTATION; AND IMPACTS ON EJ COMMUNITIES.  -
PRIORITY EFFORTS INCLUDE: UPDATING THE MASSBAYS' ECOSYSTEM DELINEATION AND ASSESSMENT (EDA) DATA AND DEVELOPING A NEW PUBLIC INTERFACE TO SHARE THE INFORMATION WITH RESEARCHERS, DECISIONMAKERS, AND PUBLIC AUDIENCES: CO-HOSTING A GULF OF MAINE SCIENCE SYMPOSIUM WITH MWRA TO HIGHLIGHT AND FOSTER GREATER SUPPORT FOR COASTAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT AND RESTORATION IN THE ECOSYSTEM; 
LAUNCHING CONTINUOUS WATER QUALITY MONITORING SYSTEMS IN COLLABORATION WITH USGS IN DUXBURY AND DANVERS AND THE LOWER NORTH SHORE; PURSUING FUNDING TO EXPAND SEED-BASED RESTORATION OF EELGRASS REGIONALLY (BUILDING ON PILOT STUDIES FUNDED BY WHOI SEA GRANT) AND EXAMINE INTERACTIONS BETWEEN AQUACULTURE AND EELGRASS CONDITION (EXPANDING ON A STUDY CO-LED WITH MIT SEA GRANT); DEVELOPING A PROCESS FOR IDENTIFYING IMPAIRED PONDS TO HELP IN IDENTIFYING POTENTIAL NEW RESTORATION PROJECTS (UNIMPAIRED PONDS WILL BE IDENTIFIED FOR PROTECTION MEASURES); INITIATING DIVERSITY, EQUITY, AND INCLUSION PLANNING FOR THE SOUTH SHORE REGION; AND COMPLETING A THREE-PRONGED APPROACH TO IMPROVE MONITORING OF COASTAL SYSTEMS USING UAV TECHNOLOGY. ACTIVITIES:KEY ACTIVITIES INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO: INCORPORATING THE MEASUREMENT OF PCO2 AND PH AS PART OF A CONTINUOUS MONITORING NETWORK; DEPLOYING PCO2 SENSORS FUNDED BY NROC; CONVENING A SCIENCE ADVISORY PANEL THAT EVOLVES FROM THE EXISTING OMSAP TO INCORPORATE WWTP AND OTHER NPDES PERMIT DISCHARGES BY TRACKING MONITORING REQUIRED IN PERMITS; CONVENING SCIENTISTS AND WORKING WITH PARTNERS TO CONVENE A GULF OF MAINE SCIENCE FORUM; PRODUCING AN UPDATED EDA 3.0 THAT BETTER EXAMINES RELATIONSHIPS AMONG STRESSOR, RESOURCE, AND SOCIO-ECONOMIC FACTORS, AND IDENTIFIES PRIORITIES FOR ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND RESTORATION; ASSESSING AND SELECTING A PLATFORM FOR BETTER VISUALIZATION OF EMBAYMENTS AT THE ECOTYPE, HABITAT, AND STRESSOR LEVEL FOR INTERNAL AND EXTERNAL USERS.SUBRECIPIENT:MBP WILL WORK WITH THE REGIONAL SERVICE PROVIDERS (RSPS) IN THE FIVE MBP GEOGRAPHIC REGIONS AND THE MYSTIC RIVER WATERSHED ASSOCIATION TO PRIORITIZE, ACTIVATE, AND IMPLEMENT PROJECTS TO ACHIEVE CCMP GOALS; AND TO DEVELOP INFORMATION TO MONITOR AND TRACK MBP PROGRESS TOWARDS CCMP GOALS. SUBAWARDS WILL SUPPORT THE COMPETITIVE HEALTHY ESTUARIES GRANT PROGRAM, AND THE PARTNERSHIP/STAKEHOLDER PROGRAMS OF THE FIVE RSPS) WHO ARE CRITICAL TO IMPLEMENTING THE CCMP IN THE 50 MASSBAYS COASTAL COMMUNITIES. THEIR ACTIVITIES ARE ALSO ESSENTIAL FOR PROVIDING NON-FEDERAL MATCH. 
- REGIONAL SERVICE PROVIDERS WILL EACH RECEIVE $100,000 TO FUND THEIR REGIONAL COORDINATOR, WITH APCC RECEIVING AN ADDITIONAL $23,715 FOR COMMUNICATIONS ASSISTANCE FOR TOTAL SUBAWARDS OF $523,715. THESE FUNDS WILL BE EXPENDED BY THE RSPS OVER 15 MONTHS, FROM JULY 1, 2024 TO SEPTEMBER 30, 2025. FUTURE SUBAWARDS WILL RUN FROM OCTOBER 1 THROUGH SEPTEMBER 30, TO AVOID GAPS IN REIMBURSEMENTS DURING THE SUMMER.  
- MBP IS ALSO SETTING ASIDE $29,169 FOR THE NEXT ROUND OF THE HEALTHY ESTUARIES SMALL-GRANT PROGRAM SCHEDULED FOR EARLY 2026. THIS AMOUNT WILL BE AUGMENTED BY FUNDING UNDER THE FY25 WORKPLAN.OUTCOMES:PCO2 SENSORS PROCURED AND DEPLOYED NEAR THE MOUTH OF THE DANVERS RIVER AND IN DKP; OMSAP SUCCESSOR PANEL ESTABLISHED TO CONSIDER NEW STRUCTURE, GOALS, AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES; GULF OF MAINE FORUM HOSTED; POTENTIAL AREAS IDEN

THE RESERVE IS ADMINISTERED BY THE N.C. THE DCM IS REQUESTING $1,850,031 WHICH WILL BE MATCHED BY $792,871 AND IS DERIVED FROM THE FOLLOWING SOURCES: 1. STATE APPROPRIATED OPERATING EXPENSES; 3. VOLUNTEER HOURS (YEAR 2 ONLY) AND 4. WAIVED INDIRECT FROM THE UNIVERSITY OF NORTH CAROLINA WILMINGTON STAFFING CONTRACT. DURING FY24-25, THE N.C. RESERVE WILL FOCUS ON RESILIENCE-RELATED TOPICS ACROSS THE PROGRAMS INCLUDING ESTUARINE SHORELINE RESEARCH, MONITORING AND TRAINING, IMPLEMENTING A HABITAT RESILIENCE PLAN AND TRANSLATING THE PLAN TO OTHER SITES, AND INCORPORATION OF INFORMATION INTO EDUCATION ACTIVITIES; CONTINUED COMMUNICATIONS TO SUPPORT STEWARDSHIP, EDUCATION, AND RESEARCH INITIATIVES; VOLUNTEER ENGAGEMENT THROUGH THE SITE STEWARDS PROGRAM; COLLABORATING WITH PARTNERS ON A VARIETY OF EXTERNALLY FUNDED PROJECTS ACROSS THE SECTORS; IMPLEMENTING THE NCNERR FY21 PAC AWARD; EVALUATING DRONES FOR HABITAT ASSESSMENT; UPDATING THE MANAGEMENT PLAN; AND MENTORING THE DAVIDSON FELLOW. THE RESERVE WILL ALSO CONTINUE ADDRESSING RECOMMENDATIONS FROM THE 312 EVALUATION. LINKAGES TO THE N.C. RESERVE 2020-2025 MANAGEMENT PLAN ARE CITED IN THE TASK OUTCOMES THROUGHOUT THE COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT WHERE APPLICABLE.

AUTOMATED IDENTIFICATION OF LARVAL MOSQUITO SPECIES WITH COMPUTER VISION - ABSTRACT. MOSQUITOES KILL NEARLY HALF-A-MILLION PEOPLE EACH YEAR. DUE TO LACK OF EFFECTIVE VACCINES FOR MOST MOSQUITO-BORNE DISEASES, PREVENTION OF MOSQUITO BITES REMAINS THE PRIMARY FOCUS OF DISEASE MITIGATION. LARVAL SURVEILLANCE - MONITORING POTENTIAL BREEDING SITES TO UNDERSTAND LARVAL SPECIES COMPOSITION, ABUNDANCE, AND SPATIAL DISTRIBUTION - IS KEY TO ENABLING PRECISION VECTOR CONTROL. IF MEDICALLY RELEVANT LARVAE ARE DETECTED, TARGETED LARVICIDE TREATMENTS CAN ELIMINATE THE POPULATION PRIOR TO EMERGENCE AS ADULT MOSQUITOES, WHEN DISEASE TRANSMISSION OCCURS. UNFORTUNATELY, DUE TO ITS RESOURCE-INTENSIVE NATURE, FEW MOSQUITO CONTROL ORGANIZATIONS (MCOS) HAVE THE CAPACITY TO CONDUCT THE FULL-PROCESS OF LARVAL SURVEILLANCE WITH IDENTIFICATION TO SPECIES. TECHNICIANS NEED TO TRAVEL TO THE POTENTIAL BREEDING SITE, COLLECT SPECIMENS, RETURN TO THE LAB FOR VISUAL MORPHOLOGICAL IDENTIFICATION UNDER A MICROSCOPE, AND IF MEDICALLY RELEVANT SPECIES ARE FOUND, RETURN TO THE FIELD FOR LARVICIDE TREATMENTS. DELAYED, INACCURATE, OR MISSING LARVAL SPECIES DATA CAN MISS AN OPPORTUNITY FOR INTERVENTION PRIOR TO EMERGENCE OF ADULTS, OR MISINFORM COSTLY UNNECESSARY TREATMENT. WHILE SIGNIFICANT EFFORTS HAVE BEEN MADE TO EXPLORE CROWDSOURCING LARVAL DATA AND UNMANNED AIRCRAFT SYSTEMS (UAS) FOR RAPID ASSESSMENTS OF POTENTIAL BREEDING SITES, IMPLEMENTATION AND TECHNICAL CHALLENGES HAVE LIMITED PRACTICAL USE OF THESE APPROACHES. NO AUTOMATED SOLUTIONS EXIST TO BUILD LARVAL SPECIES IDENTIFICATION CAPACITIES. WE PROPOSE TO DEVELOP THE FIRST COMMERCIALLY AVAILABLE IMAGE RECOGNITION PLATFORM FOR OPERATIONAL LARVAL SURVEILLANCE OF THE 20 MOST RELEVANT MOSQUITO SPECIES IN THE US. THIS PROPOSAL WILL FIRST VALIDATE THE OPTICAL REQUIREMENTS FOR SPECIES IDENTIFICATION OF LARVAE. THIS WILL ENABLE THE DEVELOPMENT OF A STANDARDIZED IMAGING CONFIGURATION THAT WILL BE USED TO BUILD A HIGH-QUALITY IMAGE DATASET OF MOSQUITO LARVAE OF DIFFERENT SPECIES CAPTURING DIAGNOSTIC MORPHOLOGICAL FEATURES. THE IMAGE DATASET WILL BE USED TO TRAIN A COMPUTER VISION SYSTEM TO IDENTIFY SPECIES OF MOSQUITO LARVAE. ULTIMATELY THE APPROACHES DEVELOPED HERE WILL ENABLE NEW LARVAL SURVEILLANCE PRODUCTS, FROM AN IDENTIFICATION FIELD-TOOL TO INFORM TECHNICIAN LARVICIDING AT THE POINT OF SPECIMEN COLLECTION, TO REMOTE SENSING SYSTEMS THAT WILL CONTINUOUSLY MONITOR HISTORICAL BREEDING SITES AND ALERT MOSQUITO CONTROL ORGANIZATIONS (MCOS) WHEN INTERVENTION IS NEEDED.

solicitations.title: CENTER FOR MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS IN CANCER (CMIC) - SUMMARY CANCER AFFECTS THE HEALTH OF MILLIONS OF AMERICANS. STUDYING MOLECULAR MECHANISMS THAT ENDOW CELLS WITH MALIGNANT PROPERTIES IS AN ESSENTIAL COMPONENT OF ADVANCING PRE-CLINICAL STUDIES AND A KEY PART OF EFFORTS TO IMPROVE PATIENT OUTCOMES. THE PURPOSE OF THIS COBRE SUBMISSION IS TO ESTABLISH THE CENTER FOR MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS IN CANCER (CMIC) AT THE UNIVERSITY OF ARKANSAS FOR MEDICAL SCIENCES (UAMS). THE MISSION OF THE CMIC IS TO STUDY MOLECULAR FEATURES AND FUNCTIONAL PROPERTIES OF BIOMOLECULES THAT DRIVE CANCER. THE UNIFYING THEME OF RESEARCH AMONG CENTER MEMBERS IS THE COUPLING OF STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY AND HIGH-RESOLUTION IMAGING WITH PRECISE, QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS OF BIOCHEMICAL AND CELLULAR PROCESSES TO UNDERSTAND HOW MOLECULAR INTERACTIONS GOVERN THE INITIATION, PROGRESSION AND TREATMENT OF CANCER. IN PHASE 1 OF THIS COBRE, WE WILL PURSUE THE FOLLOWING AIMS: (AIM 1) ESTABLISH THE CMICCREATING AN ORGANIZATIONAL FRAMEWORK THAT ENSURES THE SUCCESS OF JUNIOR INVESTIGATORS AS INDEPENDENT SCIENTISTS AND STRENGTHENS THE RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURE AT UAMS AND ITS AFFILIATE INSTITUTIONS, (AIM 2) BUILD A THEMATIC, MULTIDISCIPLINARY COBRE CENTER WITH UNIQUE COLLABORATIVE OPPORTUNITIES AND STRONG RESEARCH PROGRAMS FOCUSED ON UNDERSTANDING THE MOLECULAR BASIS OF CANCER, AND (AIM 3) IMPLEMENT A PLAN FOR LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF THE CMIC. THE ORGANIZATION OF THE CMIC WILL INCLUDE 5 RESEARCH PROJECTS, AN ADMINISTRATIVE CORE, AND 2 NEW RESEARCH CORESTHE STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY CORE AND THE BIOMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS CORE. THE ADMINISTRATIVE CORE WILL OVERSEE OPERATION OF THE CENTER, INCLUDING A PILOT PROJECT PROGRAM TO FUEL THE PIPELINE OF REPLACEMENT RESEARCH PROJECT LEADERS (RPLS) AND A FORMALIZED FACULTY DEVELOPMENT PLAN THAT INCLUDES INDIVIDUALIZED MENTORING. THE STRUCTURAL BIOLOGY CORE WILL HELP RPLS PLAN AND EXECUTE STRUCTURE-FUNCTION STUDIES BY ENSURING HIGH-QUALITY SAMPLE PREPARATION AND PROVIDING ACCESS TO WORLD-CLASS INSTRUMENTATION AND COMPUTATIONAL RESOURCES REQUIRED FOR HIGH-RESOLUTION STRUCTURAL STUDIES. THE BIOMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS CORE WILL ENABLE RESEARCHERS TO PERFORM QUANTITATIVE ANALYSIS OF MACROMOLECULAR INTERACTIONS AND DYNAMICS DOWN TO THE LEVEL OF SINGLE-MOLECULES. BOTH RESEARCH CORES WILL ASSIST USERS IN EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN, DATA ANALYSIS AND INTERPRETATION OF RESULTS, WHILE ALSO PROVIDING TRAINING AND EDUCATIONAL OUTREACH. THE CMIC WILL ADDRESS AN INSTITUTIONAL PRIORITY AT UAMS AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE MISSION OF THE WINTHROP P. ROCKEFELLER CANCER INSTITUTE BY CREATING A CRITICAL MASS OF INVESTIGATORS WHO ARE ABLE TO GAIN MOLECULAR-LEVEL INSIGHTS INTO MECHANISMS THAT ARE CRUCIAL FOR A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF CANCER BIOLOGY. OUR LONG-TERM GOAL IS TO LEVERAGE FACULTY MENTORING, STRATEGIC RECRUITMENT, AND CUTTING-EDGE CORE RESOURCES TO DEVELOP A CRITICAL MASS OF INVESTIGATORS THAT WILL SUPPORT A SELF- SUSTAINING CENTER IN WHICH RESEARCH ADVANCES OUR KNOWLEDGE OF CANCER THROUGH PRECISE AND COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSES OF MOLECULAR EVENTS THAT IMPACT MALIGNANT PATHOGENESIS.

solicitations.title: UAMS APPLICATION & TECHNICAL SUPPORT FOR FASS

PURPOSE: THE PURPOSE OF THIS GRANT IS TO IMPROVE METHODS FOR ASSESSING THE RESILIENCE OF THE ELECTRIC POWER SYSTEM IN THE US SUCH THAT IT MAINTAINS AND/OR RAPIDLY RECOVERS FROM DISRUPTIONS WHEN EXPOSED TO DAMAGING EXTREME WEATHER EVENTS, USING THE IMPACT OF HURRICANE MARIA IN PUERTO RICO AS A FIELD LABORATORY. ACTIVITIES TO BE PERFORMED: THIS STUDY WILL PERFORM THREE KEY ACTIVITIES: (1) DEVELOP A HYBRID NUMERICAL EXPERIMENTAL APPROACH FOR THE PREDICTIVE ASSESSMENT OF VULNERABILITY OF THE ELECTRIC POWER TRANSMISSION INFRASTRUCTURE WHEN EXPOSED TO EXTREME CLIMATE CONDITIONS.  USING MULTISCALE HIGH-RESOLUTION WEATHER MODELS COUPLED WITH COMPUTATIONAL FLUID DYNAMIC MODELS OF SCALED TRANSMISSION TOWERS FOR WIND TUNNEL TESTING; (2) APPLICATION OF THE MODELING FRAMEWORK IN THE CONTEXT OF AT RISK COASTAL AND ISLANDED COMMUNITIES, WITH PR AS FIRST USE CASE, AND (3) IN COLLABORATION WITH LOCAL POWER COMPANIES DEVELOP A UNIQUE FIELD TESTBED FOR MODEL EVALUATION THROUGH INSTRUMENTATION OF 10 TOWERS WITHIN THE 35 MILES LENGTH OF THE TESTBED, AND LIDAR DRONE MAPPING AND DIGITAL SURFACE MODELING OF CHANGE DEFECTION AND DAMAGE ASSESSMENT.EXPECTED OUTCOMES: THE METHODS WILL SUPPORT IMPROVEMENTS TO THE SECURITY AND RESILIENCY OF THE US ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE WHEN EXPOSED TO CLIMATE CHANGE INDUCED EXTREME WEATHER, SPECIFICALLY THE ELECTRICAL POWER INFRASTRUCTURE IN COASTAL AND ISLANDED REGIONS.INTENDED BENEFICIARIES: LOCAL RESIDENTS, BUSINESSES AND UTILITY REGULATORS, PUBLIC AND PRIVATE UTILITY COMPANIES, AND ENERGY POLICY MAKERS WILL BENEFIT FROM THESE NEW METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND THE RESULTING IMPROVED PERFORMANCE OF ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE.SUBRECIPIENT ACTIVITIES: THE RECIPIENT INTENDS TO SUBAWARD FUNDS TO NEW YORK UNIVERSITY AND CO-PI GHANDEHARI MASOUD. FUNDS WILL BE USED TO ENGAGE A GRADUATE STUDENT AND SUPPORT THE OVERALL EFFORTS OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH SUCH AS ASSISTING IN THE EXPERIMENTAL AND NUMERICAL MODELING OF ADVANCED ENVIRONMENTAL MODELING FOR POWER SECTORS.
